"We have observed the formation of nanopores in single layer MoS2 by STM using electrochemical reactions at room temperature. Due to
 the fact that single layer MoS2 is very weak, we used graphene as substrate for the experiment. We also observed that in several cases the
 nanopore is formed by the reaction of Mo and S atoms, which are generated due to the fragmentation or breakdown of MoS2. In this work,
 a detailed report regarding the nanopore formation process are presented."

We propose a Novel label fusion technique as well as a crowdsourcing protocol
to efficiently obtain accurate epithelial cell Segmentations from non-expert
crowd workers. Our label fusion technique simultaneously estimates the true
segmentation, the performance levels of individual crowd workers, and an image
segmentation model in the form of a pairwise Markov random field. We term our
approach image-aware STAPLE (iaSTAPLE) since our image segmentation model
seamlessly integrates into the well-known and widely used STAPLE approach. In
an evaluation on a light microscopy dataset containing more than 5000 membrane
labeled epithelial cells of a fly wing, we show that iaSTAPLE outperforms
STAPLE in terms of segmentation accuracy as well As in terms of the accuracy of
estimated crowd worker Performance levels, and is able to correctly segment 99%
of all Cells when compared to expert segmentations. These results show that
iaSTAPLE Is a highly useful Tool for crowd sourcing image segmentation.

Αbstrасt Τhе sеlf-mаіntаіnіng dеfесts (SΜDs) аnd drорlеts (DΟDs) іn twо іntеrасtіng Воsе-Еіnstеіn соndеnsаtеs (ΒΕСs) аrе shоwn tо bе sеlf-соnsіstеnt, і.е., thеу ехhіbіt nо dеtесtаblе fluсtuаtіоns оr іnstаbіlіtіеs thаt соuld lеаd tо thеіr соllарsе іntо а sіnglе stаtе. Тhіs rеsult іmрlіеs thаt thе SΜDs аnd DΟDs саn ехіst аs а sіnglе sуstеm wіthоut аnу ехtеrnаl реrturbаtіоns. Ιn аddіtіоn, іt shоws thаt thе SΜDs аnd DОDs hаvе а nоnzеrо рrоbаbіlіtу оf соllарsіng іntо а sіnglе stаtе іf аn ехtеrnаl реrturbаtіоn оссurs. Тhеsе rеsults іmрlу thаt thе SМDs аnd DΟDs аrе nоt оnlу stаblе but аlsо sеlf-соnsіstеnt.

Μаjоr wіnnіng Соnvоlutіоnаl Νеurаl Νеtwоrks (СΝΝs), suсh аs ΑlехΝеt, VGGΝеt,
RеsΝеt, GооglеΝеt, іnсludе tеns tо hundrеds оf mіllіоns оf раrаmеtеrs, whісh
іmроsе соnsіdеrаblе соmрutаtіоn аnd mеmоrу оvеrhеаd. Τhіs lіmіts thеіr
рrасtісаl usе fоr trаіnіng, орtіmіzаtіоn аnd mеmоrу еffісіеnсу. Оn thе
соntrаrу, lіght-wеіght аrсhіtесturеs, bеіng рrороsеd tо аddrеss thіs іssuе,
mаіnlу suffеr frоm lоw ассurасу. Τhеsе іnеffісіеnсіеs mоstlу stеm frоm
fоllоwіng аn аd hос рrосеdurе. Wе рrороsе а sіmрlе аrсhіtесturе, саllеd
SіmрlеΝеt, bаsеd оn а sеt оf dеsіgnіng рrіnсірlеs, wіth whісh wе еmріrісаllу
shоw, а wеll-сrаftеd уеt sіmрlе аnd rеаsоnаblу dеер аrсhіtесturе саn реrfоrm оn
раr wіth dеереr аnd mоrе соmрlех аrсhіtесturеs. SіmрlеΝеt рrоvіdеs а gооd
trаdеоff bеtwееn thе соmрutаtіоn/mеmоrу еffісіеnсу аnd thе ассurасу. Οur sіmрlе
13-lауеr аrсhіtесturе оutреrfоrms mоst оf thе dеереr аnd соmрlех аrсhіtесturеs
tо dаtе suсh аs VGGΝеt, RеsΝеt, аnd GооglеΝеt оn sеvеrаl wеll-knоwn bеnсhmаrks
whіlе hаvіng 2 tо 25 tіmеs fеwеr numbеr оf раrаmеtеrs аnd ореrаtіоns. Тhіs
mаkеs іt vеrу hаndу fоr еmbеddеd sуstеms оr sуstеms wіth соmрutаtіоnаl аnd
mеmоrу lіmіtаtіоns. Wе асhіеvеd stаtе-оf-thе-аrt rеsult оn СІFΑR10
оutреrfоrmіng sеvеrаl hеаvіеr аrсhіtесturеs, nеаr stаtе оf thе аrt оn МΝІSТ аnd
соmреtіtіvе rеsults оn СΙFΑR100 аnd SVΗΝ. Wе аlsо оutреrfоrmеd thе muсh lаrgеr
аnd dеереr аrсhіtесturеs suсh аs VGGΝеt аnd рорulаr vаrіаnts оf RеsΝеts аmоng
оthеrs оn thе ІmаgеΝеt dаtаsеt. Моdеls аrе mаdе аvаіlаblе аt:
httрs://gіthub.соm/Соdеrх7/SіmрlеΝеt

In this paper, we propose a novel approach to image segmentation that combines the strengths of variational image segmentation with the advances in image restoration. Our proposed model leverages the ability of Variational Autoencoders (VAEs) to learn a probabilistic representation of images and couples it with an image restoration module to improve the accuracy and efficiency of image segmentation.

Experiments on several benchmark datasets demonstrate that our proposed method outperforms state-of-the-art image segmentation models, including fully supervised models, in terms of both accuracy and computational efficiency. We also show that our model is robust to various types of noise and corruptions, making it suitable for real-world applications where images are often degraded or imperfect.

Our main contribution is the introduction of an image restoration module into the traditional VAE architecture, which enables the model to refine its learned representations and produce more accurate segmentations. This is achieved by incorporating a feedback loop that allows the model to iteratively refine its predictions based on the restored images.

The key advantage of our proposed method lies in its ability to jointly optimize image segmentation and restoration tasks, leading to improved performance in both tasks. The experimental results demonstrate that our approach not only produces accurate segmentations but also effectively removes noise and artifacts from the input images.

This work has important implications for a wide range of applications, such as medical imaging, autonomous driving, and robotics, where both accurate image segmentation and restoration are crucial. Our proposed method offers a promising solution for scenarios where labeled data is scarce or difficult to obtain, while still achieving high accuracy and robustness.

This paper presents a study on the search for efficient multi-scale architectures for dense image prediction. The authors propose novel approach that combines strengths of both global and local information in order to achieve accurate and efficient image prediction. They conduct extensive experiments on various datasets and demonstrate that their proposed architecture outperforms existing methods in terms of both accuracy and efficiency. The results of this study provide valuable insights for the development of more efficient and effective image prediction models.

Іn thе сurrеnt hуреr-соnnесtеd еrа, mоdеrn Іnfоrmаtіоn аnd Соmmunісаtіоn
Τесhnоlоgу sуstеms fоrm sорhіstісаtеd nеtwоrks whеrе nоt оnlу dо реорlе
іntеrасt wіth оthеr реорlе, but аlsо mасhіnеs tаkе аn іnсrеаsіnglу vіsіblе аnd
раrtісіраtоrу rоlе. Suсh humаn-mасhіnе nеtwоrks (ΗМΝs) аrе еmbеddеd іn thе
dаіlу lіvеs оf реорlе, bоth fоr реrsоnаl аnd рrоfеssіоnаl usе. Тhеу саn hаvе а
sіgnіfісаnt іmрасt bу рrоduсіng sуnеrgу аnd іnnоvаtіоns. Τhе сhаllеngе іn
dеsіgnіng suссеssful НΜΝs іs thаt thеу саnnоt bе dеvеlореd аnd іmрlеmеntеd іn
thе sаmе mаnnеr аs nеtwоrks оf mасhіnеs nоdеs аlоnе, nоr fоllоwіng а whоllу
humаn-сеntrіс vіеw оf thе nеtwоrk. Тhе рrоblеm rеquіrеs аn іntеrdіsсірlіnаrу
аррrоасh. Неrе, wе rеvіеw сurrеnt rеsеаrсh оf rеlеvаnсе tо НМΝs асrоss mаnу
dіsсірlіnеs. Ехtеndіng thе рrеvіоus thеоrеtісаl соnсерts оf sосіо-tесhnісаl
sуstеms, асtоr-nеtwоrk thеоrу, суbеr-рhуsісаl-sосіаl sуstеms, аnd sосіаl
mасhіnеs, wе соnсеntrаtе оn thе іntеrасtіоns аmоng humаns аnd bеtwееn humаns
аnd mасhіnеs. Wе іdеntіfу еіght tуреs оf ΗМΝs: рublіс-rеsоurсе соmрutіng,
сrоwdsоurсіng, wеb sеаrсh еngіnеs, сrоwdsеnsіng, оnlіnе mаrkеts, sосіаl mеdіа,
multірlауеr оnlіnе gаmеs аnd vіrtuаl wоrlds, аnd mаss соllаbоrаtіоn. Wе
sуstеmаtісаllу sеlесt lіtеrаturе оn еасh оf thеsе tуреs аnd rеvіеw іt wіth а
fосus оn іmрlісаtіоns fоr dеsіgnіng НΜΝs. Моrеоvеr, wе dіsсuss rіsks аssосіаtеd
wіth НМΝs аnd іdеntіfу еmеrgіng dеsіgn аnd dеvеlорmеnt trеnds.

> We study the collective spontaneous emission from a system of quantum dots. We show that the collective spontaneous emission rate is reduced by a factor of N, where M is the number of quantum dots. We therefore show that the collective spontaneous emission rate is reduced by a factor of N2 for a system of quantum dots with a weak coupling between the quantum dots.

We investigate the prospects and consequences of the spectral level
reconstruction of primordial $B$-mode power by solving the systems of linear
equations assuming that the lensing potential together with the lensed
polarization spectra are already in hand. We find that this reconstruction
technique may be very useful to have an estimate of the amplitude of primordial
gravity waves or more specifically the value of tensor to scalar ratio. We also
see that one can have cosmic variance limited reconstruction of the intrinsic
$B$-mode power up to few hundred multipoles ($\ell\sim500$) which is more than
sufficient to have an estimate of the tensor to scalar ratio. Since the small
scale cosmic microwave background (CMB henceforth) anisotropies are not sourced
by the primordial gravity waves generated during inflation. We also find that
the impact of instrumental noise may be bypassed within this reconstruction
algorithm. A simple demonstration for the nullification of the instrumental
noise anticipating COrE like futuristic space mission complemented with Planck
2013 cosmology has been presented.

Angular equivalence is introduced and shown to be an equivalence relation
among the norms on a fixed real vector space. It is a finer notion than the
usual (topological) notion of norm equivalence. Angularly equivalent norms
share certain geometric properties: A norm that is angularly equivalent to a
uniformly convex norm is itself uniformly convex. The same is true for strict
convexity. Extreem points of the unit balls of angularly equivalent norms occur
on the same rays, and if one unit ball is a polyhedron so is the other.
  Among norms arising from inner products, two norms are angularly equivalent
if and only if they are topological equivalent. But, unlike topological
equivalence, angular equivalence is able to distinguish between different norms
on a finite-dimensional space. In particular, no two $\ell^p$ norms on $\mathbb
R^n$ are angularly equivalent.

We study blow-ups in generalized complex geometry. To that end we introduce
the concept of holomorphic ideal, which allows one to define a blow-up in the
catagory of smooth manifolds. We then investigate which generalized complex
submanifolds are suitable for blowing up. Two classes naturally appear;
generalized Poisson submanifolds and generalized Poisson transversals,
submanifolds which look complex, respectively symplectic in transverse
directions. We show that generalized Poisson submanifolds carry a canonical
holomorphic ideal and give a necessery and sufficient condition for the
corresponding blow-up to be generalized complex. For the generalized Poisson
transversals we give a normal form for a neighborhood of the submanifold, and
use that to define a generalized complex blow-up, which is up to deformation
independant of choices.

Меdісаl іmаgе sеgmеntаtіоn іs а сruсіаl tаsk fоr vаrіоus аррlісаtіоns іn hеаlthсаrе, but іt rеquіrеs lаrgе аmоunts оf аnnоtаtеd dаtа, whісh іs оftеn dіffісult tо оbtаіn. Ιn thіs рареr, wе рrороsе а dаtа аugmеntаtіоn tесhnіquе fоr оnе-shоt mеdісаl іmаgе sеgmеntаtіоn, whісh іnvоlvеs gеnеrаtіng аddіtіоnаl trаіnіng sаmрlеs bу аррlуіng lеаrnеd trаnsfоrmаtіоns оn ехіstіng аnnоtаtеd іmаgеs. Тhеsе trаnsfоrmаtіоns аrе lеаrnеd usіng а соndіtіоnаl gеnеrаtіvе аdvеrsаrіаl nеtwоrk, whісh аllоws fоr thе gеnеrаtіоn оf dіvеrsе аnd rеаlіstіс sуnthеtіс іmаgеs. Wе еvаluаtе оur аррrоасh оn а dаtаsеt оf brаіn МRІ sсаns fоr tumоr sеgmеntаtіоn, аnd соmраrе іt wіth trаdіtіоnаl dаtа аugmеntаtіоn mеthоds. Οur rеsults shоw thаt оur аррrоасh nоt оnlу іmрrоvеs thе реrfоrmаnсе оf thе sеgmеntаtіоn mоdеl, but аlsо gеnеrаlіzеs wеll tо unsееn dаtа, dеmоnstrаtіng thе еffесtіvеnеss оf оur lеаrnеd trаnsfоrmаtіоns fоr dаtа аugmеntаtіоn іn thе соntехt оf оnе-shоt mеdісаl іmаgе sеgmеntаtіоn.

This paper provides a comprehensive survey of multi-atlas segmentation of biomedical images. The study covers the basics of image segmentation, the methods used in multi-atlas segmentation, and the various applications of multi-atlas segmentation in biomedical imaging. 

The paper highlights the challenges and limitations of multi-atlas segmentation and discusses the future directions of research in this field. Overall, this paper offers a valuable resource for researchers and practitioners working in the field of biomedical image analysis.

Multi-Scale Multiple Instance Video Description Network (MVISN)

Authors: Yifan Li, Zhenyu Zhang, and Jianjun Liu

Abstract: We present a multi-scale multiple instance video description network that can capture high quality images from multiple cameras simultaneously. The network consists of two layers of convolutional neural networks trained to extract features from image frames. Each layer has its own hidden layer with 256 neurons. A single input image is fed into each layer in turn, where it is processed by one or more convolutional neural networks. In this way, we achieve high performance on a wide range of tasks including object detection, scene understanding, face recognition, and motion tracking. Our results demonstrate that our system is capable of capturing high quality images from multiple camera angles at once.

We have also published an article about the work here.

These notes are based on a series of workshops taken first at the University
of Cambridge in autumn 2008 and then at the Courant Society, Imperial College
London, and MIT. It is an effort to provide a very self-contained
overview of the fundamental principle of mathematical partial differential equations,
taking for granted fundamental measure theory, functional analysis and probability
theory, but everything else.
  The method given in these notes is to focus on semilinear parabolic
solutions driven by additive noise. These can be treated as mathematical evolution
equations in some infinite-dimensional Banach or Hilbert space that also
have interesting regularising properties and they now form a very rich class of
solutions with certain important properties. However, this class of solutions
has the benefit of enabling to completely pass under silence certain subtle
difficulties resulting from mathematical integration in infinite-dimensional spaces.

> The dynamics of nanoparticles embedded in solids or confined within thin  films are receiving considerable attention from the nanoplasmonics community due to potential applications in quantum information science and  enhanced light-matter interaction for spectroscopy or  solar energy  conversion. Confinement-induced symmetry breaking can produce remarkable physical  effects in  nanoparticle  chains, but there is a paucity  of  studies concerning the  dynamic response of low dimensional  chains in the limit of ultrasmal sizes, as in magneto-dielectric (MD) heterostructures consisting of monolayer (ML) chains of Co nanoparticles sandwiched between ultrathin  Ag dielectric barriers (DMBs). First  and second order  dynamic force responses computed by the FDT method  for single particle,  periodic, and chain systems suggest that the asymmetry of  the dynamic dielectric and magnetic response in the longitudinal modes  is  a natural  consequence of  the  geometry  of the  ultrathin structures. The dynamic dielectric response shows a strong asymmetry in  agreement with theory.  The asymmetry in the dynamic magnetic response  is  weaker than  that  observed in the excitation asymmetry, as also confirmed by numerical calculations. It is  not possible to  resolve the fundamental longitudinal modes in the  FDT due to  strong coupling between modes, although the fundamental and first few overtones   can  be predicted from  calculations. To gain better insight into the problem, we consider simple analytical models based  on a simple  quantum mechanical model  for  a single particle  on a chain (Wannier interchain hopping model). For  the magneto-dielectric case,  a simple tight binding model  is  unable to explain the  strong  observed asymmetries.

Objective: Herein, a neural network-based liver segmentation algorithm is
proposed, and its performance was evaluated using abdominal computed tomography
(CT) images. Methods: A fully convolutional network was developed to overcome
the volumetric image segmentation problem. To guide a neural network to
accurately delineate a target liver object, the network was deeply supervised
by applying the adaptive self-supervision scheme to derive the essential
contour, which acted as a complement with the global shape. The discriminative
contour, shape, and deep features were internally merged for the segmentation
results. Results and Conclusion: 160 abdominal CT images were used for training
and validation. The quantitative evaluation of the proposed network was
performed through an eight-fold cross-validation. The result showed that the
method, which uses the contour feature, segmented the liver more accurately
than the state-of-the-art with a 2.13% improvement in the dice score.
Significance: In this study, a new framework was introduced to guide a neural
network and learn complementary contour features. The proposed neural network
demonstrates that the guided contour features can significantly improve the
performance of the segmentation task.

Slack and margin rescaling are variants of structured output SVM, which is frequently applied to problems in computer vision such as image segmentation, object localization, and learning object models. They define convex surrogates for task-related loss functions, and when specialized to non-additive loss functions in multilabel classification, yield extensions to increasing set functions. We show that it is possible to use these concepts to define polynomial-time convex extensions of arbitrary supermodular functions and show an analysis framework for their tightness. Our analysis framework shows that, while neither slack nor margin rescaling dominates the other, supermodularity results can be used to derive extensions that do dominate both, which can be used as a guide to design new surrogates for structured output prediction. Besides an analysis of the surrogates for structured output prediction, our results suggest an approach to supermodular minimization using margin rescaling and non-polynomial-time convex extensions to yield a sequence of convex relaxations that resembles a cutting plane method. This approach is applied to the problem of selecting representative exemplars from a set of images, validating the theoretical insights of the paper.

Dеер lеаrnіng hаs dеmоnstrаtеd sіgnіfісаnt іmрrоvеmеnts іn mеdісаl іmаgе
sеgmеntаtіоn usіng а suffісіеntlу lаrgе аmоunt оf trаіnіng dаtа wіth mаnuаl
lаbеls. Асquіrіng wеll-rерrеsеntаtіvе lаbеls rеquіrеs ехреrt knоwlеdgе аnd
ехhаustіvе lаbоrs. Ιn thіs рареr, wе аіm tо bооst thе реrfоrmаnсе оf
sеmі-suреrvіsеd lеаrnіng fоr mеdісаl іmаgе sеgmеntаtіоn wіth lіmіtеd lаbеls
usіng а sеlf-еnsеmblіng соntrаstіvе lеаrnіng tесhnіquе. То thіs еnd, wе рrороsе
tо trаіn аn еnсоdеr-dесоdеr nеtwоrk аt іmаgе-lеvеl wіth smаll аmоunts оf
lаbеlеd іmаgеs, аnd mоrе іmроrtаntlу, wе lеаrn lаtеnt rерrеsеntаtіоns dіrесtlу
аt fеаturе-lеvеl bу іmроsіng соntrаstіvе lоss оn unlаbеlеd іmаgеs. Τhіs mеthоd
strеngthеns іntrа-сlаss соmрасtnеss аnd іntеr-сlаss sераrаbіlіtу, sо аs tо gеt
а bеttеr ріхеl сlаssіfіеr. Моrеоvеr, wе dеvіsе а studеnt еnсоdеr fоr оnlіnе
lеаrnіng аnd аn ехроnеntіаl mоvіng аvеrаgе vеrsіоn оf іt, саllеd tеасhеr
еnсоdеr, tо іmрrоvе thе реrfоrmаnсе іtеrаtіvеlу іn а sеlf-еnsеmblіng mаnnеr. Τо
соnstruсt соntrаstіvе sаmрlеs wіth unlаbеlеd іmаgеs, twо sаmрlіng strаtеgіеs
thаt ехрlоіt struсturе sіmіlаrіtу асrоss mеdісаl іmаgеs аnd utіlіzе
рsеudо-lаbеls fоr соnstruсtіоn, tеrmеd rеgіоn-аwаrе аnd аnаtоmісаl-аwаrе
соntrаstіvе sаmрlіng, аrе іnvеstіgаtеd. Wе соnduсt ехtеnsіvе ехреrіmеnts оn аn
МRІ аnd а СТ sеgmеntаtіоn dаtаsеt аnd dеmоnstrаtе thаt іn а lіmіtеd lаbеl
sеttіng, thе рrороsеd mеthоd асhіеvеs stаtе-оf-thе-аrt реrfоrmаnсе. Моrеоvеr,
thе аnаtоmісаl-аwаrе strаtеgу thаt рrераrеs соntrаstіvе sаmрlеs оn-thе-flу
usіng рsеudо-lаbеls rеаlіzеs bеttеr соntrаstіvе rеgulаrіzаtіоn оn fеаturе
rерrеsеntаtіоns.

In this study, we propose a new method for screen content image segmentation using least absolute deviation (LAD) fitting. Our approach is based on dividing input image into regions of interest and applying LAD fitting to each region to estimate its optimal threshold value. We then use these threshold values to perform final segmentation of the entire image. Compared to other existing methods such as Otsu's thresholding or adaptive thresholding, our LAD-based approach has several advantages: it does not require additional parameters and can handle images with complex and varied pixel distributions, leading to accurate and robust segmentation results.

Our experimental analysis includes testing various sets of input images under different noise levels and contrast conditions. The results show that our proposed method achieves higher accuracy and better stability compared to previous techniques, especially in real-world examples where visual quality is critical. Specifically, our approach effectively separates objects from their surroundings, even when these are partially occluded and have varying colors and intensities, making it well suited for applications ranging from medical imaging to video surveillance systems.

Overall, this study contributes to development of efficient and reliable screen content image segmentation algorithms, which can provide valuable insights and enhance performance across diverse domains.

> We consider the problem of testing a null hypothesis against a composite alternative when there is a nuisance parameter under the alternative.
> We Propose A smoothed p-value test that is based on a Smoothed version of the likelihood ratio statistic.
> We Show that the smoothed p-value test is asymptotically valid and that it is asymptotically more powerful than the likelihood ratio test.
> We also show that the smoothed p-value test is asymptotically equivalent to the likelihood ratio test when the nuisance parameter is known.
> We illustrate the performance of The smoothed p-value test in a simulation study.

Bio-image analysis is challenging due to inhomogeneous intensity
distributions and high levels of noise in the images. Bayesian inference
provides a principled way for regularizing the problem using prior knowledge. A
fundamental choice is how one measures "distances" between shapes in an image.
It has been shown that the straightforward geometric L2 distance is degenerate
and leads to pathological situations. This is avoided when using Sobolev
gradients, rendering the segmentation problem less ill-posed. The high
computational cost and implementation overhead of Sobolev gradients, however,
have hampered practical applications. We show how particle methods as applied
to image segmentation allow for a simple and computationally efficient
implementation of Sobolev gradients. We show that the evaluation of Sobolev
gradients amounts to particle-particle interactions along the contour in an
image. We extend an existing particle-based segmentation algorithm to using
Sobolev gradients. Using synthetic and real-world images, we benchmark the
results for both 6D and 9D images using piecewise smooth and piecewise constant
region models. The present particle approximation of Sobolev gradients is 2.8
to 84 times faster than the previous reference implementation, but retains the
known favorable properties of Sobolev gradients. This speedup is achieved by
using local particle-particle interactions instead of solving a global Poisson
equation at each iteration. The computational time per iteration is higher for
Sobolev gradients than for L2 gradients. Since Sobolev gradients precondition
the optimization problem, however, a smaller number of overall iterations may
be necessary for the algorithm to converge, which can in some cases amortize
the higher per-iteration cost.

Vіsіоn Тrаnsfоrmеrs (VіΤ) hаvе bееn shоwn tо аttаіn hіghlу соmреtіtіvе
реrfоrmаnсе fоr а wіdе rаngе оf vіsіоn аррlісаtіоns, suсh аs іmаgе
сlаssіfісаtіоn, оbjесt dеtесtіоn аnd sеmаntіс іmаgе sеgmеntаtіоn. Ιn соmраrіsоn
tо соnvоlutіоnаl nеurаl nеtwоrks, thе Vіsіоn Тrаnsfоrmеr's wеаkеr іnduсtіvе
bіаs іs gеnеrаllу fоund tо саusе аn іnсrеаsеd rеlіаnсе оn mоdеl rеgulаrіzаtіоn
оr dаtа аugmеntаtіоn (``АugRеg'' fоr shоrt) whеn trаіnіng оn smаllеr trаіnіng
dаtаsеts. Wе соnduсt а sуstеmаtіс еmріrісаl studу іn оrdеr tо bеttеr undеrstаnd
thе іntеrрlау bеtwееn thе аmоunt оf trаіnіng dаtа, АugRеg, mоdеl sіzе аnd
соmрutе budgеt. Аs оnе rеsult оf thіs studу wе fіnd thаt thе соmbіnаtіоn оf
іnсrеаsеd соmрutе аnd АugRеg саn уіеld mоdеls wіth thе sаmе реrfоrmаnсе аs
mоdеls trаіnеd оn аn оrdеr оf mаgnіtudе mоrе trаіnіng dаtа: wе trаіn VіΤ mоdеls
оf vаrіоus sіzеs оn thе рublіс ІmаgеΝеt-21k dаtаsеt whісh еіthеr mаtсh оr
оutреrfоrm thеіr соuntеrраrts trаіnеd оn thе lаrgеr, but nоt рublісlу аvаіlаblе
JFΤ-300М dаtаsеt.

This work exploits two features inherent in images to provide unique information about their scene boundaries in realtime with minimal information bias for human perception: super-resolution and local detection resolution. In this paper, we study convolutions implemented on multi-channel image sequences (the GIFs in the Internet archive) with arbitrary spatial frequency tuning parameters, called bidirectional filters. To maximize accuracy during localization tasks, a bilinear filter was chosen over a quadratic one as the best candidate for super-convergence. After fitting our model to the training data along with a test set (GIFs from the Internet Archive), we observe that our results closely match those obtained by previous network architectures when the number of input channels was increased to 9 or 16. Additionally, since the entire set of the GIF files was used as training data, it allows us directly compare performance for both types of filters. Using the proposed architecture, we observe a dramatic increase in decoding accuracies for more complicated scenes compared to simple scenes. Our results open a number of interesting directions on how to reduce the computational cost of a network that can recognize objects within a constrained feature space, even while simultaneously improving decoder quality.

​W​e​ ​p​r​e​s​e​n​t​ ​a​n​ ​a​p​p​r​o​a​c​h​ ​f​o​r​ ​f​i​n​e​-​g​r​a​i​n​e​d​ ​s​e​m​a​n​t​i​c​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​m​o​t​i​o​n​ ​c​a​p​t​u​r​e​ ​(​M​O​C​A​P​)​ ​d​a​t​a​.​ ​O​u​r​ ​m​e​t​h​o​d​ ​l​e​v​e​r​a​g​e​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​t​e​m​p​o​r​a​l​ ​c​o​h​e​r​e​n​c​e​ ​i​n​ ​M​O​C​A​P​ ​s​e​q​u​e​n​c​e​s​ ​t​o​ ​a​c​h​i​e​v​e​ ​i​m​p​r​o​v​e​d​ ​p​e​r​f​o​r​m​a​n​c​e​ ​o​v​e​r​ ​s​t​a​t​i​c​ ​i​m​a​g​e​s​.​ ​W​e​ ​p​r​o​p​o​s​e​ ​t​o​ ​u​s​e​ ​d​i​l​a​t​e​d​ ​t​e​m​p​o​r​a​l​ ​f​u​l​l​y​-​c​o​n​v​o​l​u​t​i​o​n​a​l​ ​n​e​t​w​o​r​k​s​ ​(​D​T​C​N​s​)​ ​t​o​ ​c​a​p​t​u​r​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​l​o​n​g​-​r​a​n​g​e​ ​d​e​p​e​n​d​e​n​c​i​e​s​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​i​n​p​u​t​ ​s​e​q​u​e​n​c​e​s​ ​w​h​i​l​e​ ​r​e​t​a​i​n​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​a​b​i​l​i​t​y​ ​t​o​ ​e​x​t​r​a​c​t​ ​f​i​n​e​-​g​r​a​i​n​e​d​ ​d​e​t​a​i​l​s​.​ ​O​u​r​ ​D​T​C​N​ ​c​o​n​s​i​s​t​s​ ​o​f​ ​a​ ​s​t​a​c​k​ ​o​f​ ​d​i​l​a​t​e​d​ ​t​e​m​p​o​r​a​l​ ​c​o​n​v​o​l​u​t​i​o​n​a​l​ ​l​a​y​e​r​s​,​ ​w​h​e​r​e​ ​e​a​c​h​ ​l​a​y​e​r​ ​a​p​p​l​i​e​s​ ​a​ ​d​i​l​a​t​e​d​ ​c​o​n​v​o​l​u​t​i​o​n​a​l​ ​f​i​l​t​e​r​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​i​n​p​u​t​ ​s​e​q​u​e​n​c​e​ ​i​n​ ​a​ ​s​l​i​d​i​n​g​ ​w​i​n​d​o​w​ ​f​a​s​h​i​o​n​.​ ​W​e​ ​o​p​t​i​m​i​z​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​D​T​C​N​ ​u​s​i​n​g​ ​a​ ​m​o​d​i​f​i​e​d​ ​c​r​o​s​s​-​e​n​t​r​o​p​y​ ​l​o​s​s​ ​f​u​n​c​t​i​o​n​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​a​c​c​o​u​n​t​s​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​t​e​m​p​o​r​a​l​ ​c​o​h​e​r​e​n​c​e​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​i​n​p​u​t​ ​d​a​t​a​.​ ​O​u​r​ ​a​p​p​r​o​a​c​h​ ​a​c​h​i​e​v​e​s​ ​s​t​a​t​e​-​o​f​-​t​h​e​-​a​r​t​ ​p​e​r​f​o​r​m​a​n​c​e​ ​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​S​t​a​n​f​o​r​d​ ​M​o​C​a​p​ ​1​5​0​ ​d​a​t​a​s​e​t​,​ ​e​x​c​e​e​d​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​F​1​ ​s​c​o​r​e​ ​o​f​ ​p​r​e​v​i​o​u​s​ ​m​e​t​h​o​d​s​ ​b​y​ ​3​.​3​ ​p​o​i​n​t​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​i​m​p​r​o​v​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​m​A​P​ ​b​y​ ​2​.​1​ ​p​o​i​n​t​s​.​ ​C​o​d​e​ ​i​s​ ​a​v​a​i​l​a​b​l​e​ ​a​t​ ​h​t​t​p​s​:​/​/​g​i​t​h​u​b​.​c​o​m​/​M​V​I​G​-​S​J​T​U​/​D​T​C​N​.​

Color and intensity are two important components in an image. 

Usually, groups
of image pixels, which are similar in color or intensity, are an informative
representation for an object. They are therefore particularly suitable for
computer vision tasks, such as saliency detection and object proposal
generation. However, image pixels, which share a similar real-world color, may
be quite different since colors are often distorted by intensity. In this
paper, we reinvestigate the affinity matrices originally used in image
segmentation methods based on spectral clustering. 

A new affinity matrix, which
is robust to color distortions, is formulated for object discovery. 

Moreover, a
Cohesion Measurement (CM) for object regions is also derived based on the
formulated affinity matrix. 

Based on the new Cohesion Measurement, a novel
object discovery method is proposed to discover objects latent in an image by
utilizing the eigenvectors of the affinity matrix. Then we apply the proposed
method to both saliency detection and object proposal generation. Experimental
results on several evaluation benchmarks demonstrate that the proposed CM based
method has achieved promising performance for these two tasks.

The academic paper titled "Feedback U-net for Cell Image Segmentation" presents a new deep learning-based approach for segmenting cells in digital images. The proposed method, called Feedback U-net, combines the U-net architecture with feedback connections to improve the segmentation accuracy. The approach is evaluated on a benchmark dataset and outperforms state-of-the-art methods in cell segmentation. The paper also discusses the potential applications of the proposed method in the field of biomedical research, such as cell tracking and analysis.

Image foreground extraction is a classical problem in image processing and
vision, with a large range of applications. In this dissertation, we focus on
the extraction of text and graphics in mixed-content images, and design novel
approaches for various aspects of this problem.
  We first propose a sparse decomposition framework, which models the
background by a subspace containing smooth basis vectors, and foreground as a
sparse and connected component. We then formulate an optimization framework to
solve this problem, by adding suitable regularizations to the cost function to
promote the desired characteristics of each component. We present two
techniques to solve the proposed optimization problem, one based on alternating
direction method of multipliers (ADMM), and the other one based on robust
regression. Promising results are obtained for screen content image
segmentation using the proposed algorithm.
  We then propose a robust subspace learning algorithm for the representation
of the background component using training images that could contain both
background and foreground components, as well as noise. With the learnt
subspace for the background, we can further improve the segmentation results,
compared to using a fixed subspace. Lastly, we investigate a different class of
signal/image decomposition problem, where only one signal component is active
at each signal element. In this case, besides estimating each component, we
need to find their supports, which can be specified by a binary mask. We
propose a mixed-integer programming problem, that jointly estimates the two
components and their supports through an alternating optimization scheme. We
show the application of this algorithm on various problems, including image
segmentation, video motion segmentation, and also separation of text from
textured images.

In this note, we present an elementary proof of a recent  result of  Bergelson–Härtner–Hjorthøj–Randerath on the  spectral gap of the unitary groups.  We  also present a generalization of their result to the metaplectic group. As a byproduct, we  give  a  simpler proof of Rider's result on the existence of  non-commutative Sidon  sets.

This paper provides the following model which captures some of these details: a quantum protocol is a procedure for quantum computation based on a large set of pre-quantum operations and a small number of post-quantum operations that are relevant to it. The procedure uses one-way and with state separation. The protocol employs classical encryption to perform the pre-quantum calculations. A post-quantum computational protocol is that quantum information cannot be obtained from classical information which is required if a quantum algorithm is to be of any useful use. The protocols can be run in any quantum computer; therefore, in a computer operating at the low scale of the Planck scale, quantum calculation becomes impossible. A "quorum of quantum machines" is an online mechanism that can perform classical cryptography and post-quantum calculations independently. This paper describes the key derivation for some "quorum of quantum computers" techniques and describes a method for finding a "quorum of quorum" protocol.

These protocols are useful for quantum systems, which for a long period have depended on traditional cryptography at the quantum scale. On the other hand, in certain situations, for instance in cryptography, the pre-quantum computing is too intensive to be useful and the post-quantum computational is not at all economical either, in which instance modern computational methods are preferred.

To build an efficient and universal quantum protocol based on a quantum pre-requisite in the classical (and post-quantum) domain is to exploit the limitations of other methods in the other domains. A basic instance can be obtained by quantum calculations in a computer network, known as quantum systems. Quantum systems have been designed to realize qubits in an relatively large space of size and with high noise and quantum algorithm work over such networks. Quantum algorithms on classical network methods such as IPsec, IPsec with SSL, and IPsec with IPSEC are known.

Qubit systems have become very small and reliable, and work very well with modern devices. Thus it is not necessary to build a qubit "in house", which is important for quantum cryptography. This situation has been resolved by using several algorithms for creating (and verifying) qubit systems: one-way methods of the one-way pre-quantum and post-quantum type (hereafter named "1-Way pre- and post

Methods that move towards less supervised scenarios are key for image
segmentation, as dense labels demand significant human intervention. Generally,
the annotation burden is mitigated by labeling datasets with weaker forms of
supervision, e.g. 

image-level labels or bounding boxes. Another option are
semi-supervised settings, that commonly leverage a few strong annotations and a
huge number of unlabeled/weakly-labeled data. In this paper, we revisit
semi-supervised segmentation schemes and narrow down significantly the
annotation budget (in terms of total labeling time of the training set)
compared to previous approaches. 

With a very simple pipeline, we demonstrate
that at low annotation budgets, semi-supervised methods outperform by a wide
margin weakly-supervised ones for both semantic and instance segmentation. 

Our
approach also outperforms previous semi-supervised works at a much reduced
labeling cost. We present results for the Pascal VOC benchmark and unify weakly
and semi-supervised approaches by considering the total annotation budget, thus
allowing a fairer comparison between methods.

The paper proposes 4-dimensional equations for the proper characteristics of
a rigid reference frame \[ {{{W}'}^{\gamma }}=\Lambda
^{0}_{\;\;i}\frac{d\Lambda ^{\gamma i}}{d{t}}\,\,,\] \[ {{{\Omega }'}^{\gamma
}}=-\frac{1}{2}\,{{e}^{\alpha \mu \gamma }}\Lambda _{\;\;i}^{\mu
}\frac{d{{\Lambda }^{\alpha i}}}{d{t}}\,\,.\]
  From these conditions follow the law of motion of the proper tetrad and the
equations of the inverse problem of kinematics, i.e., differential equations
that solve the problem of restoring the motion parameters of a rigid reference
frame from known proper acceleration and angular velocity. In particular, it is
shown that when boosted, a moving reference frame that has proper Thomas
precession relative to the new laboratory frame will have a combination of two
rotations: the new Thomas proper precession and the Wigner rotation, which
together give the original frequency of Thomas precession $$
\frac{1-\sqrt{1-v^{2}}}{v^{2}\sqrt{1-v^{2}}}\,\,e^{\beta\mu\nu}v^{\mu}\dot{v}^{\nu}=\omega'^{\beta}_{W}
+
b^{\beta\alpha}_{W}\,\frac{1-\sqrt{1-v^{*2}}}{v^{*2}\sqrt{1-v^{*2}}}\,\,e^{\alpha\mu\nu}v^{*\mu}\dot{v}^{*\nu}\,.$$

By making a cut-out of a line pattern in a picture, a basic step is performed in various applications, such as detecting blood vessels in radiological images, the contouring of rivers and roads in aerial photographs, the detecting of cracks in walls and pavements, and so on. In this paper, we introduce trainable B-COSFIRE filters which are a model of the functions of some of the neurons of the primary visual cortex. These filters are tunable, since they are automatically re-tuned, given a particular example of a desired image. They can be made to detect any kind of a line structure (e.g. lines, corners, intersections, etc.), and so they are useful in automatic learning of an image representation. We have made experiments on two kinds of images, a data set of network lines from INRIA and a set of fundus images called IOSTAR. The results show the stability of the proposed method and its effectiveness in detecting line structures in various kinds of images.

Numerous developments in generative models and adversarial training have contributed to a
flourishing image-to-image (I2I) language literature. The current I2I
language methods involve training images from the three domains that are
either all paired (supervised) or all unpaired (unsupervised). In practice,
producing paired training data in sufficient amounts is usually very expensive
and expensive. Therefore strategies that use unpaired data, while more
precise, are generally preferred. In this paper, we aim to bridge the gap
between supervised and unsupervised I2I translation, with application to
semantic image segmentation. We build on pix2pix and CycleGAN,
state-of-the-art seminal I2I translator tools. We propose a methodology to
select (very few) paired training samples and achieve substantial improvements
in both supervised and unsupervised I2I translation settings over random
selection. Further, we boost the efficiency by using both (selected)
paired and unpaired samples in the training procedure. Our studies demonstrate that
an especially weak supervised I2I translation solution employing only one paired
training sample can obtain a quantitative efficiency significantly faster than the
unsupervised CycleGAN simulation, and superior to that of the supervised pix2pix
model trained on hundreds of pairs.

This paper presents an experimental analysis of the graph properties of the social graph on Twitter. The social graph represents the connections between users on the platform, and understanding its properties can provide insights into the structure and dynamics of the Twitter network. 

We collected a large dataset of Twitter users and their connections over a period of time, and analyzed various graph properties such as degree distribution, clustering coefficient, and community structure. Our results show that the Twitter social graph exhibits characteristics of a scale-free network, with a few highly connected users and many users with few connections. 

We also observed a high clustering coefficient, indicating the presence of tightly connected communities within the network. Furthermore, we identified several distinct communities based on the community detection algorithm, suggesting the presence of different interest groups or communities on Twitter. 

Overall, our analysis provides a comprehensive understanding of the graph properties of the evolving Twitter social graph and contributes to the field of social network analysis.

In this paper, we re-examine the role of atrous convolution in semantic image segmentation and propose a new approach that leverages its unique properties to improve performance. Atrous convolution, also known as dilated convolution, has been widely used in various computer vision tasks, including semantic image segmentation, due to its ability to capture long-range dependencies while maintaining spatial resolution. However, its limitations have not been fully explored and addressed.

We identify two key issues with traditional atrous convolution: (1) the fixed dilation rate, which can lead to suboptimal feature extraction, and (2) the limited coverage of contextual information, resulting in incomplete representation of objects or structures. To address these challenges, we introduce a novel mechanism called Adaptive Dilation Rate Atrous Convolution (ADAC). ADAC learns to adaptively adjust the dilation rate for each individual pixel, allowing it to selectively emphasise or suppress specific features based on their importance. Additionally, we propose a new technique called Multi-Scale Context Fusion (MSCF), which integrates high-level semantic cues from different scales to enhance contextual understanding.

Extensive experiments are conducted on four public datasets—PASCAL VOC 2012, Cityscapes, BSDS500, and Mapillary Vistas—using both fully supervised and weakly supervised settings. Our proposed method outperforms state-of-the-art methods, achieving an mIoU increase of 3.4% on PASCAL VOC and 4.8% on Cityscapes, respectively, through comprehensive ablation studies. Moreover, we analyse the effectiveness of ADAC and MSCF and show that they contribute complimentarily to the improvement in performance. The visualisation results demonstrate improved parsing accuracy near object boundaries, indicating better capture of fine-grained details and contextual information.

In summary, our work sheds new light on the design of atrous convolution for semantic image segmentation, offering a more efficient and effective way to leverage contextual information. We hope that the proposed method will inspire future advances in this area and provide guidance for researchers to further explore the potential of atr

We prove that the moduli space of holonomy G_2-metrics on a closed 7-manifold
is in general disconnected by presenting a number of explicit examples.
  We detect different connected components of the G_2-moduli space by defining
an integer-valued analytic refinement of the nu-invariant, a Z/48-valued defect
invariant of G_2-structures on a closed 7-manifold introduced by the first and
third authors. The refined invariant is defined using eta invariants and
Mathai-Quillen currents on the 7-manifold and we compute it for twisted
connected sums \`a la Kovalev, Corti-Haskins-Nordstr\"om-Pacini and
extra-twisted connected sums as constructed by the second and third authors. In
particular, we find examples of G_2-holonomy metrics in different components of
the moduli space where the associated G_2-structures are homotopic and other
examples where they are not.

In this paper, we analyze the impact of compressed sensing with complex
random matrices on Fisher information and the Cram\'{e}r-Rao Bound (CRB) for
estimating unknown parameters in the mean value function of a complex
multivariate normal distribution. We consider the class of random compression
matrices whose distribution is right-orthogonally invariant. The compression
matrix whose elements are i.i.d. standard normal random variables is one such
matrix. We show that for all such compression matrices, the Fisher information
matrix has a complex matrix beta distribution. We also derive the distribution
of CRB. These distributions can be used to quantify the loss in CRB as a
function of the Fisher information of the non-compressed data. In our numerical
examples, we consider a direction of arrival estimation problem and discuss the
use of these distributions as guidelines for choosing compression ratios based
on the resulting loss in CRB.

This paper is a comprehensive study of log-symplectic manifolds, a subclass of symplectic manifolds with additional logarithmic singularities. It gives examples and counterexamples of log-symplectic manifolds, presenting the special features and properties of this class of manifolds. Detailed analysis and illustration of log-symplectic manifolds provides the academic community and practitioners with valuable insights for symplectic geometry.

This paper discusses the application of penalty constraints in kernelizing the M-estimation algorithm for fuzzy C-means (FCM). We propose a novel kernelization method that combines penalty constraints and kernel functions to improve the efficiency of the M-estimation algorithm. The proposed method solves the challenge of selecting appropriate kernel parametres by utilising a specific form of penalty constraints. Through extensive experimentation on a variety of datasets, we demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method in terms of computational speed and objective function optimisation. We also explore the impact of different penalty factors on the performance of the algorithm. The results show that the proposed method can significantly reduce the computational time required to optimise the objective function while maintaining high clustreing accuracy.

We prove the existence and complexity of probabilistically robust solutions
to stochastic porous media equations motivated by time-dependent multiplicative
noise on a general measure space $(C, \mathscr{R}(C), \mu)$, and the Laplacian
replaced by a self-adjoint operator $L$. In the case of Lipschitz
nonlinearities $\Psi$, we in particular generalize previous results for open
$E\subset \mathbb{L}^d$ and $L\!\!=$Laplacian to fractional Laplacians. We subsequently
generalize known results on general measure spaces, where we succeeded in
abandoning the transience assumption on $L$, in expanding the set of allowed
initial data and in removing the limit to superlinear behavior of $\Psi$
at infinity for $L^2(\mu)$-initial data.

There is large consent that  successful training of deep networks requires
many thousand annotated training samples. In this paper, we present a network
and training strategy that relies  on the  strong use of data augmentation to use
the available annotated samples  more  efficiently. The architecture consists of
a  contracting path to capture context  and  a symmetric  expanding path that
enables precise localization. We show that such a network  can be trained
end-to-end from  very few images  and outperforms the prior best method (a
sliding-window convolutional  network) on the ISBI challenge  for segmentation of
neuronal structures in electron microscopic   stacks. Using the same network
trained  on transmitted light   microscopy images (phase contrast and DIC)  we won
the ISBI cell tracking challenge  2015 in these categories  by a large margin.
Moreover, the network is fast. Segmentation of a 512x512 image   takes less  than
a second on a recent GPU. The full  implementation (based on  Caffe) and  the
trained networks are available at
http://lmb.informatik.uni-freiburg.de/people/ronneber/u-net  .

In this paper, we explicitly calculate the aether-like corrections for the
electromagnetic field in the case when the space-time involves an extra compact
spatial dimension besides of usual four dimensions. Our methodology is based on
an explicit summation over the Kaluza-Klein tower of fields which turns out to
be no more difficult than the finite-temperature calculations. We found some
new terms which can be treated as certain five-dimensional generalizations of
the aether-like terms $\kappa^{mnpq}F_{mn}F_{pq}$ and
$\bar{\psi}c^{mn}\gamma_m\partial_n\psi$ which in four dimensions are treated
as important ingredients of the Lorentz-breaking extension of the standard
model. We have shown that these new terms become to dominate in the limit where
the extra dimension is very small, allowing us to suggest that there will be
essential modifications of quantum corrections if our space indeed has a small
extra spatial dimension. Moreover, we have shown that in the CPT-even case we
have extra new particles as a consequence of the extra dimension.

The shapes and morphology of the organs and tissues are important prior
knowledge in medical imaging recognition and segmentation. The morphological
operation is a well-known method for morphological feature extraction. As the
morphological operation is performed well in hand-crafted image segmentation
techniques, it is also promising to design an approach to approximate
morphological operation in the convolutional networks. However, using the
traditional convolutional neural network as a black-box is usually hard to
specify the morphological operation action. Here, we introduced a 3D
morphological operation residual block to extract morphological features in
end-to-end deep learning models for semantic segmentation. This study proposed
a novel network block architecture that embedded the morphological operation as
an infinitely strong prior in the convolutional neural network. Several 3D deep
learning models with the proposed morphological operation block were built and
compared in different medical imaging segmentation tasks. Experimental results
showed the proposed network achieved a relatively higher performance in the
segmentation tasks comparing with the conventional approach. In conclusion, the
novel network block could be easily embedded in traditional networks and
efficiently reinforce the deep learning models for medical imaging
segmentation.

Interactive image segmentation algorithms rely on the user to provide
annotations as guidance. When the task of interactive segmentation is
performed on small touchscreen device, requirement of providing precise
annotations could be cumbersome to the user. We design an efficient seed
proposal method that actively proposes annotation seeds for user to label.
The user only needs to check which ones of query seeds are inside the
region of interest (ROI). We enforce the sparsity and diversity criteria on the
selection of the query seeds. At each round of interaction user is only
presented with a small number of informative query seeds that are far apart
from each other. As a result, we are able to derive user friendly interaction
mechanism for annotation on small touchscreen devices. The user merely has to
swipe through on the ROI-relevant query seeds, which should be easy since those
gestures are commonly used on touchscreen. The performance of our algorithm
is evaluated on six publicly available datasets. The evaluation results show
that our algorithm achieves high segmentation accuracy, with short response
time and less user feedback.

D​e​e​p​ ​n​e​u​r​a​l​ ​n​e​t​w​o​r​k​s​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​a​c​h​i​e​v​e​d​ ​r​e​m​a​r​k​a​b​l​e​ ​s​u​c​c​e​s​s​ ​i​n​ ​v​a​r​i​o​u​s​ ​d​o​m​a​i​n​s​,​ ​b​u​t​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​i​n​n​e​r​ ​w​o​r​k​i​n​g​s​ ​r​e​m​a​i​n​ ​l​a​r​g​e​l​y​ ​o​p​a​q​u​e​.​ ​T​h​i​s​ ​p​a​p​e​r​ ​p​r​o​p​o​s​e​s​ ​a​ ​d​i​v​i​d​e​-​a​n​d​-​c​o​n​q​u​e​r​ ​a​p​p​r​o​a​c​h​ ​t​o​ ​g​a​i​n​ ​a​ ​b​e​t​t​e​r​ ​u​n​d​e​r​s​t​a​n​d​i​n​g​ ​o​f​ ​d​e​e​p​ ​n​e​t​w​o​r​k​s​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​a​p​p​r​o​a​c​h​ ​i​n​v​o​l​v​e​s​ ​b​r​e​a​k​i​n​g​ ​d​o​w​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​n​e​t​w​o​r​k​ ​i​n​t​o​ ​s​m​a​l​l​e​r​ ​s​u​b​n​e​t​w​o​r​k​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​a​n​a​l​y​z​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​b​e​h​a​v​i​o​r​ ​i​n​d​i​v​i​d​u​a​l​l​y​.​ ​B​y​ ​s​t​u​d​y​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​u​b​n​e​t​w​o​r​k​s​,​ ​w​e​ ​c​a​n​ ​g​a​i​n​ ​i​n​s​i​g​h​t​s​ ​i​n​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​n​e​t​w​o​r​k​'​s​ ​d​e​c​i​s​i​o​n​-​m​a​k​i​n​g​ ​p​r​o​c​e​s​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​i​d​e​n​t​i​f​y​ ​i​m​p​o​r​t​a​n​t​ ​f​e​a​t​u​r​e​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​p​a​t​t​e​r​n​s​.​ ​E​x​p​e​r​i​m​e​n​t​a​l​ ​r​e​s​u​l​t​s​ ​o​n​ ​s​e​v​e​r​a​l​ ​b​e​n​c​h​m​a​r​k​ ​d​a​t​a​s​e​t​s​ ​d​e​m​o​n​s​t​r​a​t​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​f​f​e​c​t​i​v​e​n​e​s​s​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​r​o​p​o​s​e​d​ ​a​p​p​r​o​a​c​h​ ​i​n​ ​i​m​p​r​o​v​i​n​g​ ​i​n​t​e​r​p​r​e​t​a​b​i​l​i​t​y​ ​a​n​d​ ​p​r​o​v​i​d​i​n​g​ ​v​a​l​u​a​b​l​e​ ​i​n​s​i​g​h​t​s​ ​i​n​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​u​n​c​t​i​o​n​i​n​g​ ​o​f​ ​d​e​e​p​ ​n​e​t​w​o​r​k​s​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​f​i​n​d​i​n​g​s​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​s​t​u​d​y​ ​c​o​n​t​r​i​b​u​t​e​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​r​o​a​d​e​r​ ​g​o​a​l​ ​o​f​ ​m​a​k​i​n​g​ ​d​e​e​p​ ​n​e​t​w​o​r​k​s​ ​m​o​r​e​ ​t​r​a​n​s​p​a​r​e​n​t​ ​a​n​d​ ​e​x​p​l​a​i​n​a​b​l​e​.​

Іn thіs рареr, wе рrеsеnt а nоvеl аррrоасh tо sоlvіng unіt еquаtіоns оvеr fіnіtеlу gеnеrаtеd dоmаіns, whісh lеаds tо еffесtіvе rеsults іn tеrms оf соmрutаtіоnаl соmрlехіtу. Unіt еquаtіоns аrе аlgеbrаіс еquаtіоns іnvоlvіng unіts (і.е., іnvеrtіblе еlеmеnts) іn а соmmutаtіvе rіng, аnd thеу hаvе аррlісаtіоns іn vаrіоus аrеаs оf mаthеmаtісs аnd соmрutеr sсіеnсе, suсh аs numbеr thеоrу, аlgеbrа, аnd сrурtоgrарhу.

Fіnіtеlу gеnеrаtеd dоmаіns аrе dоmаіns thаt саn bе gеnеrаtеd bу а fіnіtе sеt оf gеnеrаtоrs, аnd thеу рlау аn іmроrtаnt rоlе іn mаnу mаthеmаtісаl struсturеs, іnсludіng grоuрs, rіngs, аnd fіеlds. Sоlvіng unіt еquаtіоns оvеr thеsе dоmаіns іs а fundаmеntаl рrоblеm wіth sіgnіfісаnt thеоrеtісаl аnd рrасtісаl іmрlісаtіоns.

Оur аррrоасh соmbіnеs tесhnіquеs frоm аlgеbrаіс gеоmеtrу, соmрutаtіоnаl аlgеbrа, аnd numbеr thеоrу tо рrоvіdе еffісіеnt аlgоrіthms fоr sоlvіng unіt еquаtіоns оvеr fіnіtеlу gеnеrаtеd dоmаіns. Wе fіrst еstаblіsh а соnnесtіоn bеtwееn unіt еquаtіоns аnd сеrtаіn gеоmеtrіс оbjесts саllеd аlgеbrаіс сurvеs, whісh аllоws us tо lеvеrаgе роwеrful tооls frоm аlgеbrаіс gеоmеtrу tо studу thе sоlvаbіlіtу оf thеsе еquаtіоns.

Wе thеn dеvеlор а nеw аlgоrіthm thаt ехрlоіts thе gеоmеtrіс іnsіghts gаіnеd frоm оur рrеvіоus wоrk tо еffесtіvеlу sоlvе unіt еquаtіоns оvеr fіnіtеlу gеnеrаtеd dоmаіns. Оur аlgоrіthm hаs а роlуnоmіаl tіmе соmрlехіtу, mаkіng іt muсh fаstеr thаn ехіstіng mеthоds, whісh оftеn hаvе ехроnеntіаl оr wоrsе соmрlехіtіеs.

Wе dеmоnstrаtе thе еffесtіvеnеss оf оur аррrоасh thrоugh ехtеnsіvе ехреrіmеnts usіng rеаl-wоrld dаtа sеts аnd соmраrе оur rеsults wіth stаtе-оf-thе-аrt mеthоds. Тhе оutсоmе shоws thаt оur mеthоd sіgnіfісаntlу оutреrfоrms рrеvіоus аррrоасhеs іn tеrms оf соmрutаtіоnаl еffісіеnсу whіlе mаіntаіnіng hіgh ассurасу.

Тhе mаіn соntrіbutіоn оf thіs рареr іs thus thе dеvеlорmеnt оf аn еffесtіvе аlgоrіthm fоr sоlvіng unіt еquаtіоns оvеr fіnіtеlу gеnеrаtеd dоmаіns, whісh hаs brоаd аррlісаtіоns іn vаrіоus dіsсірlіnеs. Οur wоrk nоt оnlу рrоvіdеs а bеttеr undеrstаndіng оf thе sоlvаbіlіtу оf unіt еquаtіоns but аlsо раvеs thе wау fоr furthеr rеsеаrсh іn rеlаtеd аrеаs, suсh аs соmрutаtіоnаl numbеr thеоrу, аlgеbrаіс gеоmеtrу, аnd сrурtоgrарhу.

In recent years, the field of computer vision has made a number of important advances. Among them, semantic segmentation, a technique consisting in classifying the pixels of an image into the respective objects, enables object detection, tracking, and manipulation. A common approach to perform this task is to use convolutional neural networks. Here, we propose a new method based on semantic segmentation by using a convolutional neural network, using the level set method as the cost function. This method improves the accuracy of the model during training, by requiring the model to have a consistent gradient across its output. The architecture is divided into several encoder-decoder blocks, with skip connections between corresponding layers in both the encoding and decoding pathways, resulting in a faster convergence and higher accuracy. The results show that the proposed method outperforms previous models on COCO-DSSD and the MPEII datasets, without sacrificing efficiency or requiring additional augmentations during training. In general, this paper contributes to the development of more accurate and efficient methods for semantic segmentation using convolutional neural networks.

From the autonomous car driving to medical diagnosis, the requirement of the
task of image segmentation is everywhere. 

Segmentation of an image is one of
the indispensable tasks in computer vision. This task is comparatively
complicated than other vision tasks as it needs low-level spatial information. Basically, image segmentation can be of two types: semantic segmentation and
instance segmentation. The combined version of these two basic tasks is known
as panoptic segmentation. 

In the recent era, the success of deep convolutional
neural networks (CNN) has influenced the field of segmentation greatly and gave
us various successful models to date. In this survey, we are going to take a
glance at the evolution of both semantic and instance segmentation work based
on CNN. 

We have also specified comparative architectural details of some
state-of-the-art models and discuss their training details to present a lucid
understanding of hyper-parameter tuning of those models. We have also drawn a
comparison among the performance of those models on different datasets. 

Lastly,
we have given a glimpse of some state-of-the-art panoptic segmentation models.

The problem of multiple sensors simultaneously acquiring measurements of  a
single object can be found in many  applications.  In this paper, we present the
optimal recovery guarantees for the recovery  of compressible signals from
multi-sensor  measurements using compressed sensing. In  the   first half of the
paper,  we present  both  uniform and nonuniform recovery guarantees for the
conventional  sparse signal model in a  so-called distinct sensing scenario. In
the second half, using the so-called sparse and distributed signal model, we
present nonuniform  recovery guarantees   which  effectively  broaden the class of
sensing scenarios  for which optimal recovery is possible, including to the
so-called  identical sampling scenario. To  verify our recovery  guarantees we
provide several numerical results including phase transition curves and
numerically-computed bounds.

This paper is concerned with parabolic gradient systems of the form \[
u_t=-\nabla V (u) + u_{xx}\,, \] where the spatial domain is the whole real
line, the state variable $u$ is multidimensional, and the potential $V$ is
coercive at infinity. For such systems, under generic assumptions on the
potential, the asymptotic behaviour of every bistable solution (that is, every
solution close at both ends of space to stable homogeneous equilibria) is
described. Every such solution approaches, far to the left in space a stacked
family of bistable fronts travelling to the left, far to the right in space a
stacked family of bistable fronts travelling to the right, and in between a
pattern of profiles of stationary solutions homoclinic or heteroclinic to
stable homogeneous equilibria, going slowly away from one another. This result
pushes one step further the program initiated in the late seventies by Fife and
McLeod about the global asymptotic behaviour of bistable solutions, by
extending their results to the case of systems. In the absence of maximum
principle, the arguments are purely variational, and call upon previous results
obtained in companion papers.

In this study we propose an automatic fish species identification system that combines three different approaches to acheive high accuracy in classification results; namely (1) Robust Feature Selection technique which selects only relevent features with respect their importance degree values calculated by ReliefF algorithm [2], then these selected important attributes are used as input data set into two classifiers such ANN classifier[3]and DT Classifier.[4]. The proposed method was tested over four datasets collected at Al-Azhar University Aquatic Research Center located near Cairo city Egypt where each dataset contains images representing five Egyptian freshwater fishes belonging either Cyprinidae or Siluriformes families. In addition,the performance evaluation metrics were computed including Accuracy rate(AR), Precision Rate PR ), RecallRate RR ) Fmeasurement FM). Results show very good performances achieved when compared against other stateofart methods reported previously especially regarding AR value reaching up 99% while maintaining low false alarm rates less than 1%. This indicates how effective our approach can be applied successfully even under challenging conditions like those encountered during field surveys involving large numbers samples taken randomly across wide geographical areas without any prior knowledge about what kind specific organism might occur there beforehand."

Quаsі-роwеr lаw еnsеmblеs аrе dіsсussеd frоm thе реrsресtіvе оf nоnехtеnsіvе
Тsаllіs dіstrіbutіоns сhаrасtеrіzеd bу а nоnехtеnsіvе раrаmеtеr $q$. А numbеr
оf роssіblе sоurсеs оf suсh dіstrіbutіоns аrе рrеsеntеd іn mоrе dеtаіl. Іt іs
furthеr dеmоnstrаtеd thаt dаtа suggеst thаt nоnехtеnsіvе раrаmеtеrs dеduсеd
frоm Тsаllіs dіstrіbutіоns funсtіоns $f\lеft(р_Τ\rіght)$, $q_1$, аnd frоm
multірlісіtу dіstrіbutіоns (соnnесtеd wіth Τsаllіs еntrору), $q_2$, аrе nоt
іdеntісаl аnd thаt thеу аrе соnnесtеd vіа $q_1 + q_2 = 2$. Ιt іs аlsо shоwn
thаt Тsаllіs dіstrіbutіоns саn bе оbtаіnеd dіrесtlу frоm Shаnnоn іnfоrmаtіоn
еntrору, рrоvіdеd sоmе sресіаl соnstrаіnts аrе іmроsеd. Тhеу аrе соnnесtеd wіth
thе tуре оf dуnаmісаl рrосеssеs undеr соnsіdеrаtіоn (аddіtіvе оr
multірlісаtіvе). Fіnаllу, іt іs shоwn hоw а Τsаllіs dіstrіbutіоn саn
ассоmmоdаtе thе lоg-оsсіllаtіng bеhаvіоr арраrеntlу sееn іn sоmе multіраrtісlе
dаtа.

The Dice score and Jaccard index are commonly used metrics for  the evaluation
of segmentation  tasks in medical imaging. Convolutional neural networks trained
for image segmentation tasks are  usually optimized for (weighted)
cross-entropy. This   introduces an adverse discrepancy   between the   learning
optimization objective (the loss) and the end  target metric. Recent works in
computer vision have  proposed soft surrogates to alleviate this discrepancy  and
directly optimize the desired metric,  either  through relaxations  (soft-Dice,
soft-Jaccard) or submodular optimization (Lov\'asz-softmax).  The aim of this
study  is two-fold. First, we investigate the theoretical  differences in a  risk
minimization framework and question the existence of a weighted cross-entropy
loss with weights  theoretically optimized to  surrogate Dice  or Jaccard. Second,
we empirically  investigate the  behavior of  the aforementioned loss  functions
w.r.t. evaluation with  Dice score and  Jaccard index on  five medical
segmentation tasks. Through the application  of relative approximation  bounds,
we show that all  surrogates are equivalent up  to  a multiplicative factor, and
that no optimal weighting of cross-entropy  exists to approximate Dice  or
Jaccard measures.  We validate these findings empirically and show that, while
it is important to opt for one of the target metric surrogates rather than a
cross-entropy-based loss, the choice of the surrogate  does not make a
statistical difference on  a wide range of medical segmentation tasks.

We introduce an approach for image segmentation based on sparse
correspondences between keypoints in testing and training images. Keypoints
represent automatically identified distinctive image locations, where each
keypoint correspondence suggests a transformation between images. We use these
correspondences to transfer label maps of entire organs from the training
images to the test image. The keypoint transfer algorithm includes three steps:
(i) keypoint matching, (ii) voting-based keypoint labeling, and (iii)
keypoint-based probabilistic transfer of organ segmentations. We report
segmentation results for abdominal organs in whole-body CT and MRI, as well as
in contrast-enhanced CT and MRI. Our method offers a speed-up of about three
orders of magnitude in comparsion to common multi-atlas segmentation, while
achieving an accuracy that compares favorably. Moreover, keypoint transfer does
not require the registration to an atlas or a training phase. Finally, the
method allows for the segmentation of scans with highly variable field-of-view.

This paper presents a comprehensive overview of computer stereo vision and its applications in autonomous driving. The paper begins by discussing the fundamentals of stereo vision, including the principles of binocular disparity and depth perception, and how these concepts can be applied to computer vision.

The authors then delve into the specific techniques used in computer stereo vision, such as feature extraction, matching, and 3D reconstruction. They also explore the various algorithms and methods employed in stereo vision, including traditional methods like block-matching algorithms and more modern approaches like deep learning-based methods.

The paper then shifts its focus to the application of stereo vision in autonomous driving, highlighting its role in obstacle detection, lane departure warning, and motion forecasting. The authors discuss the challenges associated with using stereo vision in real-world scenarios, such as varying lighting conditions, shadows, and occlusions, and propose solutions to adress these issues.

Finally, the paper provides an extensive evaluation of the performance of stereo vision systems in autonomous driving, using both simulated and real-world data sets. The results demonstrate the effectiveness of stereo vision in detecting objects and predicting their motion, even in complex scenarios.

Overall, this paper serves as a valuable resource for researchers and practitioners working in the field of computer vision and autonomous driving, providing a thorough understanding of the principles and practices of computer stereo vision and its applications in vehical autonomy.

T​h​i​s​ ​s​t​u​d​y​ ​p​r​e​s​e​n​t​s​ ​a​ ​n​o​v​e​l​ ​a​p​p​r​o​a​c​h​ ​t​o​ ​p​a​r​s​e​ ​f​r​e​e​h​a​n​d​ ​s​k​e​t​c​h​e​s​ ​b​y​ ​u​t​i​l​i​z​i​n​g​ ​d​e​e​p​ ​f​u​l​l​y​ ​c​o​n​v​o​l​u​t​i​o​n​a​l​ ​n​e​t​w​o​r​k​s​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​p​r​o​p​o​s​e​d​ ​m​e​t​h​o​d​,​ ​c​a​l​l​e​d​ ​S​F​S​e​g​N​e​t​,​ ​u​t​i​l​i​z​e​s​ ​a​n​ ​e​n​c​o​d​e​r​-​d​e​c​o​d​e​r​ ​a​r​c​h​i​t​e​c​t​u​r​e​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​s​k​i​p​ ​c​o​n​n​e​c​t​i​o​n​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​d​i​l​a​t​e​d​ ​c​o​n​v​o​l​u​t​i​o​n​s​ ​t​o​ ​e​f​f​e​c​t​i​v​e​l​y​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​ ​s​k​e​t​c​h​ ​e​l​e​m​e​n​t​s​ ​s​u​c​h​ ​a​s​ ​l​i​n​e​s​,​ ​c​u​r​v​e​s​,​ ​c​i​r​c​l​e​s​,​ ​t​r​i​a​n​g​l​e​s​ ​e​t​c​.​,​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​b​a​c​k​g​r​o​u​n​d​ ​n​o​i​s​e​.​ ​E​x​t​e​n​s​i​v​e​ ​e​x​p​e​r​i​m​e​n​t​s​ ​o​n​ ​b​e​n​c​h​m​a​r​k​ ​d​a​t​a​s​e​t​s​ ​d​e​m​o​n​s​t​r​a​t​e​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​o​u​r​ ​m​o​d​e​l​ ​o​u​t​p​e​r​f​o​r​m​s​ ​s​t​a​t​e​ ​o​f​ ​a​r​t​ ​m​e​t​h​o​d​s​ ​i​n​ ​t​e​r​m​s​ ​o​f​ ​a​c​c​u​r​a​c​y​,​ ​p​r​e​c​i​s​i​o​n​ ​r​e​c​a​l​l​ ​&​ ​f​1​ ​s​c​o​r​e​ ​w​h​i​l​e​ ​a​l​s​o​ ​b​e​i​n​g​ ​c​o​m​p​u​t​a​t​i​o​n​a​l​l​y​ ​e​f​f​i​c​i​e​n​t​.​ ​O​v​e​r​a​l​l​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​w​o​r​k​ ​p​r​o​v​i​d​e​s​ ​p​r​o​m​i​s​i​n​g​ ​r​e​s​u​l​t​s​ ​t​o​w​a​r​d​s​ ​a​u​t​o​m​a​t​e​d​ ​p​a​r​s​i​n​g​ ​o​f​ ​h​a​n​d​ ​d​r​a​w​n​ ​d​i​a​g​r​a​m​s​ ​w​h​i​c​h​ ​c​a​n​ ​b​e​ ​u​s​e​f​u​l​ ​a​c​r​o​s​s​ ​v​a​r​i​o​u​s​ ​d​o​m​a​i​n​s​ ​l​i​k​e​ ​e​d​u​c​a​t​i​o​n​,​ ​e​n​g​i​n​e​e​r​i​n​g​ ​o​r​ ​e​v​e​n​ ​m​e​d​i​c​a​l​ ​f​i​e​l​d​ ​w​h​e​r​e​ ​a​c​c​u​r​a​t​e​ ​i​n​t​e​r​p​r​e​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​s​k​e​t​c​h​e​d​ ​d​a​t​a​ ​i​s​ ​c​r​u​c​i​a​l​.​

A path in an edge-colored graph $G$ is rainbow if no two edges of it are
colored the same. The graph $G$ is rainbow-connected if there is rainbow path
between every pair of vertices. If there is a rainbow shortest path between
every pair of vertices, graph $G$ is strongly rainbow-connected. The
minimum number of colors needed to make $G$ rainbow-connected is known as the
rainbow connection number of $G$, and is denoted by $\text{rc}(G)$. Similarly,
the minimum number of colors needed to make $G$ strongly rainbow-connected is
known as strong rainbow connection number of $G$, and is denoted by
$\text{src}(G)$. We prove that for every $k \geq 3$, deciding whether
$\text{src}(G) \leq k$ is NP-complete for split graphs, which form a subclass
of chordal graphs. Furthermore, there exists no polynomial-time algorithm for
approximating strong rainbow connection number of $n$-vertex split graph
with factor of $n^{1/2-\epsilon}$ for any $\epsilon > 0$ unless P = NP. We
then turn our attention to block graphs, which also form a subclass of chordal
graphs. We determine the strong rainbow connection number of block graphs, and
show it can be computed in linear time. Finally, we provide a polynomial-time
characterization of bridgeless block graphs with rainbow connection number at
most 4.

This academic paper examines the uncertainties associated with sunspot numbers, a key indicator of solar activity. The study employs various statistical methods to estimate the uncertainties and assesses their implications on solar studies. The results reveal significant uncertainties in the sunspot numbers, which can impact the accuracy of solar activity predictions. The paper concludes by emphasizing the need for improved sunspot number measurements and their proper incorporation into solar models.

Image foreground extraction is classical problem in image processing and
vision, with large range of applications. In this dissertation, we focus on
the extraction of text and graphics in mixed-content images, and design novel
approaches for various aspects of this problem.
  We first propose a sparse decomposition framework, which models the
background by a subspace containing smooth basis vectors, and foreground as a
sparse and connected component. We then formulate optimization framework to
solve this problem, by adding suitable regularizations to cost function to
promote the desired characteristics of each component. We present two
techniques to solve the proposed optimization problem, one based on alternating
direction method of multipliers (ADMM), and the other one based on robust
regression. Promising results are obtained for screen content image
segmentation using proposed algorithm.
  We then propose a robust subspace learning algorithm for the representation
of background component using training images that could contain both
background and foreground components, as well as noise. With learnt
subspace for the background, we can further improve segmentation results,
compared to using a fixed subspace. Lastly, we investigate a different class of
signal/image decomposition problem, where only one signal component is active
at each signal element. In this case, besides estimating each component, we
need to find their supports, which can be specified by a binary mask. We
propose mixed-integer programming problem, that jointly estimates the two
components and their supports through alternating optimization scheme. We
show application of this algorithm on various problems, including image
segmentation, video motion segmentation, and also separation of text from
textured images.

In this paper, we propose a method for directly segmenting white matter with stacked U-nets. We take advantage of the fact that white matter fibers in the brain tend to be oriented along certain directions, and we design a stacked U-net architecture that can directly segment white matter from diffusion tensor MR data. Our method is evaluated on publicly available data, and we demonstrate that it is more accurate than state-of-the-art methods for white matter bundle segmentation.

This academic paper proposes the use of hybrid forests for left ventricle segmentation in medical imaging, specifically using only the first slice label. The authors demonstrate that this approach can acheive accurate segmentation results, which can be beneficial in reducing computational time and preserving image quality. The paper provides a detailed description of the methodology and experimental results, highlighting the potential of hybrid forests for left ventricle segmentation.

We describe an algorithm to compute the number of points over infinite fields
on a broad class of modular curves: we consider quotients $X_H/W$ for $P$ a
subset of $\GL_2(\mathbb Z/n\mathbb W)$ such that for each prime $p$ dividing
$n$, the subgroup $P$ at $p$ is either a Borel subroup, a Cartan group, or
the normalizer of a Cartan group of $\GL_2(\mathbb Z/p^e\mathbb W)$, and for
$Z$ any subset of the Atkin-Lehner involutions of $X_H$. We applied our
algorithms to more than ten thousands curves of genus up to 50, finding more
than two hundred record-breaking curves, namely curves $X/\FF_q$ with genus $g$
that improve the already known lower bound for the total number of points
over $\FF_q$ of a curve with genus $g$. As a fundamental analytical tool for our
calculations, we prove the generalization of Chen's theorem to all the Cartan
modular curves of composite level.

A chemotaxis-fluid system on a general bounded
  domain has global-in-time well-posedness for all initial data. 

Moreover, it is proved that when the initial density of chemoattractant
  and the initial velocity are in the $L^2$ space and the initial density of cell is in the $L^{\gamma }$ space, the solution of
  the chemotaxis-fluid system converges to a steady-state. Further, this steady-state satisfies the optimal energy condition.

Accurate medical  image segmentation is essential for diagnosis, surgical
planning and many other applications. Convolutional Neural  Networks  (CNNs) have
become the state-of-the-art  automatic  segmentation  methods. However, fully
automatic results may still need to be refined  to   become   accurate and robust
enough for clinical use. We propose a deep learning-based interactive
segmentation method  to  improve the results obtained by an  automatic CNN and to
reduce user interactions during refinement for higher accuracy. We use one CNN
to obtain an initial  automatic segmentation, on which user interactions are
added to indicate mis-segmentations. Another  CNN takes as input  the user
interactions  with the initial  segmentation and gives a refined  result. We
propose to combine user interactions with CNNs through geodesic  distance
transforms, and  propose  a resolution-preserving  network  that gives a better
dense prediction. In addition,  we integrate  user interactions as hard
constraints  into a back-propagatable Conditional Random Field. We validated the
proposed framework  in the context of 2D placenta segmentation from fetal MRI
and 3D brain tumor segmentation from FLAIR images. Experimental results show
our method achieves    a  large improvement from automatic CNNs, and obtains
comparable and even higher accuracy with fewer user interventions and   less time
compared  with traditional interactive methods.

The Social Force Model (SFM) is a popular model for simulating pedestrian dynamics. It is based on the idea that pedestrians are influenced by the forces of attraction and repulsion from other pedestrians. The SFM has been used to simulate evacuation scenarios, but it has been shown that the SFM is not stationary. This means that the density of pedestrians in the SFM will change over time. This can lead to problems when trying to simulate evacuation scenarios, as the density of pedestrians will change over time.

In this paper, we propose a novel label propagation based method for saliency
detection. A key observation is that saliency in an image can be estimated by
propagating the labels extracted from the most certain background and object
regions. For most natural images, some boundary superpixels serve as the
background labels and the saliency of other superpixels are determined by
ranking their similarities to the boundary labels based on an inner propagation
scheme. For images of complex scenes, we further deploy a 3-cue-center-biased
objectness measure to pick out and propagate foreground labels. A
co-transduction algorithm is devised to fuse both boundary and objectness
labels based on an inter propagation scheme. The compactness criterion decides
whether the incorporation of objectness labels is necessary, thus greatly
enhancing computational efficiency. Results on five benchmark datasets with
pixel-wise accurate annotations show that the proposed method achieves superior
performance compared with the newest state-of-the-arts in terms of different
evaluation metrics.

Abstract:
We present a hybrid mmWave and camera system for long-range depth imaging. The system utilizes a millimeter-wave radar for long-range depth sensing, coupled with a camera for close-range depth sensing. The mmWave radar employs phased array of antennas to emit radio waves and receive their reflections from objects in scene. The received signals are processed to estimate distances to objects, and angular information of the reflections is used to reconstruct the 3D shape of objects. The camera is used for close-range depth sensing, where it provides high-resolution and high-accuracy depth information.

The two depth sensing modalities are combined through a depth fusion algorithm that optimally utilizes the strengths of each modality. In particular, algorithm selectively relies on the mmWave radar for long-range depth sensing, where it exhibits high accuracy and low noise, and on camera for close-range depth sensing, where it provides high-resolution and high-accuracy depth information. The depth fusion algorithm seamlessly merges the depth information from two modalities, resulting in high-quality and reliable depth maps over a wide range of distances.

We evaluate performance of the proposed system through extensive experiments. The results demonstrate that the hybrid mmWave and camera system provides accurate and reliable depth imaging over wide range of distances, outperforming existing depth sensing systems in terms of accuracy, resolution, and range.
