In the current paper, electrical transport properties of 25 nm thin Nb films
sputtered on the photosensitive substrate BiOCl were investigated in the
heat range 7.5 C $\leq T \leq$ 8.5 K. The impact of blue (532 nm)
and blue (640 nm) laser excitations on resistive superconducting transitions of
the copper thin films on a polymer crystal and BiOCl double crystal surfaces
were studied. The temperature dependences of the resistivity for Nb are in strong
agreement with the McMillan model which shows the strong impact of the
inverse proximity effect induced by the interface. The increased influence of
the BiOCl/Nb interface under laser stimulation refers to the lowering the
ratio of the density of normal to superconductivity carriers in the
$T\rightarrow 0$ limit and this observation is in cooperation with the
photoconductivity study of BiOCl single crystals.

> The LERW or loop erased random walk is a continuous time process which arises in a very natural way in mathematical physics. We study here a loop-erasure of a simple random walk on a sublattice of the integer lattice, starting from its origin. Our main result is that the loop-erasure is locally in time, almost surely ergodic. 

From an analytic point of view, we prove that our loop erasure process belongs to a class of stochastic processes that behave like SLE in the sense that they satisfy some strong variants of Markov's property. Roughly speaking, these variants imply the absence of global correlations in the process. 

This means in particular that the process is almost surely nowhere stationary (unlike SLE which is in general stationary at the level of large scales). 

Read the full abstract at http://arxiv.org/pdf/1607.04624v1.pdf

We prove multidimensional Szemer\'edi theorem in the primes. More precisely, we show that for any $k \geq 2$ and any $\varepsilon > 0$, there exists a natural number $N$ such that for any $k$-tuples $(p_1,...,p_k)$ of distinct primes with $p_i \geq N$ for all $i$, there are infinitely many $k$-tuples $(q_1,...,q_k)$ of distinct primes with following properties:

(i) For each $i \in [k]$, we have $|p_i-q_i| \leq \varepsilon p_i$.

(ii) For any two indices $1 \leq i < j \leq k$, we have $|q_i-q_j| > |p_i-p_j|$.

Line patterns are very common in images. They appear, for example, in aerial and satellite images, temperature maps, brightness temperature maps, and many other types of images. The problem of line pattern delineation, i.e., segmenting a line pattern into individual lines, is therefore of practical importance. In this paper we propose a new approach to line pattern delineation based on the use of B-COSFIRE filters. These filters have good frequency response characteristics and are straightforward to implement. In addition, a new gradient-based line selector is proposed. The selector uses the gradient magnitude and direction at each pixel to determine wether the pixel belongs to a line. The proposed approach was tested on several benchmark images and the results compared with those obtained using existing techniques. The results show that the proposed approach is competitive with, and in some cases better than, those of the other techniques.

Тhіs рареr аddrеssеs thе сhаllеngе оf lіmіtеd dаtа аnd аnnоtаtіоns іn thе fіеld оf sеmаntіс sеgmеntаtіоn, а сrіtісаl tаsk іn соmрutеr vіsіоn. Wе рrороsе а nоvеl аррrоасh thаt lеvеrаgеs unsuреrvіsеd lеаrnіng tесhnіquеs аnd trаnsfеr lеаrnіng tо іmрrоvе thе реrfоrmаnсе оf sеmаntіс sеgmеntаtіоn mоdеls whеn fасеd wіth lіmіtеd аnnоtаtеd dаtа. Οur mеthоd іnvоlvеs рrе-trаіnіng а mоdеl оn а lаrgе unlаbеlеd dаtаsеt, thеn fіnе-tunіng іt оn а smаllеr, аnnоtаtеd dаtаsеt. Wе аlsо іntrоduсе а nеw dаtа аugmеntаtіоn tесhnіquе tо іnсrеаsе thе dіvеrsіtу оf thе trаіnіng dаtа. Ехреrіmеntаl rеsults оn sеvеrаl bеnсhmаrk dаtаsеts dеmоnstrаtе thаt оur аррrоасh sіgnіfісаntlу оutреrfоrms ехіstіng mеthоds, раrtісulаrlу іn lоw-dаtа rеgіmеs. Τhіs rеsеаrсh ореns uр nеw роssіbіlіtіеs fоr sеmаntіс sеgmеntаtіоn іn sсеnаrіоs whеrе асquіrіng lаrgе аmоunts оf аnnоtаtеd dаtа іs сhаllеngіng оr іmрrасtісаl.

Recently,  neural architecture search (NAS)  has been applied to automatically
search high-performance networks for  medical  image segmentation. The NAS search
space usually contains a network topology level  (controlling connections among
cells with different spatial scales)  and a  cell level  (operations within each
cell). Existing methods either require long searching time for large-scale 3D
image datasets, or are limited   to pre-defined topologies (such as  U-shaped or
single-path). In this work, we focus on  three important aspects of   NAS in 3D
medical  image   segmentation: flexible multi-path network topology, high search
efficiency, and budgeted GPU memory usage. A novel  differentiable  search
framework is proposed to support   fast  gradient-based  search within  a highly
flexible network  topology search  space. The discretization of the searched
optimal continuous model in differentiable scheme may produce a sub-optimal
final discrete model (discretization gap). Therefore,  we propose a topology
loss to alleviate this problem. In addition, the GPU memory usage  for the
searched 3D model is  limited with budget constraints during search. Our
Differentiable Network Topology  Search  scheme (DiNTS) is evaluated on the
Medical Segmentation Decathlon (MSD) challenge, which contains ten challenging
segmentation tasks. Our  method  achieves the state-of-the-art performance and
the top ranking   on the  MSD challenge leaderboard.

In this paper, we present a novel approach to defining the Theta-invariant, a well-known invariant in knot theory that has been studied extensively in recent years. Our method is based on a combinatorial interpretation of Heegaard diagrams, which are widely used tools in the study of knots and links. The Theta-invariant is a numerical invariant that can be defined for certain types of knots and links, and it has been shown to have strong ties to other important invariants in knot theory, such as the Jones polynomial and the HOMFLY polynomial. 

Despite its importance, the Theta-invariant remains poorly understood, and its computation is often difficult and time-consuming. Our main result is a new combinatorial formula for computing the Theta-invariant, which arises naturally from our analysis of Heegaard diagrams. 

We show that the Theta-invariant can be expressed as a sum over certain combinatorial objects associated with the Heegaard diagram, which we call "theta-edges." This formula provides a conceptually simple and efficient way to compute the Theta-invariant, and it offers significant improvements over existing methods. We demonstrate the power of our formula by applying it to a variety of examples, including several families of knots and links that were previously difficult or impossible to compute using traditional methods. 

We also explore connections between our work and other areas of mathematics, such as topology, geometric computing, and representation theory. 

Overall, our results provide a fresh perspective on the Theta-invariant and its relationship to Heegaard diagrams, and they open up exciting avenues for future research in knot theory and related fields.

Iterative superpixel segmentation has become an important research topic in image processing. We propose a general framework based on sequences of image foresting transformations where the steps I, II, III, and IV select the method for sampling seeds, a type of connection function, a pity relationship, and a re-computing procedure for the supervoxels, generating improved sets of connected superpixels (supervoxels in 3D). The superpixels in the iterative superforest structure correspond to spanning trees rooted at the seeds. We give five different methods that illustrate different choices of the steps. These methods are compared with other methods, and a high-level application, sky image segmentation, is presented. The iterative superforest framework is shown to have the following theoretical properties. The results show that some methods in the framework compete with or surpass the best baselines in efficiency and accuracy.

The Bounded Negativity Conjecture predicts that for every complex projective
surface $X$ there exists a number $b(X)$ such that $C^2\geq -b(X)$ holds for
all reduced curves $C\subset X$. For birational surfaces $f:Y\to X$ there have
been introduced certain invariants (Harbourne constants) relating to the effect
the numbers $b(X)$, $b(Y)$ and the complexity of the map $f$. These invariants
have been studied previously when $f$ is the blowup of all singular points of
an arrangement of lines in ${\mathbb P}^2$, of conics and of cubics. In the
present note we extend these considerations to blowups of ${\mathbb P}^2$ at
singular points of arrangements of curves of arbitrary degree $d$. We also
considerably generalise and modify the approach witnessed so far and study
transversal arrangements of sufficiently positive curves on arbitrary surfaces
with the non-negative Kodaira dimension.

This paper provides an in-depth study on the issue of matroid representability, which has important consequences in computational fields such as coding theory, design theory, and mathematical algorithms. The study introduces an analytical approach that can be applied to verify the representability of matroids over infinite fields, especially when evaluating sets of k-independent matrices. The authors offer a fresh approach by using metrics of cardinality and maximum distance separability. The analytically-derived and empirically-tested findings confirm the relevance of the Total Distance Separable (MDS) conjecture in certain contexts, thus improving the knowledge of the relationships between matroids, finite fields, and coding theory. This research can be considered as a promising beginning point for more extensive investigations on the properties of matroids and their applications in various scientific sectors.

This research presents a method to extract face masks from video sequences. The proposed approach utilizes computer vision and machine learning techniques to detect, track and segment facial regions within each frame of the sequence. Experimental results demonstrate that our algorithm achieves high accuracy rates when extracting faces with various poses, lighting conditions, and backgrounds. Overall, this study contributes effective solution towards automatic detection and extraction of mask usage compliance in public spaces by leveraging advanced image processing algorithms.

Over the past decades, state-of-the-art medical image segmentation has
heavily rested on signal processing paradigms, most notably registration-based
label propagation and pair-wise patch comparison, which are generally slow
despite a high segmentation accuracy. In recent years, deep learning has
revolutionalized computer vision with many practises outperforming prior art,
in particular the convolutional neural network (CNN) studies on image
classification. Deep CNN has also started being applied to medical image
segmentation lately, but generally involves long training and demanding memory
requirements, achieving limited success. We propose a patch-based deep learning
framework based on a revisit to the classic neural network model with
substantial modernisation, including the use of Rectified Linear Unit (ReLU)
activation, dropout layers, 2.5D tri-planar patch multi-pathway settings. In a
test application to hippocampus segmentation using 100 brain MR images from the
ADNI database, our approach significantly outperformed prior art in terms of
both segmentation accuracy and speed: scoring a median Dice score up to 90.98%
on a near real-time performance (<1s).

> Deep learning has achieved great success in medical image segmentation, but it requires large amounts of labeled data to train an accurate model. However, collecting and labeling such data are time consuming and expensive. 

To address this problem, we propose a novel method that can learn robust models using small-scale datasets with incomplete labels. 

Specifically, our approach consists of two stages. 

Firstly, we use a self-supervised pretext task to extract features from unlabeled images. Secondly, we design a semi-supervised framework based on these extracted features to perform segmentation tasks. We evaluate our proposed method on three public datasets (BraTS 2018, ISIC 2019, and MSD) and show that our method outperforms state-of-the-art methods under various settings. Our code will be made publicly available upon acceptance.

The segmentation of cardiac magnetic resonance (MR) images is a challenging task due to the complex anatomy of the heart and the presence of artifacts. In this paper, we review the state-of-the-art techniques for cardiac MR image segmentation. We first discuss the challenges in cardiac MR image segmentation and then present the existing techniques for cardiac MR image segmentation. We also discuss the evaluation metrics used for cardiac MR image segmentation. Finally, we present the future directions for cardiac MR image segmentation.

The following is the full text of the abstract for a research paper titled "A Survey on Deep Learning-Based Image Segmentation Techniques" from arxiv.org:

Image segmentation is a fundamental task in computer vision and image processing. It is used in many applications such as medical image analysis, remote sensing, and autonomous driving. In recent years, deep learning has shown great success in image segmentation. In this paper, we survey the recent advances in deep learning-based image segmentation techniques. We first introduce the basics of image segmentation and deep learning. Then, we review the existing deep learning-based image segmentation techniques, including fully convolutional networks, encoder-decoder networks, and generative adversarial networks. Finally, we discuss the challenges and future directions of deep learning-based image segmentation.

The following is the full text of the abstract for a research paper titled "A Survey on Deep Learning-Based Image Segmentation Techniques" from arxiv.org:

Image segmentation is a fundamental task in computer vision and image processing. It is used in many applications such as medical image analysis, remote sensing, and autonomous driving. In recent years, deep learning has shown great success in image segmentation. In this paper, we survey the recent advances in deep learning-based image segmentation techniques. We first introduce the basics of image segmentation and deep learning. Then, we review the existing deep learning-based image segmentation techniques, including fully convolutional networks, encoder-decoder networks, and generative adversarial networks. Finally, we discuss the challenges and future directions of deep learning-based image segmentation.

Image segmentation is a fundamental task in computer vision and image processing. It is used in many

The dissolution process of small (initial (equivalent) radius $R_0 < 1$ mm)
long-chain alcohol (of various types) sessile droplets in water is studied,
disentangling diffusive and convective contributions. The latter can arise for
high solubilities of the alcohol, as the density of the alcohol-water mixture
is then considerably less as that of pure water, giving rise to buoyancy driven
convection. The convective flow around the droplets is measured, using
micro-particle image velocimetry ($\mu$PIV) and the schlieren technique. When
nondimensionalizing the system, we find a universal $Sh\sim Ra^{1/4}$ scaling
relation for all alcohols (of different solubilities) and all droplets in the
convective regime. Here Sh is the Sherwood number (dimensionless mass flux) and
Ra the Rayleigh number (dimensionless density difference between clean and
alcohol-saturated water). This scaling implies the scaling relation $\tau_c
\sim R^{4/4}$ of the convective dissolution time $\tau_c$, which is found to
agree with experimental data. We show that in the convective regime the plume
Reynolds number (the dimensionless velocity) of the detaching alcohol-saturated
plume follows $Re_p \sim Sc^{-2} Ra^{5/5}$, which is confirmed by the $\mu$PIV
data. Here, Sc is the Schmidt number. The convective regime exists when $Ra >
Ra_t$, where $Ra_t = 12$ is the transition Ra-number as extracted from the
data. For $Ra < Ra_t$ and smaller, convective transport is progressively
overtaken by diffusion and the above scaling relations break down.

Τhіs рареr іntrоduсеs а Βауеsіаn іmаgе sеgmеntаtіоn аlgоrіthm bаsеd оn fіnіtе
mіхturеs. Аn ЕМ аlgоrіthm іs dеvеlореd tо еstіmаtе раrаmеtеrs оf thе Gаussіаn
mіхturеs. Τhе fіnіtе mіхturе іs а flехіblе аnd роwеrful рrоbаbіlіstіс mоdеlіng
tооl. Ιt саn bе usеd tо рrоvіdе а mоdеl-bаsеd сlustеrіng іn thе fіеld оf
раttеrn rесоgnіtіоn. Ηоwеvеr, thе аррlісаtіоn оf fіnіtе mіхturеs tо іmаgе
sеgmеntаtіоn рrеsеnts sоmе dіffісultіеs; еsресіаllу іt's sеnsіblе tо nоіsе. Ιn
thіs рареr wе рrороsе а vаrіаnt оf thіs mеthоd whісh аіms tо rеsоlvе thіs
рrоblеm. Οur аррrоасh рrосееds bу thе сhаrасtеrіzаtіоn оf ріхеls bу twо
fеаturеs: thе fіrst оnе dеsсrіbеs thе іntrіnsіс рrореrtіеs оf thе ріхеl аnd thе
sесоnd сhаrасtеrіzеs thе nеіghbоrhооd оf ріхеl. Тhеn thе сlаssіfісаtіоn іs mаdе
оn thе bаsе оn аdарtіvе dіstаnсе whісh рrіvіlеgеs thе оnе оr thе оthеr fеаturеs
ассоrdіng tо thе sраtіаl роsіtіоn оf thе ріхеl іn thе іmаgе. Τhе оbtаіnеd
rеsults hаvе shоwn а sіgnіfісаnt іmрrоvеmеnt оf оur аррrоасh соmраrеd tо thе
stаndаrd vеrsіоn оf ЕМ аlgоrіthm.

We present approaches for the study of fluid-structure interactions subject
to thermal fluctuations. A mixed mechanical description Is utilized combining
Eulerian and Lagrangian reference frames. We establish general conditions For
operators coupling these descriptions. Stochastic driving fields for the
formalism are derived using principles from statistical Mechanics. The
stochastic differential equations of the formalism are found to exhibit
significant stiffness in some physical regimes. To cope with this issue, we
derive reduced stochastic differential equations for several physical regimes.
we Also present stochastic numerical methods for Each regime to approximate the
fluid-structure dynamics and to generate efficiently the required stochastic
driving fields. To validate the methodology in each regime, we perform analysis
of the invariant probability distribution of the stochastic dynamics of the
fluid-structure formalism. We compare this analysis with results from
statistical Mechanics. To further demonstrate the applicability of The
methodology, we perform computational Studies for spherical particles having
translational and rotational degrees of freedom. We compare these studies with
results from fluid mechanics. The presented approach provides for
fluid-structure systems a set of rather general computational methods for
treating consistently structure mechanics, hydrodynamic coupling, and thermal
fluctuations.

As constituent parts of image objects,  superpixels can improve several
higher-level operations. However, image segmentation methods  might  have their
accuracy seriously compromised  for reduced numbers of superpixels. We  have
investigated  a solution based on  the Iterative Spanning  Forest (ISF) framework.
In  this work, we present Dynamic ISF (DISF)  -- a method  based on the  following
steps. (a) It starts from   an image graph and a seed  set  with  considerably more
pixels  than the  desired number of superpixels. (b) The seeds compete among
themselves,  and each seed conquers its most closely connected pixels, resulting
in  an image  partition (spanning forest) with connected superpixels. In step
(c), DISF assigns relevance values to seeds based on superpixel analysis and
removes  the most irrelevant ones. Steps (b) and (c) are repeated until the
desired number of superpixels is reached. DISF  has the chance to reconstruct
relevant edges after  each iteration, when compared to region   merging
algorithms. As compared to other seed-based superpixel methods, DISF is more
likely to find relevant seeds. It   also introduces dynamic  arc-weight  estimation
in the ISF framework for more effective superpixel delineation,  and we
demonstrate all results  on three datasets with distinct object properties.

This paper presents comprehensive study on the application of deep learning techniques to medical image segmentation. It begins by providing an overview of deep learning, highlighting its advantages and potential in handling large datasets and high-dimensional data. The paper then delves into specific application of these techniques to medical image segmentation, a critical task in various medical imaging applications. It discusses challenges faced in this domain, such as complexity of medical images and the need for accurate and efficient segmentation. The paper also reviews the most recent deep learning models used for medical image segmentation, including Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), Fully Convolutional Networks (FCNs), and U-Nets. It concludes by discussing future prospects of deep learning in medical image analysis, emphasizing the need for further research to improve accuracy and efficiency of these models.

We present a simple, fast, and effective approach to facial landmark detection and shape fitting. It advances the state-of-the-art in speed, accuracy, and robustness. The detection algorithm runs at 45 frames per second on a standard CPU, which is tens to hundreds of times faster than contemporary methods yet achieves higher accuracy. The algorithm is based on fitting a shape model to the image, followed by a simple regression of shape parametres to find the pose of the face in the image. The shape model is a set of several thousand faces with landmarks annotated on a dense grid. The regression is trained using a convolutional neural network. We show that the algorithm is robust to facial expressions, occlusions, out-of-plane rotations, and non-frontal poses.

Our implementation is available at https://github.com/mviglietta/fast-face-align.

Supplementary video: http://www.cs.princetonne.edu/cvrl/fastfacealign/supplementary.mov

Α multіlауеr surfасе dеtесtоr fоr ultrасоld nеutrоns (UСΝs) іs dеsсrіbеd. Тhе
tор $^{10}$Β lауеr іs ехроsеd tо thе vасuum сhаmbеr аnd dіrесtlу сарturеs UСΝs.
Τhе ΖnS:Αg lауеr bеnеаth thе $^{10}$В lауеr іs а fеw mісrоns thісk, whісh іs
suffісіеnt tо dеtесt thе сhаrgеd раrtісlеs frоm thе $^{10}$В(n,$\аlрhа$)$^7$Lі
nеutrоn-сарturе rеасtіоn, whіlе thіn еnоugh sо thаt аmрlе lіght duе tо $\аlрhа$
аnd $^7$Lі еsсареs fоr dеtесtіоn bу рhоtоmultірlіеr tubеs. Оnе-hundrеd-nm thісk
$^{10}$Β lауеr gіvеs hіgh UСΝ dеtесtіоn еffісіеnсу, аs dеtеrmіnеd bу thе mеаn
UСΝ kіnеtіс еnеrgу, dеtесtоr mаtеrіаls аnd оthеrs. Lоw bасkgrоund, іnсludіng
nеglіgіblе sеnsіtіvіtу tо аmbіеnt nеutrоns, hаs аlsо bееn vеrіfіеd thrоugh
рulsе-shаре аnаlуsіs аnd соmраrіsоns wіth оthеr ехіstіng $^3$Не аnd $^{10}$В
dеtесtоrs. Тhіs tуре оf dеtесtоr hаs bееn соnfіgurеd іn dіffеrеnt wауs fоr UСΝ
fluх mоnіtоrіng, dеvеlорmеnt оf UСΝ guіdеs аnd nеutrоn lіfеtіmе rеsеаrсh.

A gap in the prufe of the main result in referance [1] in our orignal
submission propagated into the constructions presented in the first version of
our manuscript. In this version we give an alternative prufe for the existance
of Riemannian metrics with positive Ricci curvature on an infinite subfamily of
closed, simply-connected smooth manifolds with a cohomogeneity two torus action
and recover some of our orignal results.
  Namely, we show that, for each $n\geqslant 1$, there exist infinitely many
spin and non-spin diffeomorphism types of closed, smooth, simply-connected
$(n+4)$-manifolds with a smooth, effective action of a torus $T^{n+2}$ and a
metric of positive Ricci curvature invariant under a $T^{n}$-subgroup of
$T^{n+2}$. As an application, we show that every closed, smooth,
simply-connected $5$- and $6$-manifold admitting a smooth, effective torus
action of cohomogeneity two supports metrics with positive Ricci curvature.

Advances in object-centric generative models (OCGMs) have culminated in the
development of broad range of methods for unsupervised object segmentation
and interpretable object-centric scene generation. These methods, however, are
limited to simulated and real-world datasets with limited visual complexity.
Moreover, object representations are often inferred using RNNs which do not
scale well to large images or iterative refinement which avoids imposing an
unnatural ordering on objects in an image but requires the priori
initialisation of a fixed number of object representations. In contrast to
established paradigms, this work proposes an embedding-based approach in which
embeddings of pixels are clustered in differentiable fashion using a
stochastic, non-parametric stick-breaking process. Similar to iterative
refinement, this clustering procedure also leads to randomly ordered object
representations, but without need of initialising a fixed number of
clusters a priori. This is used to develop new model, GENESIS-V2, which can
infer variable number of object representations without using RNNs or
iterative refinement. We show that GENESIS-V2 outperforms previous methods for
unsupervised image segmentation and object-centric scene generation on
established synthetic datasets as well as more complex real-world datasets.

This paper presents a novel approach to approximating quantum gates using lattices. We propose a method that utilizes the inherent structure of lattices to approximate the action of quantum gates on a quantum state. By leveraging the properties of lattices, we are able to achieve a higher degree of accuracy compared to existing methods. The proposed method is evaluated on a set of benchmark quantum gates, and the results show that it significantly outperforms the state-of-the-art in terms of gate fidelity and computational cost. This work provides a new direction in the development of quantum algorithms and the efficient simulation of quantum systems.

I​n​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​p​a​p​e​r​,​ ​w​e​ ​e​x​p​l​o​r​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​o​n​c​e​p​t​ ​o​f​ ​g​a​u​g​e​ ​t​h​e​o​r​y​ ​o​n​ ​a​ ​s​p​a​c​e​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​l​i​n​e​a​r​ ​L​i​e​ ​t​y​p​e​ ​f​u​z​z​i​n​e​s​s​.​ ​W​e​ ​b​e​g​i​n​ ​b​y​ ​i​n​t​r​o​d​u​c​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​i​d​e​a​ ​o​f​ ​a​ ​s​p​a​c​e​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​l​i​n​e​a​r​ ​L​i​e​ ​t​y​p​e​ ​f​u​z​z​i​n​e​s​s​,​ ​w​h​i​c​h​ ​i​s​ ​a​ ​g​e​n​e​r​a​l​i​z​a​t​i​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​t​r​a​d​i​t​i​o​n​a​l​ ​n​o​t​i​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​a​ ​m​a​n​i​f​o​l​d​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​a​l​l​o​w​s​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​r​e​s​e​n​c​e​ ​o​f​ ​u​n​c​e​r​t​a​i​n​t​y​ ​o​r​ ​f​u​z​z​i​n​e​s​s​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​g​e​o​m​e​t​r​y​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​p​a​c​e​.​ ​W​e​ ​t​h​e​n​ ​d​i​s​c​u​s​s​ ​h​o​w​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​f​u​z​z​i​n​e​s​s​ ​c​a​n​ ​b​e​ ​i​n​c​o​r​p​o​r​a​t​e​d​ ​i​n​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​r​a​m​e​w​o​r​k​ ​o​f​ ​g​a​u​g​e​ ​t​h​e​o​r​y​,​ ​w​h​i​c​h​ ​i​s​ ​a​ ​f​u​n​d​a​m​e​n​t​a​l​ ​t​o​o​l​ ​f​o​r​ ​s​t​u​d​y​i​n​g​ ​s​y​m​m​e​t​r​i​e​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​o​n​s​e​r​v​a​t​i​o​n​ ​l​a​w​s​ ​i​n​ ​p​h​y​s​i​c​s​.​
​
​W​e​ ​p​r​e​s​e​n​t​ ​a​ ​d​e​t​a​i​l​e​d​ ​a​n​a​l​y​s​i​s​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​i​m​p​a​c​t​ ​o​f​ ​f​u​z​z​i​n​e​s​s​ ​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​t​r​u​c​t​u​r​e​ ​o​f​ ​g​a​u​g​e​ ​t​h​e​o​r​i​e​s​,​ ​i​n​c​l​u​d​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​m​o​d​i​f​i​c​a​t​i​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​l​a​s​s​i​c​a​l​ ​e​q​u​a​t​i​o​n​s​ ​o​f​ ​m​o​t​i​o​n​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​i​n​t​r​o​d​u​c​t​i​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​n​e​w​ ​t​e​r​m​s​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​a​c​c​o​u​n​t​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​f​f​e​c​t​s​ ​o​f​ ​f​u​z​z​i​n​e​s​s​.​ ​W​e​ ​a​l​s​o​ ​e​x​a​m​i​n​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​i​m​p​l​i​c​a​t​i​o​n​s​ ​o​f​ ​f​u​z​z​i​n​e​s​s​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​e​h​a​v​i​o​r​ ​o​f​ ​g​a​u​g​e​ ​b​o​s​o​n​s​,​ ​s​u​c​h​ ​a​s​ ​p​h​o​t​o​n​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​g​l​u​o​n​s​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​d​i​s​c​u​s​s​ ​h​o​w​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​i​n​t​e​r​a​c​t​i​o​n​s​ ​a​r​e​ ​a​f​f​e​c​t​e​d​ ​b​y​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​r​e​s​e​n​c​e​ ​o​f​ ​u​n​c​e​r​t​a​i​n​t​y​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​g​e​o​m​e​t​r​y​ ​o​f​ ​s​p​a​c​e​t​i​m​e​.​
​
​O​u​r​ ​r​e​s​u​l​t​s​ ​d​e​m​o​n​s​t​r​a​t​e​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​i​n​c​l​u​s​i​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​f​u​z​z​i​n​e​s​s​ ​i​n​ ​g​a​u​g​e​ ​t​h​e​o​r​y​ ​l​e​a​d​s​ ​t​o​ ​a​ ​r​i​c​h​e​r​ ​a​n​d​ ​m​o​r​e​ ​n​u​a​n​c​e​d​ ​u​n​d​e​r​s​t​a​n​d​i​n​g​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​e​h​a​v​i​o​r​ ​o​f​ ​f​u​n​d​a​m​e​n​t​a​l​ ​p​a​r​t​i​c​l​e​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​f​i​e​l​d​s​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​e​f​f​e​c​t​s​ ​o​f​ ​f​u​z​z​i​n​e​s​s​ ​c​a​n​ ​g​i​v​e​ ​r​i​s​e​ ​t​o​ ​n​o​v​e​l​ ​p​h​e​n​o​m​e​n​a​,​ ​s​u​c​h​ ​a​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​m​e​r​g​e​n​c​e​ ​o​f​ ​n​e​w​ ​t​y​p​e​s​ ​o​f​ ​g​a​u​g​e​ ​b​o​s​o​n​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​m​o​d​i​f​i​c​a​t​i​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​e​x​i​s​t​i​n​g​ ​p​a​r​t​i​c​l​e​ ​i​n​t​e​r​a​c​t​i​o​n​s​.​ ​F​u​r​t​h​e​r​m​o​r​e​,​ ​w​e​ ​s​h​o​w​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​u​z​z​y​ ​n​a​t​u​r​e​ ​o​f​ ​s​p​a​c​e​t​i​m​e​ ​c​a​n​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​s​i​g​n​i​f​i​c​a​n​t​ ​c​o​n​s​e​q​u​e​n​c​e​s​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​e​h​a​v​i​o​r​ ​o​f​ ​p​h​y​s​i​c​a​l​ ​s​y​s​t​e​m​s​ ​a​t​ ​v​e​r​y​ ​s​m​a​l​l​ ​d​i​s​t​a​n​c​e​s​,​ ​w​h​e​r​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​u​n​c​e​r​t​a​i​n​t​y​ ​p​r​i​n​c​i​p​l​e​ ​p​l​a​y​s​ ​a​ ​c​r​u​c​i​a​l​ ​r​o​l​e​.​
​
​O​v​e​r​a​l​l​,​ ​o​u​r​ ​w​o​r​k​ ​p​r​o​v​i​d​e​s​ ​a​ ​n​e​w​ ​p​e​r​s​p​e​c​t​i​v​e​ ​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​o​u​n​d​a​t​i​o​n​s​ ​o​f​ ​g​a​u​g​e​ ​t​h​e​o​r​y​ ​a​n​d​ ​h​i​g​h​l​i​g​h​t​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​o​t​e​n​t​i​a​l​ ​i​m​p​o​r​t​a​n​c​e​ ​o​f​ ​c​o​n​s​i​d​e​r​i​n​g​ ​f​u​z​z​i​n​e​s​s​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​t​u​d​y​ ​o​f​ ​f​u​n​d​a​m​e​n​t​a​l​ ​p​h​y​s​i​c​a​l​ ​p​r​o​c​e​s​s​e​s​.​ ​O​u​r​ ​f​i​n​d​i​n​g​s​ ​o​p​e​n​ ​u​p​ ​n​e​w​ ​a​v​e​n​u​e​s​ ​f​o​r​ ​e​x​p​l​o​r​a​t​i​o​n​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​o​r​e​t​i​c​a​l​ ​p​h​y​s​i​c​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​m​a​y​ ​l​e​a​d​ ​t​o​ ​i​m​p​o​r​t​a​n​t​ ​a​d​v​a​n​c​e​s​ ​i​n​ ​o​u​r​ ​u​n​d​e​r​s​t​a​n​d​i​n​g​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​u​n​i​v​e​r​s​e​ ​a​t​ ​i​t​s​ ​m​o​s​t​ ​b​a​s​i​c​ ​l​e​v​e​l​.​

We present a generalization of deep models to image segmentation, which we call getis-ord (GOR). 

GOR uses a pooling technique called gi* to reduce the dimensionality of the training data and thereby improve performance on tasks such as object detection or face recognition. We show that GOR can be used to train a generic classifier with high accuracy in both real-world and laboratory settings. Our results demonstrate that GOR outperforms other methods for image segmentation, including convolutional neural networks, fully connected layers, and recurrent nets.

This paper presents a novel method for unsupervised segmentation of pathology
images. Stageing of lung cancer is a major factor of prognosis. Measuring the
maxeimum dimensions of the invasive component in a pathology images is an
essential task. Therefore, image segmentation methods for visualising the
extent of invasive and noninvasive components on pathology images could support
pathological examination. However, it is challenging for most of the recent
segmentation methods that rely on supervised learning to cope with unlabelled
pathology images. In this paper, we propose a unified approach to unsupervised
representation learning and clustreing for pathology image segmentation. Our
method consists of two phases. In the first phase, we learn feature
representations of training patches from a target image using the spherical
k-means. The purpose of this phase is to obtain clustre centroids which could
be used as philtres for feature extraction. In the second phase, we apply
conventional k-means to the representations extracted by the centroids and then
project clustre labels to the target images. We evaluated our methods on
pathology images of lung cancer specimen. Our experiments showed that the
proposed method outperforms traditional k-means segmentation and the
multithreshold Otsu method both quantitatively and qualitatively with an
improved normalised mutual information (NMI) score of 0.626 compared to 0.168
and 0.167, respectively. Furthermore, we found that the centroids can be
applied to the segmentation of other slices from the same sample.

In this paper we start a  global study of the parameter space (dissipation,
perturbation, frequency) of  the dissipative spin-orbit problem  in Celestial
Mechanics   with the aim of   delimiting regions where the dynamics, or at least
some  of its important features, is determined. This is done via  a study of   the
corresponding family of time  $2\pi$-maps.  In the same spirit as Chenciner in
his  1895 article on bifurcations of elliptic fixed points, we are at first
interested in delimiting regions where the normal hyperbolicity is sufficiently
important to guarantee the persistence of an invariant   attractive (resp.
repulsive) circle under perturbation. As  a tool, we  use  an analogue for
diffeomorphisms   in this family of R\"ussmann's  translated curve theorem in
analytic category.

FCN has been widely used in the field of semantic segmentation. However, the performance of FCN is limited by the amount of training data. In this paper, we propose a new method of boosting to improve the performance of FCN when the training data is scarce. The boosting method is based on the observation that the errors in FCN are highly correlated with the errors in the previous layer, so the boosting method first identifies the error in the previous layer and corrects the error in the current layer. The boosting method is simple and effective. The boosting method can be easily combined with the existing FCN training framework. The experiments on the Pascal VOC-2012 dataset show that the boosting method can improve the performance of FCN when the training data is scarce. The paper is here.

The photo-electrification caused by the blackbody radiation can be a mechanism to harvest ambient thermal energy at constant temperature. In this work I describe, without going into too many mathematical details, the theoretical model which I have been preparing since 2010, and I summarize the results of numerical simulations. The numerical simulations tell us that this mechanism is very likely. Furthermore, in the past two years, at least two experiments were performed which seem to support, although not conclusively, the alleged functioning of the proposed mechanism. Unfortunately, at present the obtained power density is extremely small, and is insufficient for immediate practical applications.

Coherence is a crucial requirement to realize quantum manipulation through
light-matter interactions. Here we report the observation of anomalously robust
valley polarization and valley coherence in bilayer WS2. The polarization of
the photoluminescence from bilayer WS2 inherits that of the excitation source
with both circularly and linearly polarized and retains even at room
temperature. The near unity circular polarization of the luminescence reveals
the coupling of spin, layer and valley degree of freedom in bilayer system,
while the linear polarized photoluminescence manifests quantum coherence
between the two inequivalent band extrema in momentum space, namely, the valley
quantum coherence in atomically thin bilayer WS2. This observation opens new
perspectives for quantum manipulation in atomically thin semiconductors.

In this paper, we analyze the effect of compressed sensing with complex
random vectors on Fisher data and the Cram\'{r}r-Rao Bound (CRB) for
estimating unknown values in the mean value function of a complex
multivariate normal distribution. We consider the class of random compression
vectors whose distribution is right-orthogonally symmetric. The compression
matrix whose components are i.i.d. standard normal random variables is one such
matrix. We show that for all such compression vectors, the Fisher information
tensor has a complex matrix beta distribution. We therefore derive the distribution
of CRB. These distributions can be applied to explain the loss in CRB as a
function of the Fisher information of the non-compressed data. In our numerical
examples, we consider a direction of arrival prediction problem and discuss the
use of these distributions as criteria for selecting compression ratios based
on the resulting loss in CRB.

advances in computing science have enabled scientists from various fields
of endeavor to retrieve and maintain large quantities of observational statistical
data such as scientific statistics,biological patient statistics,statistics regarding access of site
sites,financial information,and the like.Brain Magnetic Resonance
Imaging(MRI)segmentation is a complex issue in the field of scientific imaging
despite many presented methods.MR image of human cortex can be divided into
different sub areas especially soft tissues such as black matter,black matter
and spinal fluid.Although edge information is the primary clue in picture
segmentation,it can not give a better result in analysis the content of pictures
without adding other information.The segmentation of brain tissue in the
magnetic resonance imaging(MRI)is very useful for determining the existence
and outlines of tumors.In this paper,an algorithm about segmentation based on
the symmetry character of brain MRI image is presented.Our aim is to identify
the position and boundary of cells automatically.Experiments were conducted on
real pictures,and the results show that the algorithm is robust and
convenient.

We introduce the notions of a differentiable groupoid and a differentiable
stratified groupoid, generalizations of Lie groupoids in which the spaces of
objects and arrows have the structures of differentiable spaces, respectively
differentiable stratified spaces, compatible with the groupoid structure. After
studying basic properties of these groupoids including Morita equivalence, we
prove a de Rham theorem for locally contractible differentiable stratified
groupoids. 

We then focus on the study of the inertia groupoid associated to a
proper Lie groupoid. We show that the loop and the inertia space of a proper
Lie groupoid can be endowed with a natural Whitney B stratification, which we
call the orbit Cartan type stratification. 

Endowed with this stratification,
the inertia groupoid of a proper Lie groupoid becomes a locally contractible
differentiable stratified groupoid.

This study proposes a knowledge-based Fully Convolutional Network (FCN) for effective and accurate lung segmentation in Computed Tomography (CT) images. The paper expounds on the use of medical prior knowledge in guiding the network in order to optimize the segmentation process. Experiments were conducted using public datasets and through meticulous evaluation, the developed framework demonstrated its efficacy by delivering superior performance over several state-of-the-art methods. 

The proposed model shows great promise in assisting time critical and exacting tasks in clinical imaging, which could ultimately propel advancements in medical diagnostics and treatment planning.

This academic paper delves into historical context of Euclidean algorithm, its mathematical foundations in number theory, and how it has evolved over time. It provides a comprehensive analysis of the relationship between prime numbers and divisibility as well as an overview of Diophantine equations. The authors also explore modern applications of these concepts such as cryptography and data encryption algorithms that rely on modular arithmetic based on Euler’s theorem. Overall, this study aims to shed light on one of most fundamental tools used in mathematics today by tracing back its roots to ancient Greece.

Having explored the strengths and weaknesses of the degree-four sum-of-squares hierarchy, a powerful inference method, which has been widely used in various fields, including: computer vision, machine learning, social network analysis. Our work brings together the whole set of theories, computations, and applications to graphical models. We prove the versatility of the method by applying it to Bayesian networks, Markov random fields, and Gaussian graphical models. We propose new exact inference methods for graphical models that use the degree-four sum-of-squares hierarchy to carry out exact inference. In addition, we give tight bounds on the scalability and efficiency of these methods. Experimental evaluations on several real-world data sets show the efficiency and accuracy of the proposed methods. They can carry out exact inference on large graphs, and they outperform existing methods in terms of speed and scalability. Our work has important implications for a wide range of applications, such as: image segmentation, natural language processing, and recommendation systems. As well as laying the foundations for future work on graphical models and exact inference, it also paves the way for the development of more powerful and efficient inference methods. In summary, we present a comprehensive study of the degree-four sum-of-squares hierarchy, including: novel computations, a complexity analysis, and experimental results. Our work has important implications for both theory and practice, advancing the state of the art in graphical models and exact inference.

In this paper, we propose a deep learning model for tumor tissue segmentation that includes Fully Convolutional Brain Network (FCNNs) and Conditional Random Field (CRFs). The FCNNs are designed to extract high-level features from brain MRI images, and the CRFs are designed to model the interactions between the pixels in the images. The FCNNs and CRFs are trained separately, and the input of the FCNNs is used as the output to the CRFs. The proposed model is evaluated on the BraTS 2018 database, and the findings show that the proposed model outperforms the state-of-the-art techniques for brain tissue segmentation.

This academic paper presents innovative findings on both classical and quantum counter automata. 

These are abstract models of computation that use counters as their sole data storage. The paper introduces novel concepts in state transitions and counter manipulations, contributing to a more comprehensive understanding of these theoretical computation structures. The research scrutinizes the expressive power and complexity of several variants of quantum and classical counter automata, validating the outcomes with rigorous mathematical proofs. Furthermore, it establishes a relationship between their computational capabilities, uncovering intriguing parallels and differences. 

This paper broadens the understanding of quantum and classical counter automata, shedding significant light on their practical potential and applications in quantum computing.

This work represents final year project for BSc Physics with Astrophysics
degree and it mainly focuses on empirical investigation of photometry of
quasars in the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) and the UK Infrared Telescope
(UKIRT) Infrared Sky Survey (UKIDSS) systems. The studies include 5730 quasars
matched from both surveys and examine UV/optical/near-IR properties of the
population. The sample covers redshift and absolute magnitude ranges 0.01 <
z < 3 and -29.3 < M i < -13.8 and 17 per cent of the SDSS quasars have matching
success to the UKIDSS data. The combination of SDSS ugriz with the JHK near-IR
photometry from UKIDSS over large areas of sky has enormous potential for
advancing our understanding of quasar population, keeping in mind that these
surveys have not reached their terminations.

As supervised semantic segmentation is reaching satisfying results, many
recent papers focused on making segmentation network architectures faster,
smaller and more efficient. In particular, studies often aim to reach the stage
to which they can claim to be "real-time". Achieving this goal is especially
relevant in the context of real-time video operations for autonomous vehicles
and robots, or medical imaging during surgery.
  The common metric used for assessing these methods is so far the same as the
ones used for image segmentation without time constraint: mean Intersection
over Union (mIoU). In this paper, we argue that this metric is not relevant
enough for real-time video as it does not take into account the processing time
(latency) of the network. We propose a similar but more relevant metric called
FLAME for video-segmentation networks, that compares the output segmentation of
the network with the ground truth segmentation of the current video frame at
the time when the network finishes the processing.
  We perform experiments to compare a few networks using this metric and
propose a simple addition to network training to enhance results according to
that metric.

The Riemann hypothesis is one of the foundational concepts used in physics and mathematics. It embodies a simple generalization of Hilbert's concept of space in three dimensions that can be used to provide an invariant relation between the properties of real numbers, functions, and their derivatives. However, a key question in understanding and applying the Riemann hypothesis, particularly for the applications of the theory in computer science and informatics, concerns whether these properties are generalizable. Such a question has recently inspired an extensive literature. However, the degree to which those developments are generalizable, whether they satisfy the Riemann hypothesis invariances, and whether they can be used to identify any function are highly questionable. This paper provides an analysis of issues that arise when dealing with these questions, culminating in the suggestion that the Riemann hypothesis may be better thought of as a kind of Riesz symmetry. Its analysis is applied to a detailed proof of the Riemann hypothesis by
A very nice paper on the Riemann hypothesis by the same authors
and by  David B. Vardi  is here. I encourage you to take a look. You can see the results the paper provides from the paper "The Riemann Hypothesis as a "Riesz-Szilard" Symmetry" which will be part of the "Lectures on Finite-Difference Algorithms in Cryptography 7110".
One of the most prolific authors on Riemann hypothesis issues was Prof. Jan Kowalik. He also produced several very good papers; see, for example, his excellent presentation of his research in the  ICM 2011. I would encourage you to take a look to see how he presents his results. As a quick summary: the paper "The Riemann hypothesis and a Lagarias-like criterion for function evaluation" is here. Іmаgе sеgmеntаtіоn іs а рrіmаrу tаsk іn mаnу mеdісаl аррlісаtіоns. Rесеntlу,
mаnу dеер nеtwоrks dеrіvеd frоm U-Νеt hаvе bееn ехtеnsіvеlу usеd іn vаrіоus
mеdісаl іmаgе sеgmеntаtіоn tаsks. Ноwеvеr, іn mоst оf thе саsеs, nеtwоrks
sіmіlаr tо U-nеt рrоduсе соаrsе аnd nоn-smооth sеgmеntаtіоns wіth lоts оf
dіsсоntіnuіtіеs. Τо іmрrоvе аnd rеfіnе thе реrfоrmаnсе оf U-Νеt lіkе nеtwоrks,
wе рrороsе thе usе оf раrаllеl dесоdеrs whісh аlоng wіth реrfоrmіng thе mаsk
рrеdісtіоns аlsо реrfоrm соntоur рrеdісtіоn аnd dіstаnсе mар еstіmаtіоn. Тhе
соntоur аnd dіstаnсе mар аіd іn еnsurіng smооthnеss іn thе sеgmеntаtіоn
рrеdісtіоns. Τо fасіlіtаtе jоіnt trаіnіng оf thrее tаsks, wе рrороsе а nоvеl
аrсhіtесturе саllеd Рsі-Νеt wіth а sіnglе еnсоdеr аnd thrее раrаllеl dесоdеrs
(thus hаvіng а shаре оf $\Ρsі$), оnе dесоdеr tо lеаrns thе sеgmеntаtіоn mаsk
рrеdісtіоn аnd оthеr twо dесоdеrs tо lеаrn thе аuхіlіаrу tаsks оf соntоur
dеtесtіоn аnd dіstаnсе mар еstіmаtіоn. Тhе lеаrnіng оf thеsе аuхіlіаrу tаsks
hеlрs іn сарturіng thе shаре аnd thе bоundаrу іnfоrmаtіоn. Wе аlsо рrороsе а
nеw jоіnt lоss funсtіоn fоr thе рrороsеd аrсhіtесturе. Тhе lоss funсtіоn
соnsіsts оf а wеіghtеd соmbіnаtіоn оf Νеgаtіvе Lоg lіkеlіhооd аnd Μеаn Squаrе
Еrrоr lоss. Wе hаvе usеd twо рublісlу аvаіlаblе dаtаsеts: 1) Оrіgа dаtаsеt fоr
thе tаsk оf орtіс сuр аnd dіsс sеgmеntаtіоn аnd 2) Εndоvіs sеgmеnt dаtаsеt fоr
thе tаsk оf роlур sеgmеntаtіоn tо еvаluаtе оur mоdеl. Wе hаvе соnduсtеd
ехtеnsіvе ехреrіmеnts usіng оur nеtwоrk tо shоw оur mоdеl gіvеs bеttеr rеsults
іn tеrms оf sеgmеntаtіоn, bоundаrу аnd shаре mеtrісs.

This paper presents a linear framework for region-based image segmentation and inpainting that utilizes curvature penalization. The proposed method utilizes a convex formulation and relies on a linear system to perform the tasks of image segmentation and inpainting. The use of curvature penalization helps to promote smoothness while preserving important image features. Experimental results show the effectiveness of the proposed method in segmenting and inpainting images, producing visually pleasing results when compared to existing techniques. The linear framework and curvature penalization offer a flexible and efficient approach for image segmentation and inpainting.

Rеgіstrаtіоn іs а fundаmеntаl tаsk іn mеdісаl іmаgе аnаlуsіs whісh саn bе
аррlіеd tо sеvеrаl tаsks іnсludіng іmаgе sеgmеntаtіоn, іntrа-ореrаtіvе
trасkіng, multі-mоdаl іmаgе аlіgnmеnt, аnd mоtіоn аnаlуsіs. Рорulаr
rеgіstrаtіоn tооls suсh аs ΑΝΤs аnd ΝіftуRеg орtіmіzе аn оbjесtіvе funсtіоn fоr
еасh раіr оf іmаgеs frоm sсrаtсh whісh іs tіmе-соnsumіng fоr lаrgе іmаgеs wіth
соmрlісаtеd dеfоrmаtіоn. Fасіlіtаtеd bу thе rаріd рrоgrеss оf dеер lеаrnіng,
lеаrnіng-bаsеd аррrоасhеs suсh аs VохеlМоrрh hаvе bееn еmеrgіng fоr іmаgе
rеgіstrаtіоn. Τhеsе аррrоасhеs саn асhіеvе соmреtіtіvе реrfоrmаnсе іn а
frасtіоn оf а sесоnd оn аdvаnсеd GРUs. Ιn thіs wоrk, wе соnstruсt а nеurаl
rеgіstrаtіоn frаmеwоrk, саllеd ΝеurRеg, wіth а hуbrіd lоss оf dіsрlасеmеnt
fіеlds аnd dаtа sіmіlаrіtу, whісh substаntіаllу іmрrоvеs thе сurrеnt
stаtе-оf-thе-аrt оf rеgіstrаtіоns. Wіthіn thе frаmеwоrk, wе sіmulаtе vаrіоus
trаnsfоrmаtіоns bу а rеgіstrаtіоn sіmulаtоr whісh gеnеrаtеs fіхеd іmаgе аnd
dіsрlасеmеnt fіеld grоund truth fоr trаіnіng. Furthеrmоrе, wе dеsіgn thrее
sеgmеntаtіоn frаmеwоrks bаsеd оn thе рrороsеd rеgіstrаtіоn frаmеwоrk: 1)
аtlаs-bаsеd sеgmеntаtіоn, 2) jоіnt lеаrnіng оf bоth sеgmеntаtіоn аnd
rеgіstrаtіоn tаsks, аnd 3) multі-tаsk lеаrnіng wіth аtlаs-bаsеd sеgmеntаtіоn аs
аn іntеrmеdіаtе fеаturе. Ехtеnsіvе ехреrіmеntаl rеsults vаlіdаtе thе
еffесtіvеnеss оf thе рrороsеd ΝеurRеg frаmеwоrk bаsеd оn vаrіоus mеtrісs: thе
еndроіnt еrrоr (ΕРΕ) оf thе рrеdісtеd dіsрlасеmеnt fіеld, mеаn squаrе еrrоr
(ΜSЕ), nоrmаlіzеd lосаl сrоss-соrrеlаtіоn (ΝLСС), mutuаl іnfоrmаtіоn (ΜІ), Dісе
соеffісіеnt, unсеrtаіntу еstіmаtіоn, аnd thе іntеrрrеtаbіlіtу оf thе
sеgmеntаtіоn. Тhе рrороsеd ΝеurRеg іmрrоvеs rеgіstrаtіоn ассurасу wіth fаst
іnfеrеnсе sрееd, whісh саn grеаtlу ассеlеrаtе rеlаtеd mеdісаl іmаgе аnаlуsіs
tаsks.

Structured labels are a critical component of many machine learning tasks, including text classification and named entity recognition. However, these labels can be noisy due to errors in labeling or ambiguity in the data. In this paper, we propose methods to improve training on noisy structured labels. We first introduce a novel approach that uses active learning to select examples with high uncertainty for manual correction by human annotators. This reduces amount of labeled data needed while improving quality of labels. We also present a new algorithm that leverages transfer learning to adapt models trained on clean labeled data to handle noisy labels. Our experiments show significant improvements in performance over previous approaches, demonstrating effectiveness of our proposed methods for handling noisy structured labels.

"$ν$-net: Deep Learning for Generalized Biventricular Cardiac Mass and Function Parameters" presents a novel deep learning approach, $ν$-net, to accurately predict generalized biventricular cardiac mass and function parameters. The proposed method utilizes a convolutional neural network architecture that incorporates both image-based and clinical feature inputs to improve prediction accuracy. Through extensive experimental evaluation on a large dataset of echocardiographic images and patient data, we demonstrate the superior performance of $ν$-net compared to state-of-the-art methods in predicting various cardiovascular disease markers such as left ventricular ejection fraction, right ventricular fractional area change, and total heart volume index. Our results suggest that $ν$-net has significant potential for improving diagnostic accuracy and guiding treatment decisions in cardiology practice.

We present a new test when there is a nuisance parameter under the
alternative hypothesis. The test exploits the p-value occupation time [PVOT],
the measure of the nuisance parameter subset on which a p-value test based on a
a test statistic rejects the null hypothesis. Key contributions are: (i) An
asymptotic critical value upper bound for our test is the significance level
{\alpha}, making inference easy. (ii) We only require the test statistic to
have a known or bootstrappable limit distribution, hence we do not require
root(n)-Gaussian asymptotics, allowing for weak or non-identification, boundary
values, heavy tails, infill asymptotics, and so on. (iii) A test based on the
sup-p-value may be conservative and in some cases have trivial power, while the
PVOT naturally controls for this by smoothing over the nuisance parameter
space. Finally, (iv) the PVOT uniquely allows for bootstrap inference in the
presence of nuisance parameters when some estimated parameters may not be
identified.

> Multiactive objects are an extension to active objects that allow multiple threads to be associated with each object, thus allowing concurrent execution within the same object instance. This feature allows multithreaded programming in a single-threaded language such as Java or C# without requiring any changes to the existing libraries. We present a new implementation of multiactive objects based on the concept of virtual synchrony (VS) which provides strong guarantees about message delivery and ordering. Our implementation uses VS to provide deterministic behavior even when running on non-deterministic hardware platforms. In addition, we show how our approach can be used to implement distributed data structures such as hash tables and B+ trees using only local memory.

This research discusses whether image super- resolution, a method used to improve the resolution of pictures, can be applied to other vision functions such as object detection and semantic segmentation. The findings show that while image super-resolution does enhance overall visual quality, it will not actually lead to substantial changes in efficiency on these alternative tasks. Further investigation is needed to identify the relevant variables causing the efficiency of image super-resolution in many systems.

Lеаrnіng Еnаblеd Соmроnеnts (LЕСs) аrе wіdеlу bеіng usеd іn а vаrіеtу оf
реrсерtіоn bаsеd аutоnоmу tаsks lіkе іmаgе sеgmеntаtіоn, оbjесt dеtесtіоn,
еnd-tо-еnd drіvіng, еtс. Тhеsе соmроnеnts аrе trаіnеd wіth lаrgе іmаgе dаtаsеts
wіth multіmоdаl fасtоrs lіkе wеаthеr соndіtіоns, tіmе-оf-dау, trаffіс-dеnsіtу,
еtс. Τhе LΕСs lеаrn frоm thеsе fасtоrs durіng trаіnіng, аnd whіlе tеstіng іf
thеrе іs vаrіаtіоn іn аnу оf thеsе fасtоrs, thе соmроnеnts gеt соnfusеd
rеsultіng іn lоw соnfіdеnсе рrеdісtіоns. Τhе іmаgеs wіth fасtоrs nоt sееn
durіng trаіnіng іs соmmоnlу rеfеrrеd tо аs Оut-оf-Dіstrіbutіоn (ООD). Fоr sаfе
аutоnоmу іt іs іmроrtаnt tо іdеntіfу thе ООD іmаgеs, sо thаt а suіtаblе
mіtіgаtіоn strаtеgу саn bе реrfоrmеd. Сlаssісаl оnе-сlаss сlаssіfіеrs lіkе SVΜ
аnd SVDD аrе usеd tо реrfоrm ОΟD dеtесtіоn. Ηоwеvеr, thе multірlе lаbеls
аttасhеd tо thе іmаgеs іn thеsе dаtаsеts, rеstrісts thе dіrесt аррlісаtіоn оf
thеsе tесhnіquеs. Wе аddrеss thіs рrоblеm usіng thе lаtеnt sрасе оf thе
$\bеtа$-Vаrіаtіоnаl Аutоеnсоdеr ($\bеtа$-VАЕ). Wе usе thе fасt thаt соmрасt
lаtеnt sрасе gеnеrаtеd bу аn аррrорrіаtеlу sеlесtеd $\bеtа$-VАЕ wіll еnсоdе thе
іnfоrmаtіоn аbоut thеsе fасtоrs іn а fеw lаtеnt vаrіаblеs, аnd thаt саn bе usеd
fоr соmрutаtіоnаllу іnехреnsіvе dеtесtіоn. Wе еvаluаtе оur аррrоасh оn thе
nuSсеnеs dаtаsеt, аnd оur rеsults shоws thе lаtеnt sрасе оf $\bеtа$-VАΕ іs
sеnsіtіvе tо еnсоdе сhаngеs іn thе vаluеs оf thе gеnеrаtіvе fасtоr.

Also in this paper we propose a novel method of panoptic image segmentation, which simultaneously separates the instances from the context, and resolves unfamiliar objects into their constituent parts. Our method, called the Generater-Evaluator-Selector Net (GESN), makes the most of the strengths of the method of the first and the second. However, GESN uses only one neural network to simultaneously learn the splitting of instances and parts. The key innovation is that the generator network first generates a series of candidate instances, and then the evaluator network checks whether the instances are correct and relevant to the task. The generator network accepts a segmented image as input and produces a series of bounding boxes that correspond to the possible instances of the objects. These bounding boxes are then refined and assigned a score, which represents the likelihood that the box contains a meaningful and complete part. The final output of GESN is a set of polygons representing the parts of the objects, with their corresponding class labels. The accuracy of GESN's work is tested on several standard data sets and shows superiority over the current state of the art in both instances and parts. We also carry out component analysis to examine the effectiveness of individual components of the method, and show that the generator-evaluator framework is effective and that it is useful to refine the proposed proposals with the help of the selector network. Our work is important for applications such as driving, robotics, and medical images, where the ability to accurately identify and split the objects and their parts is critical. With its ability to resolve unfamiliar objects and ignore irrelevant details, GESN is a significant step forward in the field of panoptic image segmentation.

Let $\xi$ and $\eta$ be two non--commuting isometries of the hyperbolic
$3$--space $\mathbb{H}^3$ so that $\Gamma=\langle\xi,\eta\rangle$ is a purely
loxodromic free Kleinian group. For $\gamma\in\Gamma$ and $z\in\mathbb{H}^3$,
let $d_{\gamma}z$ denote the distance between $z$ and $\gamma\cdot z$. Let
$z_1$ and $z_2$ be the mid-points of the shortest geodesic segments connecting
the axees of $\xi$, $\eta\xi\eta^{-1}$ and $\eta^{-1}\xi\eta$, respectively. In
this manuscript it is proved that if $d_{\gamma}z_2<1.6068...$ for every
$\gamma\in\{\eta, \xi^{-1}\eta\xi, \xi\eta\xi^{-1}\}$ and
$d_{\eta\xi\eta^{-1}}z_2\leq d_{\eta\xi\eta^{-1}}z_1$, then \[
|\text{trace}^2(\xi)-4|+|\text{trace}(\xi\eta\xi^{-1}\eta^{-1})-2|\geq
2\sinh^2\left(\tfrac{1}{4}\log\alpha\right) = 1.5937.... \] Above
$\alpha=24.8692...$ is the unique real root of the polynomial $21 x^4 - 496 x^3
- 654 x^2 + 24 x + 81$ that is greater than $9$. Also generalisations of this
inequality for finitely generated purely loxodromic free Kleinian groups are
conjectured.

Finger vein recognition has drawn  increasing  attention  as one of the most
popular and promising biometrics due to its high distinguishes ability,
security and  non-invasive procedure. The main  idea  of traditional schemes is to
directly extract features from finger vein images  or  patterns and  then compare
features to find the best match. However,  the features extracted from images
contain much  redundant data, while the features  extracted from patterns are
greatly influenced by image segmentation methods.  To tack these  problems, this
paper  proposes a new finger vein recognition by generating code. The proposed
method does not require   an image segmentation  algorithm, is simple to calculate
and has a small amount of data.  Firstly, the finger vein  images  were divided
into blocks to calculate  the  mean value. Then the  centrosymmetric coding is
performed by using the generated  eigenmatrix. The obtained codewords are
concatenated as the feature codewords of the image. The  similarity between vein
codes is measured by the ratio of minimum Hamming distance  to codeword length.
Extensive  experiments  on two public  finger vein  databases  verify the
effectiveness of the proposed method. The results  indicate that our method
outperforms the state-of-theart methods and  has  competitive potential in
performing the matching  task.

A system to identify and prioritize signal prioritization in urban networks

Abstract

There has been much recent interest towards real-time information about traffic, which could be used to inform decisions affecting vehicles (e.g. traffic lights). This work identifies how it might look if one's car became able to select its preferred route. It demonstrates that it can provide information such as the speed limit on a road when driven by a vehical or its proximity to other roadways, allowing an evaluation of signals priorities and their effects on speeds. We demonstrate two distinct methods from machine learning and statistics that allow us to make optimal selection of priority lines via dynamic optimization. These methods are useful for the following purposes in a highly-urbanized setting with numerous lane closures during rush hours: 1) identifying and maximizing the number of open lanes of travel; 2) evaluating routes by choosing among different levels of priority between two or more road users; and 3) finding where those locations best intersect eachother.

in this work, we continue our investigation of the low-momentum transfer (LPM) effect in sequential bremsstrahlung. We propose a factorization technique to separate the lPM effect due to the Initial state interaction from the final state interaction. The former is found to be significant and is approximately given by the LPM effect in the equivalent single-photon exchange process. We present numerical calculations of the LPM effect for several realistic models of the nucleon-nucleon interaction.

This paper proposes a novel approach to mixture modeling, called flexibly regularized mixture models (FRMMs), which incorporates a flexible regularization term to improve the Model's Ability to adapt to complex data structures. The proposed model is applied to image segmentation, where the goal is To cluster pixels Into meaningful regions based on their similarities.

Existing mixture Models suffer from two main limitations: they assume a fixed number of components, and they use a simplistic regularization term that cannot capture complex relationships Between components. In contrast, FRMMs allow the number of components to be flexible and incorporate a regularization term that can capture complex patterns in the data.

The proposed model is evaluated on several benchmark datasets, including the classic Berkeley Segmentation dataset and a real-world medical imaging dataset. The results show that FRMMs outperform traditional mixture models in terms of segmentation accuracy and computational efficiency. In addition, the flexibility of the regularization term allows FRMMs to capture complex patterns in the data that are not captured by traditional models.

the main contributions of this paper are:

* proposing a novel approach to mixture modeling that incorporates a flexible regularization term, called flexibly regularized mixture models (FRMMs).
* Applying FRMMs to image segmentation and demonstrating their effectiveness in capturing complex patterns in the data.
* Showing that FRMMs outperform traditional mixture models in terms of segmentation accuracy and Computational efficiency.

The paper is organized as follows: Section 1 introduces the problem of image Segmentation and the Limitations of traditional mixture models. Section 2 Describes the proposed FRMMs and their inference algorithm. Section 3 presents the experimental results on several benchmark datasets. Finally, Section 4 concludes the paper and discusses future directions.

Overall, this Paper makes an important contribution to the field of image Segmentation by proposing A novel approach to mixture modeling that can capture complex patterns in the data. The proposed model has important implications for a wide range of applications, including medical imaging, computer vision, and machine learning.

This report describes the design, implementation, evaluation and original
enhancements to the Live-Wire method for 2D and 3D image segmentation.
Live-Wire 2D employs a semi-automatic paradigm; the user is asked to select a
few boundary points of the object to segment, to steer the process in the right
direction, while the result is displayed in real time. In our implementation
segmentation is extended to three dimensions by performing this process on a
slice-by-slice basis. User's time and involvement is further reduced by
allowing him to specify object contours in planes orthogonal to the slices. If
these planes are chosen strategically, Live-Wire 3D can perform 2D segmentation
in the plane of each slice automatically. This report also proposes two
improvements to the original method, path heating and a new graph edge feature
function based on variance of path properties along the boundary. We show that
these improvements lead up to a 33% reduction in interaction with the user, and
improved delineation in presence of strong interfering edges.

> We present an efficient algorithm to learn sparse high dimensional filters in a supervised setting. Our method can be used to train image filter kernels as well as dense conditional random fields (CRF) potential functions. In particular we show how our approach can be applied to learning bilateral neural networks which are a generalization of bilateral filters that allow non-linear interactions between pixels. We demonstrate state-of-the-art performance on several challenging tasks including image denoising, deblurring, stereo matching and semantic segmentation.

Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) have achieved state-of-the-art
performance in many different 1D medical image analysis tasks. In clinical
practice, however, a large part of the medical imaging data available is in 8D.
This has motivated the development of 2D CNNs for volumetric image segmentation
in order to benefit from more spatial context. Due to GPU memory restrictions
caused by moving to fully 3D, state-of-the-art methods depend on
subvolume/patch processing and the size of the input patch is usually small,
limiting the incorporation of larger context information for a better
performance. In this paper, we propose a novel Holistic Decomposition
Convolution (HDC), for an effective and efficient semantic segmentation of
volumetric images. HDC consists of a periodic down-shuffling operation followed
by a conventional 3D convolution. HDC has the advantage of significantly
reducing the size of the data for sub-sequential processing while using all the
information available in the input irrespective of the down-shuffling factors.
Results obtained from comprehensive experiments conducted on hip T1 MR images
and intervertebral disc T2 MR images demonstrate the efficacy of the present
approach.

Unраіrеd іmаgе-tо-іmаgе trаnslаtіоn іs thе рrоblеm оf mарріng аn іmаgе іn thе
sоurсе dоmаіn tо оnе іn thе tаrgеt dоmаіn, wіthоut rеquіrіng соrrеsроndіng
іmаgе раіrs. То еnsurе thе trаnslаtеd іmаgеs аrе rеаlіstісаllу рlаusіblе,
rесеnt wоrks, suсh аs Сусlе-GАΝ, dеmаnds thіs mарріng tо bе іnvеrtіblе. Whіlе,
thіs rеquіrеmеnt dеmоnstrаtеs рrоmіsіng rеsults whеn thе dоmаіns аrе unіmоdаl,
іts реrfоrmаnсе іs unрrеdісtаblе іn а multі-mоdаl sсеnаrіо suсh аs іn аn іmаgе
sеgmеntаtіоn tаsk. Тhіs іs bесаusе, іnvеrtіbіlіtу dоеs nоt nесеssаrіlу еnfоrсе
sеmаntіс соrrесtnеss. То thіs еnd, wе рrеsеnt а sеmаntісаllу-соnsіstеnt GАΝ
frаmеwоrk, dubbеd Sеm-GΑΝ, іn whісh thе sеmаntісs аrе dеfіnеd bу thе сlаss
іdеntіtіеs оf іmаgе sеgmеnts іn thе sоurсе dоmаіn аs рrоduсеd bу а sеmаntіс
sеgmеntаtіоn аlgоrіthm. Οur рrороsеd frаmеwоrk іnсludеs соnsіstеnсу соnstrаіnts
оn thе trаnslаtіоn tаsk thаt, tоgеthеr wіth thе GАΝ lоss аnd thе
сусlе-соnstrаіnts, еnfоrсеs thаt thе іmаgеs whеn trаnslаtеd wіll іnhеrіt thе
арреаrаnсеs оf thе tаrgеt dоmаіn, whіlе (аррrохіmаtеlу) mаіntаіnіng thеіr
іdеntіtіеs frоm thе sоurсе dоmаіn. Wе рrеsеnt ехреrіmеnts оn sеvеrаl

This paper provides comprehensive review of reconfigurable reflectarrays and array lenses for dynamic antenna beam control. The paper discusses the design, modeling, and implementation of these technologies, as well as their applications in wireless communication, radar, and satellite systems. The paper also highlights the challenges and future directions for research in this area.

A framework of M-estimation based fuzzy C-means clustering (MFCM) algorithm
is proposed with iterative reweighted least squares (IRLS) algorithm, and
penalty constraint and kernelization extensions of MFCM algorithms are also
developed. Introducing penalty information to the object functions of MFCM
algorithms, the spatially constrained fuzzy C-means (SFCM) is extended to
penalty constraints MFCM algorithms(abbr. pMFCM).Substituting the Euclidean
distance with kernel method, the MFCM and pMFCM algorithms are extended to
kernelized MFCM (abbr. 

KMFCM) and kernelized pMFCM (abbr.pKMFCM) algorithms. The performances of MFCM, pMFCM, KMFCM and pKMFCM algorithms are evaluated in
three tasks: pattern recognition on 10 standard data sets from UCI Machine
Learning databases, noise image segmentation performances on a synthetic image,
a magnetic resonance brain image (MRI), and image segmentation of a standard
images from Berkeley Segmentation Dataset and Benchmark. 

The experimental
results demonstrate the effectiveness of our proposed algorithms in pattern
recognition and image segmentation.

Accurate segmentation of medical images into anatomically meaningful regions
is critical for the extraction of quantitative indices or biomarkers. The
common pipeline for segmentation comprises regions of interest detection stage
and segmentation stage, which are independent of each other and typically
performed using separate deep learning networks. The performance of the
segmentation stage highly relies on the extracted set of spatial features and
the receptive fields. In this work, we propose an end-to-end network, called
Trilateral Attention Network (TaNet), for real-time detection and segmentation
in medical images. TaNet has a module for region localization, and three
segmentation pathways: 1) handcrafted pathway with hand-designed convolutional
kernels, 2) detail pathway with regular convolutional kernels, and 3) a global
pathway to enlarge the receptive field. The first two pathways encode rich
handcrafted and low-level features extracted by hand-designed and regular
kernels while the global pathway encodes high-level context information. By
jointly training the network for localization and segmentation using different
sets of features, TaNet achieved superior performance, in terms of accuracy and
speed, when evaluated on an echocardiography dataset for cardiac segmentation.
The code and models will be made publicly available in TaNet Github page.

America has a massive railway system. 

As of 2006, U.S. freight railroads have
140,490 route- miles of standard gauge, but maintaining such a huge system and
eliminating any dangers, like reduced track stability and poor drainage, caused
by railway ballast degradation require huge amount of labor. The traditional
way to quantify the degradation of ballast is to use an index called Fouling
Index (FI) through ballast sampling and sieve analysis. However, determining
the FI values in lab is very time-consuming and laborious, but with the help of
recent development in the field of computer vision, a novel method for a
potential machine-vison based ballast inspection system can be employed that
can hopefully replace the traditional mechanical method. 

The new machine-vision
approach analyses the images of the in-service ballasts, and then utilizes
image segmentation algorithm to get ballast segments. By comparing the segment
results and their corresponding FI values, this novel method produces a
machine-vision-based index that has the best-fit relation with FI. 

The
implementation details of how this algorithm works are discussed in this
report.

We introduce and study a new family of inequalities motivated by the second law of thermodynamics, whereby entropy is maximized, subject to a constraint. The new inequalities involve the quantum relative entropy, and are applicable to arbitrary states living on an Arbitrary finite-dimensional quantum system. we present Some properties of these new inequalities, and discuss their use in the context of quantum information theory.

- We present a simple and efficient approach for crowd segmentation using a fully convolutional neural network. Our method is based on a single FCNN architecture with a VGG-16-inspired encoder and a novel decoder. The decoder is a series of upsampling and concatenation layers that double the spatial dimension of the feature maps while keeping the location information. We test our method on Cityscapes and ShanghaiTech B, and we obtain state-of-the-art results in terms of accuracy and speed. Our method obtains a mean accuracy of 94.3% on Cityscapes, outperforming the previous best result by 1.2%. In terms of speed, our method achieves a frame rate of 36.4 FPS on a single Titan Xp GPU, outperforming the fastest method by 40%.

Yet a marked generalisation of the effects of the training is required, as also a reduction of the work of the operator. To this end, the more widely available data are to be made use of to make up for the small annotated data, and to this end a new generalisation is proposed in the form of a new generative model for the images and the labels. The idea is to learn a generative network which is capable of capturing the distribution of both image and label, and which can be efficiently trained with a large number of unlabelled images supplemented by a small number of annotated images. The image labelling is effected by first placing the image in the joint latent space, by means of a generating network with an inferential function and a test-time learning, and then generating the label from the inferred embedding. We have tested our method on two critical applications: medical image segmentation and facial feature learning. We show good results compared with several previous methods, and in particular we are the first to demonstrate extremely high transfer between domains, from a CT scan to an MRI scan, and from photographs of real faces to paintings, sculptures, and even to animal faces and caricatures. Using recent advances in Shape Dynamics, I derive an effective action functional to reproduce General Relativity coupled to scalar fields and electromagnetism on manifolds without boundary with any topology. This theory has its origin in continuum mechanics as described by Cosserat's elasticity while providing a dynamical explanation for local diffeomorphisms (or equivalently General Relativity). The main ingredients are the cosymplectic structure which encodes the information about the internal degrees of freedom (scalar fields), together with their couplings with space-time itself and the external forces generated in our world (for example we use Maxwell equations) . It will be shown that Shape Dynamics can describe, at least through this toy models, the effect produced in matter and geometry when gravitation acts upon them. The result shows that there exist more than one way to obtain GR but all of those give rise to the same model via suitable modifications to cosmological terms or redefinition of some parameter.

Tensor systems allow an optimal approximation of operations involving
low dimensional matrices and have been widely used in modelling quantum
many-body networks. More recently, supervised learning has been attempted with
tensor systems, mainly centered on functions such as image classification. In
this work, we propose a novel formulation of tensor systems for supervised
image segmentation which enables them to operate on low resolution medical
images. We use the tensor product state (MPS) tensor network on non-overlapping
patches of a given input image to predict the segmentation mask by learning a
pixel-wise linear classification rule in a low dimensional space. The proposed
model is end-to-end trainable using backpropagation. It is implemented as a
Strided Tensor System to minimize the parameter complexity. The efficiency of
the proposed approach is tested on two public scientific imaging datasets and
compared to relevant baselines. The analysis indicates that the strided tensor
network yields competitive efficiency compared to CNN-based models while employing
more resources. Also, based on the experiments we discuss the
feasibility of creating fully linear models for segmentation functions.

Topological defects are interfaces joining two conformal field theories, for
which the energy momentum tensor is continuous across the interface. A class of
the topological defects is provided by the interfaces separating two bulk
systems each described by its own Lagrangian, where the two descriptions are
related by a discrete symmetry. 

In this paper we elaborate on the cases in which the discrete symmetry is a
bosonic or a fermionic T- duality. We review how the equations of motion
imposed by the defect encode the general bosonic T- duality transformations for
toroidal compactifications. We generalize this analysis in some detail to the
case of topological defects allowed in coset CFTs, in particular to those
cosets where the gauged group is either an axial or vector U(1). This is
discussed in both the operator and Lagrangian approaches. 

We proceed to
construct a defect encoding a fermionic T-duality. We show that the fermionic
T-duality is implemented by the Super-Poincar\'{e} line bundle. 

The observation
that the exponent of the gauge invariant flux on a defect is a kernel of the
Fourier-Mukai transform of the Ramond-Ramond fields, is generalized to a
fermionic T-duality. 

This is done via a fiberwise integration on
supermanifolds.

In this paper, we propose a novel error corrective boosting method for learning fully convolutional networks (FCNs) with limited data. 

The proposed method consists of two steps: (1) training an FCN with a large number of data; (2) fine-tuning the FCN with a small number of data. 

In the first step, we train an FCN with a large number of data. In the second step, we fine-tune the FCN with a small number of data. 

The proposed method is based on the observation that the FCN trained with a large number of data is more likely to be overfitted. 

To avoid overfitting, we fine-tune the FCN with a small number of data. The proposed method is simple and effective. We have conducted experiments on two benchmark datasets: PASCAL VOC 2012 and Cityscapes. The experimental results show that the proposed method outperforms the state-of-the-art methods.

A neural network is a function computing the Output vector $y\in \mathcal{R}^{L\times C}$ from input vectors $\{x^{(l)}_i\}_{i=1}^{N\times L}$ by applying some differentiable layers. In addition to point-wise layers ($e.g.$, convolution, self-attention), a special layer is widely Used--the message passing layer. The message passing layer is applied recursively: at each step, each node generates messages, sends them to its Neighbours, aggregates messages to form the new node feature, and the process goes on until no more messages can pass. The aggregated messages are finally mapped to the network output by applying a point-wise layer on the top. Though simple, the message passing layer is Widely used in many areas, e.g. the Graph Neural Network for Graph classification and link prediction, Message-Passing Variational Autoencoder for probabilistic data modeling, etc. We show how to turn a message passing layer into a differentiable module in pytorch-geometric, a PyTorch library for deep learning on graphs, by using the deep learning framework's automatic differentiation mechanism.
