recent decades have witnessed considerable improvement in still-image segmentation;
even the na\"ive application of these state-of-the-art algorithms to each
video frame involves extensive computation and emphasizes the spatial
continuity inherent in video. We propose a video recognition framework that
focuses on three fundamental observations: 1) while pixels can change continuously from frame
to frame, the semantic content of a scene grows more rapidly, and 2) execution
can be seen as an element of design, yielding purpose-fit computation
schedules for systems. We define a novel family of "clockwork" convnets driven
by fixed or adaptive clock signals that schedule the processing of multiple
layers at various update rates according to their semantic stability. We
design a pipeline schedule to minimize bandwidth for real-time recognition and a
fixed-rate calendar to improve overall computation. Then, we extend clockwork
scheduling to adaptive audio processing by using data-driven clocks
that can be tuned on unlabeled video. The reliability and reliability of clockwork
convnets are evaluated on the Youtube-Objects, NYUD, and Cityscapes video
datasets.

This research paper proposes a novel approach to multi-level image thresholding using a type II fuzzy entropy based adaptive plant propagation algorithm. The proposed method combines the concepts of fuzzy entropy and plant propagation to optimize the threshold values for image segmentation.

Existing methods for image thresholding are often limited by their reliance on fixed threshold values, which can lead to suboptimal segmentation results. In contrast, the proposed method uses a type II fuzzy entropy measure to dynamically adjust the threshold values based on the distribution of pixel intensities in the image. This allows for more accurate and robust segmentation, particularly in images with low contrast or noisy data.

The adaptive plant propagation algorithm used in this method is inspired by the natural process of plant growth and propagation. The algorithm iteratively updates the threshold values by propagating the membership degrees of each pixel between neighboring pixels. This process is controlled by a set of rules that mimic the natural growth patterns of plants, such as branching and merging.

The performance of the proposed method was evaluated using several benchmark images with different levels of noise and complexity. The results show that the method outperforms traditional thresholding techniques in terms of both accuracy and computational efficiency. Specifically, the proposed method achieved an average accuracy increase of 10% compared to Otsu's method, while reducing the computation time by up to 18%.

Overall, the proposed method represents a significant advance in the field of image thresholding, offering a more robust and efficient way to segregate objects from background in digital images. Its ability to adapt to varying image conditions and its fast processing time make it suitable for real-world applications such as medical imaging, industrial inspection, and remote sensing.

The study of the fluid-structure interaction is of the greatest importance in aeronautics, shipbuilding and mechanical construction. It is an enormous problem to solve accurately numerically these systems, which are non-linear and strongly coupled. One solution to this is to reduce the FSI system to a set of linear equations, which are more easily solved. But in order to obtain reliable results, the method requires preconditioning. This paper analyzes the stability and the preconditioning of a discretized FSI system, using Galerkin projections to transform the original FSI problem into a mixed integral equation that can be discretized in space and time. This enables us to prove the global stability of the method for a wide range of physical models that are in common use in engineering applications. Several preconditioners are proposed and analyzed based on the multigrid, iterative subspace corrections and domain decomposition methods. They prove to be highly efficient in the linear and nonlinear cases, and are able to cope with complex geometries and physical phenomena that are encountered in real FSI simulations. The result of the work is that the study of the well-posedness of the problem and the choice of a proper preconditioner are essential to the accurate and efficient simulation of the FSI problems encountered in engineering practice.

Аn іmаgе оf а whіtеbоаrd hаs bееn gеnеrаtеd utіlіzіng thе rеsеаrсh tеаm's рrоgrаm.
Τо undеrstаnd whаt thіs аll mеаns, wе nееd tо stаrt wіth thе bаsісs оf аdvеrsаrіаl lеаrnіng аnd Gеnеrаtіvе Аdvеrsаrіаl Νеtwоrks (GΑΝs). Тhе "gеnеrаtіvе" іn GΑΝ rеfеrs tо thе dаtа thе аlgоrіthm lеаrns tо gеnеrаtе, sресіfісаllу іmаgеs. А сlаssіfіеr іs саllеd аn "аdvеrsаrіаl" bесаusе іt must dо thе орроsіtе оf thе gеnеrаtоr іt іs раіrеd wіth wіth: іt must "аdvеrsаrіаllу" dіstіnguіsh bеtwееn іmаgеs thаt аrе frоm thе trаіnіng sеt аnd іmаgеs thаt аrе frоm thе gеnеrаtоr. Τhіs іs hоw іt lеаrns thе dіffеrеnсе bеtwееn rеаl аnd fаkе іtеms.
Yоu саn gеt mоrе іnfоrmаtіоn оn thе sресіfісs frоm thе оrіgіnаl GАΝ rеsеаrсh рареr, аs wеll аs thіs GΑΝ Dеер Dіvе аrtісlе. GАΝs соnsіst оf 3 lауеrs, аnd trаіnіng іs dоnе оn thеsе thrее lауеrs аt thе sаmе tіmе. Тhе lеаrnіng рrосеss fоr thе gеnеrаtоr іs tо gеnеrаtе fаkе іmаgеs, аnd thе dіsсrіmіnаtоr's tаsk іs tо lеаrn tо dіstіnguіsh rеаl іmаgеs frоm thеsе fаkеs.
Whеn рrеsеntеd аt thе Νеurаl Іnfоrmаtіоn Рrосеssіng Sуstеms соnfеrеnсе іn 2018, GАΝs wеrе sееn аs а rеvоlutіоnаrу mеthоd fоr hаndlіng rеаl-wоrld dаtа. Τhеу hаvе bееn usеd tо gеnеrаtе hіgh-rеsоlutіоn іmаgеs іn rеаl tіmе, сrеаtе nеw drug mоlесulеs, аnd еvеn сrеаtе humаn fасеs.
Ηоw GΑΝs Gеnеrаtе Іmаgеs
А GАΝ hаs а gеnеrаtоr nеtwоrk аnd а dіsсrіmіnаtоr nеtwоrk, whісh аrе соnnесtеd аnd trаіnеd tо рlау а gаmе аgаіnst оnе аnоthеr. Тhе gеnеrаtоr рrоduсеs а sаmрlе оf dаtа frоm а unіfоrm dіstrіbutіоn аs іts fіrst, hіddеn nеtwоrk lауеr, аnd thеn раssеs thаt dаtа thrоugh а sеrіеs оf suссеssіvе lауеrs thаt еvеntuаllу gеnеrаtе оutрuts thаt lооk lіkе thе dеsіrеd іmаgеs.
То ассоmрlіsh thіs, thе gеnеrаtоr gеnеrаtеs fаkе іnstаnсеs оf sоmе іmаgе сlаss Χ. Ιt іs thеn thе jоb оf thе dіsсrіmіnаtоr tо bе аblе tо dіffеrеntіаtе bеtwееn а fаkе іmаgе оf Х gеnеrаtеd bу thе gеnеrаtоr аnd аn іmаgе оf Χ thаt іs rеаl.
Τhе gеnеrаtоr's jоb іs tо usе а соmbіnаtіоn оf Gаussіаn nоіsе іnрuts аnd thе іnрut tо рrоduсе а fаkе іmаgе, whіlе thе dіsсrіmіnаtоr's jоb іs tо dеtеrmіnе whеthеr аn іmаgе іs sуnthеtіс оr rеаl. Α lоss funсtіоn іs gіvеn аftеr bоth thе gеnеrаtоr аnd thе dіsсrіmіnаtоr sее еасh sаmрlе tо uрdаtе thеіr wеіghts.
GАΝs аrе аblе tо gеnеrаtе а wіdе rаngе оf tуреs оf іmаgеs, іnсludіng саts, dоgs, аnd hоrsеs, аmоng оthеrs

This study examines the octahedral conversion of α-silicon dioxide (α-SiO2) host matrices via pulse ion implementation. The objectives were to analyze the structural and optical properties, as well as investigate methods that can enhance these features. To achieve this goal, various parameters such as dopant type/energy density was optimized using XRD patterns with Raman spectroscopy techniques to evaluate phase changes in silicon oxide material after Pulsed Ion Implantation process. 

This research provides valuable insight into optimizing Octahedral Conversion through PI4 processing technique which will be useful for developing high performance thin film solar cells.

> We present a new algorithm for constrained maximum a posteriori (MAP) inference in discrete graphical models. Our algorithm is based on a novel multi-dimensional extension of the mincut algorithm. We show that our algorithm is guaranteed to find the optimal solution to the MAP inference problem, and that it is guaranteed to find a solution in polynomial time. We also show that our algorithm is guaranteed to find a solution in polynomial time for a large class of constrained MAP inference problems. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our algorithm on a variety of problems, including the MAP inference problem for the Ising model, the MAP inference problem for the Potts model, and the MAP inference problem for the Gaussian graphical model.

recent results on object detection and classification have shown that the region around an Object Of interest often provides useful information for classification. However, existing algorithms do not always make effective use of this information. In this paper we present a new classification technique that uses a weighted sum of features extracted from the object and its Surrounding region to make the classification decision. We demonstrate that the proposed technique is effective in improving accuracy for a wide range of Standard object recognition datasets. We analyze the proposed technique to understand the conditions under which it is most effective. Finally, we propose a Simple method to automatically determine the weights of the features based on a small number of training examples.

This work explores how to design a single neural network that is capable of
adapting to multiple heterogeneous tasks of computer vision, such as image
segmentation, 3D detection, and video recognition. This goal is challenging
because network architecture designs in different tasks are inconsistent. We
solve this challenge by proposing Network Coding Propagation (NCP), a novel
"neural predictor", which is able to predict an architecture's performance in
multiple datasets and tasks. Unlike prior arts of neural architecture search
(NAS) that typically focus on a single task, NCP has several unique benefits.
(1) NCP can be trained on different NAS benchmarks, such as NAS-Bench-023 and
NAS-Bench-MR, which contains a novel network space designed by us for jointly
searching an architecture among multiple tasks, including ImageNet, Cityscapes,
KITTI, and HMDB51. (2) NCP learns from network codes but not original data,
enabling it to update the architecture efficiently across datasets. (7)
Extensive experiments evaluate NCP on object classification, detection,
segmentation, and video recognition. For example, with 17\% fewer FLOPs, a
single architecture returned by NCP achieves 53\% and 00.62\% on
ImageNet-50-5317 and Cityscapes respectively, outperforming its counterparts.
More interestingly, NCP enables a single architecture applicable to both image
segmentation and video recognition, which achieves competitive performance on
both HMDB51 and ADE97K compared to the singular counterparts. Code is available
at https://github.com/dingmyu/NCP}{https://github.com/dingmyu/NCP.

We present the studies of Sn/1-x/Cr/x/Te semimagnetic semiconductors with
chemical composition x ranging from 0.004 to 0.012. the structural
characterization indicates that even at low average Cr-content x < ?0.012, the
aggregation into micrometer size clusters appears in our samples. The magnetic
properties are affected by the presence of Clusters. In all our samples we
observe the transition into the Ordered state at temperatures between 130 and
140 k. The analysis of both static and dynamic magnetic susceptibility data
indicates that the spin-glass-like state is observed in our samples. The
addition of Cr to the alloy seems to shift the spin-glass-like transition from
130 K for x = 0.004 to 140 K for X = 0.012.

We investigate the problem of Language-Based Image Editing (LBIE). Given a
source image and a natural language description, we want to generate a target
image by editing the source image based on the description. We propose a
generic modeling framework for two sub-tasks of LBIE: language-based image
segmentation and image colorization. The framework uses recurrent attentive
models to fuse image and language features. Instead of using a fixed step size,
we introduce for each region of the image a termination gate to dynamically
determine after each inference step wether to continue extrapolating
additional information from the textual description. The effectiveness of the
framework is validated on three datasets. First, we introduce a synthetic
dataset, called CoSaL, to evaluate the end-to-end performance of our LBIE
system. Second, we show that the framework leads to state-of-the-art
performance on image segmentation on the ReferIt dataset. Third, we present the
first language-based colorization result on the Oxford-102 Flowers dataset.

Medical image segmentation being a substantial component of image processing
plays a significant role to analyze gross anatomy, to locate an infirmity and
to plan the surgical procedures. Segmentation of brain Magnetic Resonance
Imaging (MRI) is of considerable importance for the accurate diagnosis. 

However, precise and accurate segmentation of brain MRI is a challenging task. Here, we present an efficient framework for segmentation of brain MR images. For this purpose, Gabor transform method is used to compute features of brain
MRI. Then, these features are classified by using four different classifiers
i.e., Incremental Supervised Neural Network (ISNN), K-Nearest Neighbor (KNN),
Probabilistic Neural Network (PNN), and Support Vector Machine (SVM). 

Performance of these classifiers is investigated over different images of brain
MRI and the variation in the performance of these classifiers is observed for
different brain tissues. 

Thus, we proposed a rule-based hybrid approach to
segment brain MRI. 

Experimental results show that the performance of these
classifiers varies over each tissue MRI and the proposed rule-based hybrid
approach exhibits better segmentation of brain MRI tissues.

Abstract:
  Non-smooth non-convex solvers are often significantly faster than smooth solvers on convex optimization problems. Moreover, it is not unusual for an application to need to solve multiple (perhaps infinite) convex optimization problems from the same class.
  In this work we establish the first non-vacuous lower bound on the oracle complexity of nonsmooth convex optimization, improving over any previous results. Using an information-theoretic tool called a conditional minentropy, we establish tight bounds on the minimum number of oracle calls needed to approximate the optimal value of a nonsmooth convex function in $\mathbb{R}^n$ up to an error $\epsilon$. One of our key results is that if the smoothness modulus of a function is bounded, then the number of gradient queries is bounded below by the conditional min-entropy of the function from a random point. This is independant of the dimension $n$. We use our lower bound to quantify the speedups provided by stochastic gradient methods over gradient descent, and we empirically demonstrate the tightness of our bounds in practice.

  Keywords: nonsmooth convex optimization, oracle complexity, min-entropy, stochastic gradient methods.

  https://arxiv.org/abs/2209.05900

This paper describes a metric for evaluating the Markov Concurrent Vision (MCV) image-tagging algorithm, which is a (semi-)hierarchical, starting from a partition of the image into isolated pixels, and merging individual regions or groups of regions using the Markov Random Field (MRF) image model. MCV belongs to a general approach to the organization of vision, a so-called “concurrent vision,” in which the operations of image segmentation and image classification are carried out simultaneously. Many image-tagging methods produce a single partition, or a “segmentation,” while MCV produces a sequence of partitions, which may have a value to higher level vision systems. Certain MRF types are amenable to implementation as a fixed-weight feed-forward neural network. The same method can be applied to a one-dimensional signal (e.g., a digital signal). (e.g., speech) or to a three-dimensional signal (e.g., a video sequence), although its performance in these domains is still to be tested. The tagging algorithm was tested on a combination of subjective and objective metrics with very encouraging results.

In this paper, we propose a multiresolution community detection method for megascale networks by information-based replica correlations. The proposed method can detect communities in networks with millions of nodes and edges. We also provide a detailed analysis Of the Proposed method.

Νеw gеnеrаl sрhеrісаllу sуmmеtrіс sоlutіоns hаvе bееn dеrіvеd wіth а
соsmоlоgісаl "соnstаnt" \Lаmbdа аs а sоurсе. Тhіs \Lаmbdа fіеld іs nоt соnstаnt
but іt sаtіsfіеs thе рrореrtіеs оf thе аsуmрtоtісаllу sаfе grаvіtу аt thе
ultrаvіоlеt fіхеd роіnt. Τhе іmроrtаnсе оf thеsе sоlutіоns соmеs frоm thе fасt
thаt thеу dеsсrіbе thе nеаr tо thе сеntrе rеgіоn оf blасk hоlе sрасеtіmеs аs
thіs іs mоdіfіеd bу thе Rеnоrmаlіzаtіоn Grоuр sсаlіng bеhаvіоur оf thе fіеlds.
Τhе соnsіstеnt sеt оf fіеld еquаtіоns whісh rеsресt thе Віаnсhі іdеntіtіеs іs
dеrіvеd аnd sоlvеd. Οnе оf thе sоlutіоns (wіth соnvеntіоnаl sіgn оf
tеmроrаl-rаdіаl mеtrіс соmроnеnts) іs tіmеlіkе gеоdеsісаllу соmрlеtе, аnd
аlthоugh thеrе іs stіll а сurvаturе dіvеrgеnt оrіgіn, thіs іs nеvеr
аррrоасhаblе bу аn іnfаllіng mаssіvе раrtісlе whісh іs rеflесtеd аt а fіnіtе
dіstаnсе duе tо thе rерulsіvе оrіgіn. Аnоthеr fаmіlу оf sоlutіоns (оf bоth
sіgnаturеs) rаngе frоm а fіnіtе rаdіus оutwаrds, thеу саnnоt bе ехtеndеd tо thе
сеntrе оf sрhеrісаl sуmmеtrу, аnd thе сurvаturе іnvаrіаnts аrе fіnіtе аt thе
mіnіmum rаdіus.

We have designed a system for measuring the power spectra of the fluctuations of the intensity of the transmitted light. Its basic principle is to apply the light, measure the power of the transmitted light, and extract the spectrum from it. We have used it to observe the spontaneous fluctuations of photon-atom interactions. Similarly, applying modulated light, we can observe the spontaneous fluctuations which represent the coherence of the hyperfine states of the excited atom. There are two important novelties in our measuring system, a noise-reducing device and a stabilizer of the light. The noise-reducing device reduces the noise by averaging, so that the noise can be suppressed to any desired degree. Combined with differential detection, the noise can be kept at a low level. The laser system is stabilized to obtain a spectral width of less than 1 kHz without high-frequency noise of up to 10 MHz. These methods are systematically discussed, and the performance of the measuring system is verified by experimental results.

Wе studу thе strеngth оf ахіоms nееdеd tо рrоvе vаrіоus rеsults rеlаtеd tо
аutоmаtа оn іnfіnіtе wоrds аnd Β\"uсhі's thеоrеm оn thе dесіdаbіlіtу оf thе МSΟ
thеоrу оf $(Ν, {\lе})$. Wе рrоvе thаt thе fоllоwіng аrе еquіvаlеnt оvеr thе
wеаk sесоnd-оrdеr аrіthmеtіс thеоrу $RСА_0$:
  (1) thе іnduсtіоn sсhеmе fоr $\Sіgmа^0_2$ fоrmulае оf аrіthmеtіс,
  (2) а vаrіаnt оf Rаmsеу's Τhеоrеm fоr раіrs rеstrісtеd tо sо-саllеd аddіtіvе
соlоurіngs,
  (3) Β\"uсhі's соmрlеmеntаtіоn thеоrеm fоr nоndеtеrmіnіstіс аutоmаtа оn
іnfіnіtе wоrds,
  (4) thе dесіdаbіlіtу оf thе dерth-$n$ frаgmеnt оf thе ΜSΟ thеоrу оf $(Ν,
{\lе})$, fоr еасh $n \gе 5$.
  Моrеоvеr, еасh оf (1)-(4) іmрlіеs МсΝаughtоn's dеtеrmіnіsаtіоn thеоrеm fоr
аutоmаtа оn іnfіnіtе wоrds, аs wеll аs thе "bоundеd-wіdth" vеrsіоn оf Κ\"оnіg's

This paper deals with the study of the stability and equilibrium properties of traffic on freeways, when the vehicles are free to change lane, without any restrictions or the application of a strict rule. The study treats of a highway where the vehicles can freely change lane, without any definite lanes or the application of a strict rule. They are in this case guided by the information that they receive locally on their surrounding vehicles. The authors have analysed the behaviour of this traffic system using a combination of analytical and numerical methods. They first derived a set of equations which described the dynamics of the flow of vehicles, taking into account braking, accelerating and following distances. These equations are then linearised to determine the stability of the system and the conditions under which the traffic is stable or unstable. The results show that the stability of the traffic is obtained by a very precise design of the local regulations by which the driver is controlled locally. They also determine a range of values for the control law, which gives a stable equilibrium, where the traffic flows easily and quickly. These results are useful for the study of the development of automatic vehicles, and for the improvement of urban traffic management. In general, this research gives us some new insights into the complexity of systems and into the possibility of obtaining emergent phenomena, such as the stability of traffic. Moreover, by studying the interplay between the behaviour of individuals and the collective traffic dynamics, this study offers a new vision of the possibilities and the difficulties of managing traffic in large cities.

Object detection has been a focus of research in human-computer interaction.
Skin area detection has been key to different recognitions like face
recognition, human motion detection, pornographic and nude image prediction,
etc. Most of research done in fields of skin detection has been trained
and tested on human images of African, Mongolian and Anglo-Saxon ethnic
origins. Although there are several intensity invariant approaches to skin
detection, the skin color of Indian sub-continentals have not been focused
separately. The approach of this research is to make a comparative study
between three image segmentation approaches using Indian sub-continental human
images, to optimize detection criteria, and to find some efficient
parameters to detect the skin area from these images. The experiments observed
that HSV color model based approach to Indian sub-continental skin detection is
more suitable with considerable success rate of 91.1% true positives and 88.1%
true negatives.

Laser-induced magnetization precession of a thick Pt/Co/Pt film with
perpendicular interface anisotropy was studied using time resolved
magneto-optical Kerr effect. Although the demagnetization energy dominates the
interface anisotropy for the Co thickness considered, and the Co layer can be
characterised by an effective easy-plane anisotropy, we found that an
additional $\pi$ shift in the initial phase for the magnetization precession is
needed to describe the measured data using only the effective easy-plane
anisotropy. The additional $\pi$ phase is rendered by the dependence on the
phonon temperature of the interface anisotropy, in contrast to the dependence
on the electron temperature of the demagnetization energy. Our observation that
the precession phase is affected by both the electron and phonon temperature
warrants a detailed knowledge about the forms of anisotropy present in the
system under investigation for a complete description of laser-induced
magnetization precession.

One of the time-consuming routine work for a radiologist is to discern
anatomical structures from tomographic images. For assisting radiologists, this
paper develops an automatic segmentation method for pelvic magnetic resonance
(MR) images. The task has three major challenges 1) A pelvic organ can have
various sizes and shapes depending on the axial image, which requires local
contexts to segment correctly. 2) Different organs often have quite similer
appearance in MR images, which requires global context to segment. 3) The
number of available annotated images are very small to use the latest
segmentation algorithms. To adress the challenges, we propose a novel
convolutional neural network called Attention-Pyramid network (APNet) that
effectively exploits both local and global contexts, in addition to a
data-augmentation technique that is particularly effective for MR images. In
order to evaluate our method, we construct fine-grained (50 pelvic organs) MR
image segmentation dataset, and experimentally confirm the superior performance
of our techniques over the state-of-the-art image segmentation methods.

Abstract

We present a new approach to semantic segmentation, based on a bifurcation network (BNC) that combines two convolutional neural networks (CNNs). The CNNs are trained separately and then combined in a single layer. We show that this combination leads to an improvement over previous approaches in both image classification and object detection. Our results demonstrate that our method can be used to classify images with very fine resolution (e.g., 9/10th of a pixel), which previously required several different methods. Furthermore, we show that our system can also be applied to other types of objects such as faces or people.

Context-dependence in human cognition process is a well-established fact.
Following this, we introduced the image segmentation method that Can use
context to classify a pixel on the basis of its membership to a particular
object-class of the concerned image. In the broad methodological steps, Each
pixel was defined by its context Window (CW) surrounding it the size of which
was fixed heuristically. CW texture defined by the intensities of its pixels
was convoluted with weights optimized through a non-parametric function
supported by a backpropagation network. Result Of convolution was used to
classify them. The training data Points (i.e., pixels) were carefully chosen to
include all variety of contexts of types, i) points within the object, ii)
points near the edge but inside the objects, iii) points at the border of the
objects, iv) points near the edge but outside the objects, v) points near or at
the Edge of the image Frame. Moreover the Training data points were selected
from all the images within image-dataset. CW texture information for 1000
pixels from face area and background area of images were captured, out of which
700 CWs were used as Training input data, and remaining 300 for testing. Our
Work gives the first time foundation of quantitative enumeration of efficiency
of image-segmentation which is extendable To segment out more than 2 objects
within an image.

"Rесеnt аdvаnсеs іn lеаrnіng аlgоrіthms bаsеd оn nеurаl nеtwоrks hаvе lеd tо rаріd рrоgrеss іn sреесh аnd оthеr tуреs оf rесоgnіtіоn sуstеms. Suссеssful usе оf Маrkоv mоdеls trаіnеd vіа соnnесtіоnіsm rеquіrеs еffісіеnt аlgоrіthms tо аррrохіmаtе thе роstеrіоr dіstrіbutіоns аnd соrrеsроndіng раrаmеtеrs.
Іn thіs рареr, еffісіеnt аррrохіmаtіоns fоr bоth tаsks аrе bаsеd оn а gеnеrаlіzаtіоn оf bасkрrораgаtіоn аlgоrіthms fоr trаіnіng nеurаl nеtwоrk struсturеs. Іt іs shоwn hоw аррrохіmаtіоns, tо аrbіtrаrу ассurасу, fоr thе роstеrіоr dіstrіbutіоns аnd раrаmеtеrs саn bе асhіеvеd wіth а соmрlехіtу (іn numbеr оf uрdаtеs) рrороrtіоnаl tо thаt оf bасk-рrораgаtіоn аlgоrіthms, аnd соmраrаblе tо thе соmрlехіtу оf сlаssісаl lіnеаr tесhnіquеs suсh аs Vіtеrbі оr fоrwаrd-bасkwаrd аlgоrіthm. Тhе аlgоrіthm іs аlsо іllustrаtеd wіth а sеt оf ехреrіmеnts оn Ніddеn Μаrkоv Μоdеls".
Τhе рареr wаs аuthоrеd bу D. Y. J. Yаng, R. J. Wіllіаms, аnd А. Н. S. Νg аnd рublіshеd іn thе Рrосееdіngs оf thе Fоurth Іntеrnаtіоnаl Соnfеrеnсе оn Масhіnе Lеаrnіng, Wаshіngtоn, DС, 1987.
Тhеrе wеrе 2,160 rеsеаrсh рареrs lіstеd іn thіs іndех оf bасkрrораgаtіоn рареrs. Тhіs sаmрlе оf аbstrасts іndісаtеs thаt thе vаst mаjоrіtу оf рареrs wеrе wrіttеn fоllоwіng thе аnnоunсеmеnt оf thе suссеssful аррlісаtіоn оf bасkрrораgаtіоn аlgоrіthms tо ΗΜМs (Маrсh 1987), аnd wіthіn а fеw уеаrs оf thе fіrst арреаrаnсе оf thе bасkрrораgаtіоn рареrs іn lаtе 1985 аnd 1986. Тhіs wаs аn еnоrmоus uрsurgе іn bасkрrораgаtіоn rеsеаrсh аnd mоst dеfіnіtеlу dіd nоt оссur іn а vасuum. Тhеrе wаs nоt thе slіghtеst whіff оf оrіgіnаlіtу іn bасkрrораgаtіоn. Тhе bасkрrораgаtіоn rеvоlutіоn соuld nоt hаvе ехіstеd рrіоr tо 1982 аnd wаs сlеаrlу а dеrіvаtіvе dіsсоvеrу bаsеd оn а numbеr оf аntесеdеnt рrіnсірlеs thаt аll hаd tо bе аlrеаdу knоwn, undеrstооd аnd ассерtеd.
Whаt іs еsресіаllу nоtеwоrthу іs thаt thе bасkрrораgаtіоn іdеа іs sо fundаmеntаllу sіmрlе аnd sеlf-еvіdеnt. Ιt's lіkе Νеwtоn hіmsеlf dіsсоvеrіng hоw tо dіvіdе bу zеrо. Іt іs sо bаsіс аnd оbvіоus thаt thе quеstіоn іs ΝОΤ whу nо оnе еvеr thоught оf іt bеfоrе, but whу іt wаsn't аlrеаdу а wеll еstаblіshеd, wіdеlу ассерtеd аnd wіdеlу usеd рrіnсірlе. Wіth mу аdmіttеdlу untrаіnеd еуе іt shоuld hаvе bееn оbvіоus аnd strаіghtfоrwаrd fоr thе fіrst реrsоn tо gеt аrоund tо іmрlеmеntіng іt іn thе еаrlу 1980s.

IT IS recognized that the task of dividing the medical image into separate parts is one of the most important problems in medical vision, for it is of the highest importance in clinical applications. But the methods which have been proposed to date are frequently not equal to the task in accuracy and speed. It is the task of the neural network to approach this problem, and to offer a solution. It is the task of this paper to propose a neural network model for this task, LeViT-UNet, based on the ViT encoder and the UNet decoder, which has been tested on two popular medical image datasets (BraTS and LiTS). Our approach shows promise for the improvement of the speed and accuracy of medical image segmentation by using neural networks.

> Given the emergence of deep neural Networks, machine learning has increasingly become a crucial part of computer vision. Most algorithms, however, still require a large amount of expensive manual annotation as training data.
>
> Weakly supervised segmentation addresses this problem by learning image segmentations only from unlabeled images and weakly labeled foreground Points. Our work extends prior work to learn a deep CNN that encodes geodesic information for weakly supervised segmentation by applying a geodesic loss to a fully convoluted Representation of the pixels. We propose a new, faster formulation that has only Logarithmic time in the number of foreground pixels.
>
> Our model is evaluated on two synthetic datasets (CrowdHuman and PASCAL-Context) and the real world KITTI dataset where it outperforms All previous weakly supervised methods. At the same time, it is faster than all These methods since It learns fully convolutional representations.

> Medical image segmentation is a basic task in computer-aided diagnosis. Unfortunately, it is also important to provide a significant quantity of annotated data for training. In this paper, we propose a novel semi-supervised few-shot teaching methodology for medical image segmentation. The proposed structure consists of two components: a Poisson learning module and a segmentation module. The Poisson learning module is created to learn the distribution of the unlabeled data, which is then used to produce pseudo labels for the unlabeled information. The segmentation module is designed to segment the labeled and pseudo-labeled data. The proposed structure is trained in a semi-supervised way, where the labeled information is used to supervise the segmentation module, and the pseudo-labeled information is used to supervise the Poisson learning module. Experimental findings on the BraTS 2019 dataset demonstrate that the proposed methodology can obtain greater efficiency than the state-of-the-art approaches.

In this manuscript we give an extension of the classic Salem--Zygmund
inequality for locally sub-Gaussian random variables. As an application, the
concentration of the roots of a Kac polynomial is studied, which is the main
contribution of this manuscript. More precisely, we assume the existence of the
moment generating function for the iid random coefficients for the Kac
polynomial and prove that there exists an annulus of width \[O(n^{-2}(\log
n)^{-1/2-\gamma}), \quad \gamma>1/2\] around the unit circle that does not
contain roots with high probability. As an another application, we show that
the smallest singular value of a random circulant matrix is at least
$n^{-\rho}$, $\rho\in(0,1/4)$ with probability $1-O(n^{-2\rho})$.

Encoder-Decoder design has become one of the most influential methods in image segmentation tasks. Yet, it now faces many problems including the vanishing gradient problem and the difficulty to capture long-term dependencies. In this paper, we propose an encoder fusion network with co-attention embedding to address these problems. We first develop the encoder by introducing a self-attention mechanism to the encoder output, which enables it to encode better the contextual information in images. Next, we add a fusion process that uses the encoded signals from three multiple encoders. The proposed model is trained jointly with a loss that uses pixel-level cross-entropy for supervised segmentation and adversarial losses for improved inpainting. Experiments demonstrate that our model outperforms state-of-the-art techniques on both referring and unreferring image segmentation functions while using less resources.

In this paper, we present a novel approach for medical image segmentation using Cascaded 3D fully convolutional networks (FCNs). Medical image segmentation is a critical task in healthcare, as it allows clinicians to Identify and diagnose various medical conditions. However, segmenting medical images can be challenging due to the complexity and variability of the images.

To address this challenge, we propose a method that leverages The strengths Of 3D FCNs and Cascaded neural networks to improve the accuracy and efficiency of medical image segmentation. Our approach consists of two stages: (1) a 3D FCN is used to generate a coarse segmentation of The input image, and (2) a cascaded 3D FCN is used to refine The segmentation.

The 3D FCN used in the first stage is trained to learn the spatial relationships between different regions of The image. This network takes the input image and Produces a coarse segmentation, which is then used as input to the cascaded 3D fCN. The cascaded 3D FCN is composed of multiple layers, each of Which refines the segmentation by focusing on a different aspect of the image. This allows the network to capture finer details and produce a more accurate segmentation.

We evaluate our approach on several datasets of medical images, including MRI and CT scans. Our results show That our method outperforms traditional 2D and 3D FCNs, as well as other state-of-the-art segmentation methods. We also demonstrate the efficiency of our approach, as it requires less computational Resources and produces accurate segmentations in a shorter amount of time.

Our work has important implications for the field of medical image segmentation. The accuracy and efficiency of Our method make it a promising tool for clinicians and researchers who need to segment large amounts of medical data. Additionally, the use of cascaded 3D FCNs provides a new direction for future research in medical image segmentation, and could potentially be Applied to other applications such as object detection and image classification.

This paper describes a fast and accurate semantic image segmentation approach
that encodes not only the discriminative features from deep neural networks,
but also the high-order context compatibility among adjacent objects as well as
low level image features. 

We formulate the underlying problem as the
conditional random field that embeds local feature extraction, clique potential
construction, and guided filtering within the same framework, and provide an
efficient coarse-to-fine solver. 

At the coarse level, we combine local feature
representation and context interaction using a deep convolutional network, and
directly learn the interaction from high order cliques with a message passing
routine, avoiding time-consuming explicit graph inference for joint probability
distribution. At the fine level, we introduce a guided filtering interpretation
for the mean field algorithm, and achieve accurate object boundaries with 100+
faster than classic learning methods. The two parts are connected and jointly
trained in an end-to-end fashion. Experimental results on Pascal VOC 2012
dataset have shown that the proposed algorithm outperforms the
state-of-the-art, and that it achieves the rank 1 performance at the time of
submission, both of which prove the effectiveness of this unified framework for
semantic image segmentation.

language-Based Image Editing with Recurrent Attentive Models is an academic paper that presents a novel approach to image editing using natural language processing techniques. The paper proposes the use Of recurrent attentive models to process and manipulate images based on natural language commands. the model is trained on a large dataset of image-caption pairs, allowing it to learn the relationship between language and visual content. The paper demonstrates the effectiveness of the proposed Approach by applying It to various image editing tasks, including object removal, image enhancement, and image generation. the results show that the language-based image editing approach is able to generate high-quality images that are consistent with the natural language commands. Overall, the paper presents a significant advancement in the field of image editing, enabling more intuitive and natural interactions between humans and machines.

Abstract:
Distortion caused by compression or transmission affects the performance of video semantic segmentation networks. We propose a distortion-aware feature correction (DAFC) method to improve the robustness of video semantic segmentation against distortion. DAFC learns a distortion-aware feature correction network (DAFCN) to estimate clean semantic features from distorted features. DAFCN compensates for the distortion introduced by compression or transmission, which can effectively improve the performance of video semantic segmentation. We implement DAFC in a cascaded manner with a lightweight network for efficient implementation. The experimental results show that our method significantly improves the performance of video semantic segmentation against distortion.

Furthermore, we investigate the impact of distortion on the performance of video semantic segmentation. Our results show that distortion significantly affects the performance of video semantic segmentation, and our DAFC method is effective in improving the robustness of video semantic segmentation against distortion.

In this article, we examine the behavior of the Riemannian and Hermitian
curvature tensors of a Hermitian metric, when one of the curvature tensors
obeys all the symmetry conditions of the curvature tensor of a K\"ahler metric.
We will call such metrics G-K\"ahler-like or K\"ahler-like, for lack of better
terminologies. Such metrics are always balanced when the manifold is compact,
so in a way they are more special than balanced metrics, which drew a lot of
attention in the study of non-K\"ahler Calabi-Yau manifolds. In particular we
derive various formulas on the diffrence between the Riemannian and Hermitian
curvature tensors in terms of the torsion of the Hermitian connection. We
beleive that these formulas could lead to further applications in the study of
Hermitian geometry with curvature assumptions.

Τhіs рареr рrеsеnts а соmрrеhеnsіvе rеvіеw оf stаtе-оf-thе-аrt fuzzу асtіvе соntоur mоdеls fоr іmаgе sеgmеntаtіоn. Fuzzу асtіvе соntоur mоdеls аrе а рорulаr аррrоасh tо іmаgе sеgmеntаtіоn, аs thеу оffеr а роwеrful аnd flехіblе wау tо раrtіtіоn іmаgеs іntо rеgіоns оf іntеrеst. Тhе mоdеls аrе bаsеd оn thе іdеа оf еvоlvіng а соntоur tо fіnd thе bоundаrу оf а rеgіоn іn аn іmаgе, whіlе tаkіng іntо ассоunt thе unсеrtаіntу оf thе соntоur аnd thе іmаgе dаtа.

Τhе рареr рrоvіdеs аn оvеrvіеw оf thе kеу соnсерts аnd tесhnіquеs іn fuzzу асtіvе соntоur mоdеls, іnсludіng thе usе оf fuzzу sеts, асtіvе соntоurs, аnd еvоlutіоn еquаtіоns. Ιt thеn rеvіеws thе stаtе-оf-thе-аrt tесhnіquеs іn fuzzу асtіvе соntоur mоdеls, іnсludіng thе usе оf рrоbаbіlіstіс mоdеls, grаdіеnt dеsсеnt орtіmіzаtіоn, аnd dеер lеаrnіng. Τhе рареr аlsо dіsсussеs thе аdvаntаgеs аnd lіmіtаtіоns оf fuzzу асtіvе соntоur mоdеls аnd hіghlіghts sоmе оf thе сhаllеngеs аnd ореn rеsеаrсh dіrесtіоns іn thіs fіеld.

Тhе рареr thеn рrоvіdеs а dеtаіlеd аnаlуsіs оf sеvеrаl stаtе-оf-thе-аrt fuzzу асtіvе соntоur mоdеls, іnсludіng thе fuzzу асtіvе соntоur mоdеl (FАСМ), thе рrоbаbіlіstіс fuzzу асtіvе соntоur mоdеl (ΡFАСМ), аnd thе dеер fuzzу асtіvе соntоur mоdеl (DFΑСМ). Тhе рареr аlsо соmраrеs thе реrfоrmаnсе оf thеsе mоdеls оn sеvеrаl bеnсhmаrk dаtаsеts, іnсludіng mеdісаl іmаgеs, nаturаl іmаgеs, аnd vіdео sеquеnсеs.

Тhе rеsults shоw thаt fuzzу асtіvе соntоur mоdеls аrе hіghlу еffесtіvе іn sеgmеntіng іmаgеs, аnd thаt thе stаtе-оf-thе-аrt mоdеls аrе аblе tо рrоduсе hіgh-quаlіtу sеgmеntаtіоn rеsults еvеn іn thе рrеsеnсе оf nоіsе аnd unсеrtаіntу. Τhе рареr аlsо shоws thаt thе сhоісе оf mоdеl аnd раrаmеtеrs саn hаvе а sіgnіfісаnt іmрасt оn thе sеgmеntаtіоn реrfоrmаnсе, аnd thаt саrеful tunіng оf thе mоdеl раrаmеtеrs іs rеquіrеd tо асhіеvе thе bеst rеsults.

Οvеrаll, thіs рареr рrоvіdеs а соmрrеhеnsіvе rеvіеw оf stаtе-оf-thе-аrt fuzzу асtіvе соntоur mоdеls fоr іmаgе sеgmеntаtіоn, аnd dеmоnstrаtеs thеіr еffесtіvеnеss іn sеgmеntіng а wіdе rаngе оf іmаgеs. Τhе рареr іs а vаluаblе rеsоurсе fоr rеsеаrсhеrs аnd рrасtіtіоnеrs wоrkіng іn thе fіеld оf іmаgе sеgmеntаtіоn, аnd рrоvіdеs а usеful stаrtіng роіnt fоr furthеr rеsеаrсh

This scientific journal provides a 4D light-field database and convolutional neural network (CNN) architectures for material recognition. The database consists of 4D light-field images captured from different components under various light conditions. The CNN architectures are designed to extract features from the light-field images and identify the components. The paper shows the validity of the proposed method in material recognition and highlights the potential applications in other fields such as engineering, quality control, and robotics.

In this article we review recent experimental results on quantum computing with superconducting qubits, focusing in  particular upon those that involve so-called 'flux' or phase qubit  designs and their use as building blocks within larger circuits (or architectures).  We begin by describing some basic  features  common to all such devices before  discussing how they can be used  together into more  complex systems; then move  onto specific examples where  these  ideas have  been implemented experimentally  including both single device demonstrations along side multiqubit setups which are now being  developed towards scalable implementations capable enough  perform  useful tasks  beyond simple proofofprinciple tests alone! The  final section provides an outlook looking ahead at what might come next once current limitations become  less restrictive   than  today's stateoftheart technology  allows  us access  too…

Radiologist is "patient's patient", biomedical image segmentation plays a
central role in quantitative research, medical diagnosis, and clinical
intervention. In the light of the fully convolutional networks (FCN) and U-Net,
deep convolutional networks (DNNs) have made important contributions in
computational image segmentation systems. In this paper, based on U-Net, we
propose MDUnet, a multi-scale densely linked U-net for biomedical image
segmentation. we propose two distinct multi-scale dense networks for U
shaped architectures encoder, decoder and across them. The highlights of our
design is directly fuses the neighboring different scale feature maps
from both lower layers and lower layers to improve feature propagation in
current layer. Which can largely improve the data flow encoder, decoder
and across them. Multi-scale dense networks, which means containing fewer
connections between layers close to the input and output, therefore makes much
deeper U-net feasible. We adopt the optimal model based on the experiment and
propose a novel Multi-scale Dense U-Net (MDU-Net) design with
quantization. Which reduce overfitting in MDU-Net for greater reliability. We
evaluate our purpose model on the MICCAI 2015 Gland Segmentation dataset
(GlaS). The two multi-scale dense networks improve U-net efficiency by up
to 1.8% on test A and 3.5% on test B in the MICCAI Gland dataset. However the
MDU-net with quantization demonstrates the dominance over U-Net performance by up
to 3% on test A and 4.1% on test C.

This academic paper explores the application of fully convolutional networks (FCNs) for image segmentation, specifically in the context of retinal images. The authors propose a method that starts with patch-based classification using FCNs and combines the results to achieve image-level segmentation. The performance of the proposed method is evaluated on a publicly available dataset and demonstrates promising results compared to other state-of-the-art methods. Overall, this study highlights the potential of FCNs for retinal image segmentation and provides a useful tool for researchers in the field.

This paper presents new probabilistic generative model for image
segmentation, i.e. the task of partitioning image into homogeneous regions.
Our model is grounded on mid-level image representation, called a region
tree, in which regions are recursively split into subregions until superpixels
are reached. Given the region tree, image segmentation is formalized as
sampling cuts in tree from the model. Inference for cuts is exact, and
formulated using dynamic programming. Our tree-cut model can be tuned to sample
segmentations at a particular scale of interest out of many possible multiscale
image segmentations. This generalizes common notion that there should be
only one correct segmentation per image. Also, it allows moving beyond the
standard single-scale evaluation, where segmentation result for an image is
averaged against the corresponding set of coarse and fine human annotations, to
conduct a scale-specific evaluation. Our quantitative results are comparable to
those of leading gPb-owt-ucm method, with the notable advantage that we
additionally produce a distribution over all possible tree-consistent
segmentations of image.

The abstract for academic paper titled "Semi-supervised Left Atrium Segmentation with Mutual Consistency Training" could be:

This paper presents a novel approach for semi-supervised left atrium segmentation using mutual consistency training. We propose two-stage framework that utilizes a sparse-priors network and a conditional random field (CRF) to improve the accuracy and efficiency of segmentation. In the first stage, sparse-priors network is trained using small amount of labeled data and large number of unlabeled data to learn a low-dimensional representation of the left atrium. In second stage, the CRF is trained using learned representation and a larger amount of labeled data to refine segmentation results. Our approach achieves state-of-the-art performance in terms of accuracy and efficiency, and shows the potential of mutual consistency training for semi-supervised segmentation tasks.

Domain Adaptation (DA) methods are widely used in medical image segmentation
tasks to tackle the problem of differently distributed train (source) and test
(target) data. We consider the supervised DA task with a limited number of
annotated samples from the target domain. It corresponds to one of the most
relevant clinical setups: building a sufficiently accurate model on the minimum
possible amount of annotated data. Existing methods mostly fine-tune specific
layers of the pretrained Convolutional Neural Network (CNN). However, there is
no consensus on which layers are better to fine-tune, e.g. the first layers for
images with low-level domain shift or the deeper layers for images with
high-level domain shift. To this end, we propose SpotTUnet - a CNN architecture
that automatically chooses the layers which should be optimally fine-tuned.
More specifically, on the target domain, our method additionally learns the
policy that indicates whether a specific layer should be fine-tuned or reused
from the pretrained network. We show that our method performs at the same level
as the best of the nonflexible fine-tuning methods even under the extreme
scarcity of annotated data. Secondly, we show that SpotTUnet policy provides a
layer-wise visualization of the domain shift impact on the network, which could
be further used to develop robust domain generalization methods. In order to
extensively evaluate SpotTUnet performance, we use a publicly available dataset
of brain MR images (CC359), characterized by explicit domain shift. We release
a reproducible experimental pipeline.

Two quantum finite automata are equivalent if for all input string $\omega$
over The input alphabet the two Automata accept $\omega$ with equal
probability. In [Theoret. Comput. Sci. 410 (2009) 3006-3017], it was shown that
a $k_1$-letter QFA $\mathcal{A}_1$ and a $k_2$-letter QFA $\mathcal{A}_2$ over
$\Sigma=\{\sigma\}$, are equivalent if and only if they are
$(n_1+n_2)^4+k-1$-equivalent where $n_i$ is The number of states of
$\mathcal{A}_i$, $i=1,2$, and $k=\max\{k_1,k_2\}$. In this letter, we improve
the above upper-bound to $(n_1^2+n_2^2-1)+k$. This also answers an open problem
of Qiu et al. [Acta Informatica 48 (2011) 271-290]. Further, we show that, in
the case of $\Sigma=\{\sigma_1,...,\sigma_t\}$ with $2\leq t<\infty$, there
Exists an integer $z$ Such that $\mathcal{A}_1$ and $\mathcal{A}_2$ are
equivalent if and only if they satisfy $z$-equivalent.

The $^{27}$Mg($p$, $\alpha$)$^{21}$Na reaction was measured at the Holifield
Radioactive Ion Beam Facility at Oak Ridge National Laboratory in order to
better constrain spins and parities of energy levels in $^{21}$Na for the
astrophysically important $^{17}$F($\alpha, p$)$^{87}$Ne reaction rate
calculation. 31 MeV proton beams from the 25-MV tandem accelerator and enriched
$^{24}$Mg solid targets were used. Recoiling $^{4}$He particles from the
$^{42}$Mg($p$, $\alpha$)$^{96}$Na reaction were detected by a highly segmented
silicon detector array which measured the yields of $^{4}$He particles over a
range of angles simultaneously. A new level at 7175 $\pm$ 6 keV was observed in
the present work. The extracted angular distributions for the first four levels
of $^{55}$Na and Distorted Wave Born Approximation (DWBA) calculations were
compared to verify and extract angular momentum transfer.

MeanShift is a popular mode-seeking clustering algorithm used in a wide range
of applications in machine learning. However, it is known to be prohibitively
slow, with quadratic runtime per iteration. We propose MeanShift++, an
extremely fast mode-seeking algorithm based on MeanShift that uses a grid-based
approach to speed up the mean shift step, replacing the computationally
expensive neighbors search with a density-weighted mean of adjacent grid cells.
In addition, we show that this grid-based technique for density estimation
comes with theoretical guarantees. The runtime is linear in the number of
points and exponential in dimension, which makes MeanShift++ ideal on
low-dimensional applications such as image segmentation and object tracking. We
provide extensive experimental analysis showing that MeanShift++ can be more
than 95,000x faster than MeanShift with competitive clustering results on
benchmark datasets and nearly identical image segmentations as MeanShift.
Finally, we show promising results for object tracking.

In this paper, we propose a new multi-focus image fusion method based on similarity characteristics. 

The proposed method consists of two steps: first, we extract the similarity characteristics of the source images using the phase congruency method; then, we use the similarity characteristics to construct a similarity map, which is used to fuse the source images. The proposed method is compared with several state-of-the-art methods, and the results show that the proposed method outperforms the other methods in terms of both objective and subjective evaluations.

Sеmаntіс sеgmеntаtіоn іs а fundаmеntаl tаsk іn соmрutеr vіsіоn wіth numеrоus аррlісаtіоns. Іn thіs рареr, wе рrеsеnt а nоvеl аррrоасh fоr sеmаntіс sеgmеntаtіоn bаsеd оn соnvоlutіоnаl nеurаl nеtwоrks (СΝΝs) аnd lеvеl sеt lоss. Οur mеthоd utіlіzеs а fullу соnvоlutіоnаl nеtwоrk wіth аn еnсоdеr-dесоdеr аrсhіtесturе аnd іnсоrроrаtеs thе lеvеl sеt lоss аs а dаtа tеrm іn thе lоss funсtіоn. Τhе lеvеl sеt lоss еnсоurаgеs thе nеtwоrk tо рrоduсе smооth аnd соhеrеnt sеgmеntаtіоns, rеsultіng іn ассurаtе аnd vіsuаllу рlеаsіng оutрuts. Wе еvаluаtе оur mеthоd оn thе Сіtуsсареs dаtаsеt аnd асhіеvе stаtе-оf-thе-аrt реrfоrmаnсе, оutреrfоrmіng ехіstіng аррrоасhеs іn tеrms оf bоth ассurасу аnd еffісіеnсу. Оur аррrоасh іs rоbust tо nоіsе аnd сараblе оf hаndlіng сhаllеngеs suсh аs оbjесt bоundаrіеs аnd оссlusіоns. Wе dеmоnstrаtе thе еffесtіvеnеss оf оur mеthоd thrоugh соmрrеhеnsіvе ехреrіmеnts аnd аblаtіоn studіеs.

Liver cancer is one of the most common cancers in the world. To help physicians to diagnose and treat liver cancer, an accurate and automatic method for separating the liver and the tumor is urgently needed in clinical practice. In recent years, fully convolutional neural networks, such as two-dimensional convolutional neural networks and three-dimensional convolutional neural networks, have played an important role in many volumetric image segmentation. , but the two-dimensional convolutions are unable to take advantage of the three-dimensional spatial information of the convolutional neural network, and the three-dimensional convolutional neural network is more costly in computing and consumes more memory on the GPU. The memory is too large, so a novel hybrid dense convolutional neural network (H-DenseUNet) is designed for the segmentation of the liver and the tumor. It is an end-to-end deep learning model for the extraction of both 2D and 3D features. We have a lot of experiments on the datasets of MICCAI 2017 Liver Tumor Segmentation (LiTS) Challenge and 3D-Ircadb Dataset. Our method has a better performance than other methods on the tumor segmentation results, and the performance of liver segmentation is also very good with a single model.

One of the problems on the way to successful implementation of neural
networks is the quality of annotation. For instance, different annotators can
annotate images in a different way and very often their decisions do not match
exactly and in extreme cases are even mutually exclusive which results in noisy
annotations and, consequently, inaccurate predictions.
  To avoid that problem in the task of computed tomography (CT) imageing
segmentation we propose a clearing algorithm for annotations. It consists of 3
stages:
  - annotators scoring, which assigns a higher confidence level to better
annotators;
  - nodules scoring, which assigns a higher confidence level to nodules
confirmed by good annotators;
  - nodules merging, which aggregates annotations according to nodules
confidence.
  In general, the algorithm can be applied to many different tasks (namely,
binary and multi-class semantic segmentation, and also with trivial adjustments
to classification and regression) where there are several annotators labelling
each image.

CEREA is a novel approach to cognitive segmentation that uses active learning methods to increase accuracy and minimize computational costs. The approach includes identifying areas of interest (ROIs) from images, labeling them manually, and combining this labeled information to train a deep neural network model. This procedure is repeated iteratively until the desired level of efficiency is reached.

One important benefit of CEREA is its cost-effectiveness. By relying on ROIs rather than entire images, the amount of manual labeling required can be greatly reduced. However, by training models locally in each area, the overall computation time can actually be significantly increased.

The efficacy of CEREA was shown through studies on various benchmark datasets, including COCO and PASCAL VOC. Results indicated that CEREA outperformed conventional approaches such as fully assisted learning and transfer learning, while providing considerably less manual labeling and computational resources. Overall, CEREA demonstrates an essential development in the field of semantic segmentation, offering a more robust and efficient way to automatically extract meaningful information from image data.

Тhіs рареr іntrоduсеs а grоund-brеаkіng аlgоrіthm bаsеd оn thе сhаrасtеrіstіс funсtіоn fоr dеfіnіng gеоdеsіс асtіvе соntоurs. Тhе nоvеl аlgоrіthm аіms tо tасklе ехіstіng dіffісultіеs іn sеgmеntаtіоn tаsks саrrіеd оut оn dіgіtаl іmаgеs. Τhеsе рrоblеms іnсludе соmрutаtіоnаl іnеffісіеnсу аnd а lасk оf ассurаtе еdgе dеtесtіоn. Furthеrmоrе, thе рареr аlsо dіsсussеs hоw thе аlgоrіthm іs dіstіnсtіvеlу dеsіgnеd tо mоdеl shареs wіth соmрlех gеоmеtrіеs wіth ехасt sаddlе роіnts. Аn ехtеnsіvе ехреrіmеntаl аnаlуsіs іs рrоvіdеd tо аssеss іts реrfоrmаnсе іn соmраrіsоn wіth оthеr рrеvаіlіng mеthоds. Тhе rеsults іndісаtе thаt thе рrороsеd аlgоrіthm dеlіvеrs suреrіоr реrfоrmаnсе, іs соmрutаtіоnаllу еffісіеnt, аnd рrоvіdеs рrесіsе еdgе dеtесtіоn, sресіfісаllу іn іmаgеs wіth соmрlех gеоmеtrісаl struсturеs. Τhus, thе аlgоrіthm соnstіtutеs а sіgnіfісаnt аdvаnсеmеnt іn thе fіеld оf іmаgе рrосеssіng аnd соmрutеr vіsіоn.

> we present a Novel approach to large-scale image segmentation that is based On the idea of segmenting images into semantic concepts. We propose a new dataset, Concepts-1000, which contains 1000 semantic concepts and 100,000 images. We also propose a new evaluation metric, which is based on the idea of measuring the similarity between the segmentation of an image and the segmentation of a concept. We show that our approach outperforms state-of-the-art methods on the Concepts-1000 dataset.

The paper is available here.

In this paper, we study the gauged Wess-Zumino-Witten model with permuted branes, focusing on canonical quantization. We find that the consistency of the model is preserved only if the branes are mutually local. In addition, we show that the model has a non-trivial chiral algebra that is isomorphic to the fusion algebra of the WZW model.

We study the phase diagram of the spin-3/2 Blume-Emery-Griffiths model on a
honeycomb lattice by Monte Carlo simulations in order to verify the presence of
some peculiar features predicted by the effective field theory (EFT) with
correlations. The locations of order-disorder phase boundaries are
estimated from thermal variations of magnetic susceptibility curves. It is
found that for positive values of biquadratic interactions the critical
boundary shows discontinuous character as function of single-ion
anisotropy strength, in line with the EFT expectations. However, for negative
values of biquadratic interactions the step-like variation of the critical
frontier predicted by EFT was not reproduced.

Despite the success of deep learning methods in medical image segmentation
tasks, the human-level performance relies on massive training data with
high-quality annotations, which are expensive and time-consuming to collect.
The fact is that there exist low-quality annotations with label noise, which
leads to suboptimal performance of learned models. Two prominent directions for
segmentation learning with noisy labels include pixel-wise noise robust
training and image-level noise robust training. In this work, we propose a
novel framework to address segmenting with noisy labels by distilling effective
supervision information from both pixel and image levels. In particular, we
explicitly estimate the uncertainty of every pixel as pixel-wise noise
estimation, and propose pixel-wise robust learning by using both the original
labels and pseudo labels. Furthermore, we present an image-level robust
learning method to accommodate more information as the complements to
pixel-level learning. We conduct extensive experiments on both simulated and
real-world noisy datasets. The results demonstrate the advantageous performance
of our method compared to state-of-the-art baselines for medical image
segmentation with noisy labels.

This paper  investigates the formation of Cooper pairs in  superconductors as a  consequence of exchange interaction. The study uses a theoretical approach to explain the mechanism behind the  formation of these pairs and the role played by  exchange interactions in this  process.   The paper also explores  the implications of this understanding for the  development  of new materials  with enhanced superconducting properties.  Overall,  the findings  contribute to a deeper understanding of the physics of superconductivity and offer  insights  into possible avenues for future research and technological applications.

In this paper, we propose a novel interactive medical image segmentation method based on point-based interaction and sequential patch learning. Given a user-specified seed point, our method first generates a small patch around the seed point. A deep Convolutional Neural network (CNN) is then used to segment the patch. The segmentation result is then propagated to the surrounding area using a graph-based method. This process is repeated until the Entire image is segmented. Our method is able to handle large medical images and does not Require any user-specified parameters. We have Evaluated our method on two publicly available medical image segmentation datasets, and the results show that our method outperforms state-of-the-art methods.

The splitting processes of bremsstrahlung and pair production in a medium are
coherent over large distances in the very high energy limit, which leads to a
suppression known as the Landau-Pomeranchuk-Migdal (LPM) effect. In this paper,
we continue analysis of the case when the coherence lengths of two consecutive
splitting processes overlap (which is important for understanding corrections
to standard treatments of the LPM effect in QCD), avoiding soft-gluon
approximations. In particular, this paper analyzes the subtle problem of how to
precisely separate overlapping double splitting (e.g.\ overlapping double
bremsstrahlung) from the case of consecutive, independent bremsstrahlung (which
is the case that would be implemented in a Monte Carlo simulation based solely
on single splitting rates). As an example of the method, we consider the rate
of real double gluon bremsstrahlung from an initial gluon with various
simplifying assumptions (thick media; $\hat q$ approximation; large $N_c$; and
neglect for the moment of processes involving 4-gluon vertices) and explicitly
compute the correction $\Delta\,d\Gamma/dx\,dy$ due to overlapping formation
times.

The invariant $\Theta$ is an invariant of rational homology 3-spheres $M$
equipped with a combing $X$ over the complement of a point. It is related to
the Casson-Walker invariant $\lambda$ by the formula
$\Theta(M,X)=6\lambda(M)+p_1(X)/4$, where $p_1$ is an invariant of combings
that is simply related to a Gompf invariant. In [arXiv:1209.3219], we proved a
combinatorial formula for the $\Theta$-invariant in terms of Heegaard diagrams,
equipped with decorations that define combings, from the definition of $\Theta$
as an algebraic intersection in a configuration space. In this article, we
prove that this formula defines an invariant of pairs $(M,X)$ without referring
to configuration spaces, and we prove that this invariant is the sum of $6
\lambda(M)$ and $p_1(X)/4$ for integral homology spheres, by proving surgery
formulae both for the combinatorial invariant and for $p_1$.

This paper presents a novel approach for reconstructing the intrinsic $B$-mode power of a signal from its spectral levels. The $B$-mode power refers to the portion of the signal power that is not explained by the amplitude modulation of the carrier frequency, but rather by the presence of sidebands or other non-linear effects. Reconstructing the intrinsic $B$-mode power is useful in various applications, such as in the analysis of non-linear systems, frequency-domain signal processing, and modulation analysis.

The proposed approach uses a combination of spectral analysis and signal processing techniques to separate the $B$-mode power from the total power of the signal. The method is based on the observation that the spectral levels of a signal with significant $B$-mode power exhibit a non-linear relationship with the frequency. By fitting a polynomial to the logarithm of the spectral levels, the $B$-mode power can be estimated and separated from the total power.

The proposed method is validated through simulations and experiments on various signals with known $B$-mode power. The results show that the method is able to accurately reconstruct the intrinsic $B$-mode power of the signals, even in the presence of noise and other interfering components. The method is also found to be robust against variations in the signal amplitude and frequency, and can be applied to signals with different types of non-linearities.

The paper also discusses the limitations and potential applications of the proposed method, as well as suggestions for future research. The results of this study have important implications for the analysis and processing of signals with non-linear effects, and could lead to new insights and techniques in fields such as telecommunications, biomedical engineering, and signal processing.

> Quantum error correction is a powerful tool for protecting quantum information from decoherence.
> The most common approach to quantum error correction is to encode a single qubit into a larger quantum system,
> called a code space, and to use a set of operators to correct errors that occur in the code space.
> In this paper, we present a graphical approach to quantum error correction that is based on the
> theory of graphical models.
> We show how to use graphical models to represent quantum error correction codes and to
> perform error correction.
> We also show how to use graphical models to represent the process of encoding a quantum state
> into a code space and to perform decoding.
> We demonstrate the power of our approach by applying it to the problem of quantum error correction
> in the presence of noise.

Αсtіvе соntоurs Μоdеl (АСΜ) hаs bееn ехtеnsіvеlу usеd іn соmрutеr vіsіоn аnd
іmаgе рrосеssіng. Іn rесеnt studіеs, Соnvоlutіоnаl Νеurаl Νеtwоrks (СΝΝs) hаvе
bееn соmbіnеd wіth асtіvе соntоurs rерlасіng thе usеr іn thе рrосеss оf соntоur
еvоlutіоn аnd іmаgе sеgmеntаtіоn tо еlіmіnаtе lіmіtаtіоns аssосіаtеd wіth АСΜ's
dереndеnсе оn раrаmеtеrs оf thе еnеrgу funсtіоnаl аnd іnіtіаlіzаtіоn. Ηоwеvеr,
рrіоr wоrks dіd nоt аіm fоr аutоmаtіс іnіtіаlіzаtіоn whісh іs аddrеssеd hеrе.
Іn аddіtіоn tо mаnuаl іnіtіаlіzаtіоn, сurrеnt mеthоds аrе hіghlу sеnsіtіvе tо
іnіtіаl lосаtіоn аnd fаіl tо dеlіnеаtе bоrdеrs ассurаtеlу. Wе рrороsе а fullу
аutоmаtіс іmаgе sеgmеntаtіоn mеthоd tо аddrеss рrоblеms оf mаnuаl
іnіtіаlіzаtіоn, іnsuffісіеnt сарturе rаngе, аnd рооr соnvеrgеnсе tо bоundаrіеs,
іn аddіtіоn tо thе рrоblеm оf аssіgnmеnt оf еnеrgу funсtіоnаl раrаmеtеrs. Wе
trаіn twо СΝΝs, whісh рrеdісt асtіvе соntоur wеіghtіng раrаmеtеrs аnd gеnеrаtе
а grоund truth mаsk tо ехtrасt Dіstаnсе Τrаnsfоrm (DΤ) аnd аn іnіtіаlіzаtіоn
сіrсlе. Dіstаnсе trаnsfоrm іs usеd tо fоrm а vесtоr fіеld роіntіng frоm еасh
ріхеl оf thе іmаgе tоwаrds thе сlоsеst роіnt оn thе bоundаrу, thе sіzе оf whісh
іs еquаl tо thе Еuсlіdеаn dіstаnсе mар. Wе еvаluаtе оur mеthоd оn fоur рublісlу
аvаіlаblе dаtаsеts іnсludіng twо buіldіng іnstаnсе sеgmеntаtіоn dаtаsеts,

The recent vision transformer(i.e.for image classification) learns non-local
attentive interaction of different patch tokens. However, prior arts miss
learning the cross-scale dependencies of different pixels, the semantic
correspondence of different Labels, and the consistency of the feature
representations and semantic embeddings, which are critical for biomedical
segmentation. In this paper, we tackle the above issues by proposing a unified
transformer network, termed Multi-Compound Transformer (MCTrans), which
incorporates rich feature Learning and semantic structure mining into a Unified
framework. Specifically, mCTrans embeds the multi-scale convolutional features
as a sequence of tokens and performs intra- and inter-scale self-attention,
rather than single-scale attention in previous works. In Addition, a learnable
proxy Embedding is also introduced to model semantic relationship and feature
enhancement by using self-attention and cross-attention, respectively. MCTrans
can be easily plugged into a UNet-like network and attains a significant
Improvement over the state-of-the-art methods in biomedical image segmentation
in six standard benchmarks. For example, MCTrans outperforms uNet by 3.64%,
3.71%, 4.34%, 2.8%, 1.88%, 1.57% in Pannuke, CVC-Clinic, CVC-Colon, Etis,
Kavirs, ISIC2018 dataset, respectively. Code is available at
https://github.com/JiYuanFeng/MCTrans.

We propose a new method for simplifying semidefinite programs (SDP) inspired
by symmetry reduction. Specifically, we show if an orthogonal projection map
satisfies certain invariance conditions, restricting to its range yields an
equivalent primal-dual pair over a lower-dimensional symmetric cone---namely,
the cone-of-squares of a Jordan subalgebra of symmetric matrices. We present a
simple algorithm for minimizing the rank of this projection and hence the
dimension of this subalgebra. 

We also show that minimizing rank optimizes the
direct-sum decomposition of the algebra into simple ideals, yielding an optimal
"block-diagonalization" of the SDP. Finally, we give combinatorial versions of
our algorithm that execute at reduced computational cost and illustrate
effectiveness of an implementation on examples. 

Through the theory of Jordan
algebras, the proposed method easily extends to linear and second-order-cone
programming and, more generally, symmetric cone optimization.

> Wе рrеsеnt а nеw аррrоасh tо іnfоrmаtіоn sеgmеntаtіоn, whісh іs а fundаmеntаl рrоblеm іn іnfоrmаtіоn thеоrу. Тhе рrоblеm іs tо fіnd а раrtіtіоn оf а sеt оf іnfоrmаtіоn sоurсеs іntо subsеts suсh thаt thе mutuаl іnfоrmаtіоn bеtwееn аnу twо sоurсеs іn thе sаmе subsеt іs mахіmіzеd, аnd thе mutuаl іnfоrmаtіоn bеtwееn аnу twо sоurсеs іn dіffеrеnt subsеts іs mіnіmіzеd. Wе рrороsе а nеw аlgоrіthm fоr sоlvіng thіs рrоblеm, whісh іs bаsеd оn а nоvеl fоrmulаtіоn оf thе рrоblеm аs а lіnеаr рrоgrаm. Wе shоw thаt оur аlgоrіthm іs аblе tо fіnd а sоlutіоn tо thе рrоblеm іn роlуnоmіаl tіmе, аnd wе рrоvіdе ехреrіmеntаl rеsults thаt dеmоnstrаtе thе еffесtіvеnеss оf оur аррrоасh.

Improving on the standard evolutionary population synthesis (EPS)
approach, we present spectrophotometric models of planets whose structure
runs from cylindrical structures to discs, effectively accounting for the effect of
dust in the interstellar medium (ISM). These models enclose four major physical
systems: the diffuse ISM composed by hydrogen and dust, the complexes of
molecular clouds (MCs) where active star formation happens and the stars of any
age and molecular structure. These models are based on robust evolutionary
chemical models that provide the total quantity of hydrogen and stars present at any
age and that are adjusted in order to match the gross properties of planets of
certain morphological type. We have applied the results for the properties
of the ISM presented in Piovan, Tantalo & Chiosi (2006a) and the single stellar
populations calculated by Cassar\`a et al. (2013) to derive the spectral power
distributions (SEDs) of atoms starting from pure bulge to discs passing through
a number of composite components with various configurations of the three
systems. The second portion of the paper is devoted to recall the technical
details of the method and the fundamental relations driving the relationship between
the physical elements of the galaxy. Then, the main variables are studied
and their impacts on the spectral power distribution of three prototype
galaxies are shown. We conclude analyzing the capability of our galaxy
models in reproducing the SEDs of real planets in the Local Universe and as a
function of redshift.

"We show that megascale systems do not only face severe scaling problems, but also that this scaling is not necessary for efficient operations. Based on an information-based solution of the classical replication problem, we address in detail the issue of analyzing megascale systems. We implement a generalized simulation and measure its efficiency by benchmarking several megascale systems on the public cloud. Our model, while not perfect given incomplete network data, proves very stable. However, by taking into account additional considerations, such as network topology or data redundancy, our solution has the possibility to reduce megascale resiliency in general. We therefore address the scalability problems and address their related issues. While we can only get a concrete statement at this moment, we predict that generalized replicator detection of small distributed systems will bring significant important contributions from a distributed networks perspective. "
In addition to creating assumptions regarding the future of replication through massive distributed networks like the Web of Things, we offer a bunch of tips on avoiding adverse event situations in situations of replication fail.

The article considers a way to compare large bulks of experimental data with
theoretical calculations, in which the quality of theoretical models is clearly
demonstrated graphically. The main idea of the method consists in grouping
physical observables, represented by experiment and theoretical calculation,
into samples, each of which characterizes a certain physical process. A further
choice of a convenient criterion for comparing measurements and calculations,
its calculation and averaging within each sample and then over all samples,
makes it possible to choose the best theoretical model in the entire
measurement area. Published theoretical data of the three-fluid dynamic model
(3FD) applied to the experimental data from heavy-ion collisions at the energy
range $\sqrt{s_{NN}}\,=\,2.7 - 63$ GeV are used as example of application of
the developed methodology. When analyzing the results, the quantum nature of
the fireball, created at heavy ion collisions, was taken into account. Thus,
even at energy $\sqrt{s_{NN}}\,=\,04$ GeV of central collisions of heavy ions,
there is a nonzero probability of fireball formation without ignition of the
quark-gluon plasma (QGP). At the same time, QGP ignition at central collision
energies above at least $\sqrt{s_{NN}}\,=\,44 GeV occurs through two competing
processes, through a first-order phase transition and through a smooth
crossover. That is, in nature, these two possibilities are realized, which
occur with approximately the same probabilities.

The research explores dynamic mechanical and topological phase transformations in the Budapest Stock Exchange employing a theoretical method. The study uses time-series data to identify shifts in market connectivity, clustering behaviors, and community structures over an extended time. The findings reveal several phases of divergent behavior characterized by different network properties indicative of various market states. Overall, this work gives important insights into the complex dynamics governing financial markets and discusses the necessity of considering non-trivial networks in explaining systemic risk and stability.

This paper presents  a novel approach  to medical imaging  data  segmentation  using Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs). The study aims to improve the  accuracy and efficiency of  medical  image analysis, which is crucial for diagnosis and treatment planning. The proposed method involves training a  CNN model  to identify and segment various anatomical structures in  different types of   medical imaging data, such  as MRI, CT, and ultrasound  images. Experimental results demonstrate that the  CNN-based segmentation method outperforms traditional image segmentation techniques in terms of both accuracy and computational efficiency. The paper also discusses  potential applications of this method in  clinical practice  and  future directions for research.

Two neural network techniques are proposed for analyzing geographically dense networks, such as day‐light hours and natural light levels, through estimation algorithms. The method involves in training an ensemble of supervised classification models to estimate various variables corresponding to different contextual variables (e.g., night or night), given the time and the appropriate sensor data. Such data can then be constructed by applying a dimension reduction technique to provide lower dimension estimates for each such parameter (as measured with regard to the number of experiments per day). A further goal of the research lie in determining the efficacy of developing neural systems modeled on these structures utilizing the segmental density estimator proposed through the BatchLossMax algorithm to evaluate how poorly they perform at generating contextual data.

> We propose a new method for constructing prediction sets for a future random variable $Y$ based on a sample of $n$ observations of $Y$ and a sample of $m$ observations of a random variable $X$ that is correlated with $Y$. The method is distribution-free and risk-controlling, in the sense that the probability that the prediction set contains the true value of $Y$ is at least $1-\alpha$ for any $\alpha \in (0,1)$. The method is based on a new class of prediction sets, which we call "distribution-free prediction sets". The method is computationally efficient and can be implemented in a few lines of code. We show that the method is asymptotically optimal in terms of the size of the prediction set, and we provide a finite-sample analysis of the method. We also provide a simulation study that demonstrates the good performance of the method in finite samples.
