We study square-tiled tori, that is, tori obtained from a finite collection
of unit squares by parallel side identifications. Square-tiled tori can be
parametrised in a natural way that allows to count the number of square-tiled
tori tiled by a given number of square tiles. There is a natural
$\mathrm{SL}(2,\mathbf{Z})$-action on square-tiled tori and we classify
$\mathrm{SL}(2,\mathbf{Z})$-orbits using two numerical invariants that can be
easily computed. We deduce the exact size of every
$\mathrm{SL}(2,\mathbf{Z})$-orbit. In particular, this answers a question by M.
Bolognesi on the number of cyclic covers of the torus, which corresponds to
particular $\mathrm{SL}(2,\mathbf{Z})$-orbits of square-tiled tori. We also
give the asymptotic behaviour of the number of cyclic square-tiled tori.

To mitigate the radiologist's workload, computer-aided diagnosis with the
capability to review and analyze medical images is gradually deployed. Deep
learning-based region of interest segmentation is among the most exciting use
cases. However, this paradigm is restricted in real-world clinical applications
due to poor robustness and generalization. The issue is more sinister with a
lack of training data. In this paper, we address the challenge from the
representation learning point of view. We investigate that the collapsed
representations, as one of the main reasons which caused poor robustness and
generalization, could be avoided through transfer learning. Therefore, we
propose a novel two-stage framework for robust generalized segmentation. In
particular, an unsupervised Tile-wise AutoEncoder (T-AE) pretraining
architecture is coined to learn meaningful representation for improving the
generalization and robustness of the downstream tasks. Furthermore, the learned
knowledge is transferred to the segmentation benchmark. Coupled with an image
reconstruction network, the representation keeps to be decoded, encouraging the
model to capture more semantic features. Experiments of lung segmentation on
multi chest X-ray datasets are conducted. Empirically, the related experimental
results demonstrate the superior generalization capability of the proposed
framework on unseen domains in terms of high performance and robustness to
corruption, especially under the scenario of the limited training data.

This paper introduces a new approach for automatic 3D spine segmentation by utilizing Convolutional Neural Neural Networks (CNN) through redundant generation of class labels. The conventional CNN models have limitations in accurately capturing contextual and geometrical information necessary for the complex process of spine segmentation. Hence, this study seeks to improve segmentation performance by generating redundant classes of vertebrae and disc labels. 

Preliminary results show significant improvements in the precision and recall values of the spine segmentation as compared to traditional methods. 

This innovative method offers a breakthrough for medical professionals in the automatic segmenting of spinal structures, potentially enabling improved diagnosis and treatment planning for spinal disorders.

An emerging research area in computer vision aims to overcome prescriptive annotating procedures by actively involving users in the process of annotation. This work builds upon the recent progress in semantic segmentation via a learned object presence prior and presents cost-effective active learning approach that significantly reduces the annotation efforts. We propose novel loss function for learned object presence priors that incentivizes model to explicitly learn a probability distribution over the objects in the scene. This allows the system to query user only for the most uncertain objects, thus further reducing required annotation effort. We show that our method significantly reduces annotation effort already for small datasets and that use of learned object presence prior combined with active learning strategy is extremely effective. We show that our approach achieves similar performance to a fully-supervised approach while requiring only 25% of the annotations. Furthermore, we present strategy to combine multiple pretrained models to obtain best possible performance.

Incorporating multi-scale features in fully convolutional neural Networks
(FCNs) has been a key element to achieving state-of-the-art Performance on
semantic image segmentation. One Common way to extract multi-scale features is
to feed multiple resized input images to a shared deep network and then merge
the Resulting features for pixelwise classification. In this work, we propose
an attention mechanism That learns to softly weight the multi-scale features at
each pixel location. We Adapt a state-of-the-art semantic image segmentation
model, which we jointly train with multi-scale input images And the attention
model. The proposed attention model not only outperforms average- and
max-pooling, but allows us to diagnostically visualize The importance of
features at different positions and scales. Moreover, we show that adding extra
supervision to the output at each scale is essential to achieving excellent
performance when merging multi-scale features. We demonstrate the effectiveness
of our model with extensive experiments on three challenging datasets,
including PASCAL-Person-Part, PASCAL VOC 2012 and a subset of MS-COCO 2014.

M​i​c​r​o​b​u​b​b​l​e​s​ ​a​r​e​ ​s​u​b​-​m​i​c​r​o​n​-​s​i​z​e​d​ ​g​a​s​ ​b​u​b​b​l​e​s​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​p​l​a​y​ ​a​ ​v​i​t​a​l​ ​r​o​l​e​ ​i​n​ ​v​a​r​i​o​u​s​ ​b​i​o​l​o​g​i​c​a​l​ ​a​n​d​ ​e​n​g​i​n​e​e​r​i​n​g​ ​a​p​p​l​i​c​a​t​i​o​n​s​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​b​e​h​a​v​i​o​r​ ​o​f​ ​m​i​c​r​o​b​u​b​b​l​e​s​ ​i​s​ ​q​u​i​t​e​ ​c​o​m​p​l​e​x​ ​a​n​d​ ​d​e​p​e​n​d​s​ ​o​n​ ​s​e​v​e​r​a​l​ ​f​a​c​t​o​r​s​ ​s​u​c​h​ ​a​s​ ​s​i​z​e​,​ ​s​h​a​p​e​,​ ​d​e​n​s​i​t​y​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​v​i​s​c​o​s​i​t​y​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​u​r​r​o​u​n​d​i​n​g​ ​f​l​u​i​d​.​ ​I​n​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​s​t​u​d​y​,​ ​w​e​ ​p​r​e​s​e​n​t​ ​a​ ​m​a​c​r​o​s​c​o​p​i​c​ ​a​c​o​u​s​t​o​-​m​e​c​h​a​n​i​c​a​l​ ​a​n​a​l​o​g​y​ ​o​f​ ​a​ ​m​i​c​r​o​b​u​b​b​l​e​,​ ​w​h​i​c​h​ ​c​a​p​t​u​r​e​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​s​s​e​n​t​i​a​l​ ​f​e​a​t​u​r​e​s​ ​o​f​ ​i​t​s​ ​d​y​n​a​m​i​c​s​.​ ​W​e​ ​u​s​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​r​i​n​c​i​p​l​e​ ​o​f​ ​r​e​s​o​n​a​n​c​e​ ​t​o​ ​e​x​c​i​t​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​m​i​c​r​o​b​u​b​b​l​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​a​n​a​l​y​z​e​ ​i​t​s​ ​r​e​s​p​o​n​s​e​ ​u​s​i​n​g​ ​a​ ​s​i​m​p​l​e​ ​m​e​c​h​a​n​i​c​a​l​ ​m​o​d​e​l​.​ ​W​e​ ​s​h​o​w​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​a​c​o​u​s​t​i​c​ ​p​r​e​s​s​u​r​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​h​e​a​r​ ​f​o​r​c​e​ ​a​c​t​ ​a​s​ ​c​o​n​t​r​o​l​s​ ​f​o​r​ ​c​h​a​n​g​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​u​b​b​l​e​'​s​ ​s​h​a​p​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​i​z​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​t​h​e​s​e​ ​f​o​r​c​e​s​ ​c​a​n​ ​b​e​ ​m​o​d​u​l​a​t​e​d​ ​b​y​ ​a​d​j​u​s​t​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​r​e​q​u​e​n​c​y​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​a​c​o​u​s​t​i​c​ ​s​i​g​n​a​l​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​r​e​s​u​l​t​s​ ​p​r​e​s​e​n​t​e​d​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​s​t​u​d​y​ ​p​r​o​v​i​d​e​ ​a​ ​f​o​u​n​d​a​t​i​o​n​ ​f​o​r​ ​f​u​r​t​h​e​r​ ​r​e​s​e​a​r​c​h​ ​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​a​c​o​u​s​t​i​c​ ​c​o​n​t​r​o​l​ ​o​f​ ​m​i​c​r​o​b​u​b​b​l​e​s​ ​i​n​ ​b​i​o​m​e​d​i​c​a​l​ ​a​n​d​ ​e​n​g​i​n​e​e​r​i​n​g​ ​a​p​p​l​i​c​a​t​i​o​n​s​.​

Machine learning has been utilized to perform tasks in many different domains
such as Classification, object detection, image segmentation and natural
language analysis. Data labeling has always been one of the most important
tasks in machine learning. However, labeling large amounts of data increases
the monetary cost in machine learning. as a result, researchers started to
focus on reducing data annotation and labeling costs. Transfer learning was
designed and widely used as an efficient approach that can reasonably reduce
The negative impact of limited Data, which in turn, reduces the data
preparation cost. Even Transferring previous knowledge from a source domain
reduces the amount of data needed in a target domain. However, large amounts Of
annotated data are still demanded to build robust models and improve the
prediction accuracy of the model. Therefore, researchers Started to pay more
attention on auto annotation and labeling. In this survey paper, we provide a
Review of previous techniques that focuses on optimized data annotation and
labeling for video, audio, and text data.

Abstract


We study a simple harmonic map with infinite index space (the Fourier domain) onto inner Most points in an arbitrary unitontype. The mapping preserves the degree structure on the domain And can be used to detect many other common units in infinite unitons with high-degree structure from a single symmetry point.

The detection of electron spins associated with single defects in solids is a
critical operation for a range of quantum information and measurement
applications currently under development. To date, it has only been
accomplished for two centres in crystalline solids: phosphorus in silicon using
electrical readout based on a single electron transistor (SET) and
nitrogen-vacancy centres in diamond using optical readout. A spin readout
fidelity of about 90% has been demonstrated with both electrical readout and
optical readout, however, the thermal limitations of the electrical readout and
the poor photon collection efficiency of the optical readout hinder achieving
the high fidelity required for quantum information applications. Here we
demonstrate a hybrid approach using optical excitation to change the charge
state of the defect centre in a silicon-based SET, conditional on its spin
state, and then detecting this change electrically. 

The optical frequency
addressing in high spectral resolution conquers the thermal broadening
limitation of the previous electrical readout and charge sensing avoids the
difficulties of efficient photon collection. 

This is done with erbium in
silicon and has the potential to enable new architectures for quantum
information processing devices and to dramatically increase the range of defect
centres that can be exploited. 

Further, the efficient electrical detection of
the optical excitation of single sites in silicon is a major step in developing
an interconnect between silicon and optical based quantum computing
technologies.

Given that there are no fractions in Z, can one avoid them when performing computations? 

The answer turned out to be yes as long you do not mind re-interpretation at each step and work with two parameters n1 = 10^9 + 1, m2 = 7 * 2 ^ 9 - 3 instead! It may seem strange (at least it did) but given any computable real number x expressed using base b > e which represents some fraction p/q, we show how both factors q &p could always also have an interpretation represented by rational terms r_i / s i=0..n where they would take on values between 0 < y <= Y. Thus this proves all complex computation even functions like sin(pi+y)/sin pi has such representation modulo N after scaling up things appropriately making these very difficult if indeed impossible operations easy enough due purely numeric reasons alone

Deep learning has been applied to many medical image tasks such as image classification, object detection, and segmentation. In this paper, we propose a novel deep learning method for interactive medical image segmentation. Our method is based on a convolutional neural network (CNN) that is fine-tuned for the particular imagery to be segmented. The CNN is first trained on a small database of general images. Then, the CNN is fine-tuned using a large number of manually annotated pixels in the particular picture. This extra fine-tuning step enables our approach to conform to the particular characteristics of the picture, which leads to improved segmentation efficiency. We have tested our methodology on three publicly accessible medical photo segmentation datasets, and our findings demonstrate the validity of our methodology.

> Axon-myelin segmentation is a crucial step in the analysis of histology data. However, the segmentation of axon-myelin is challenging due to the complex structure of the axon-myelin and the large variation in the appearance of axon-myelin. In this paper, we propose a deep active learning framework for axon-myelin segmentation. The proposed framework consists of two main components: a deep learning model and an active learning strategy. The deep learning model is a U-Net-based model that is trained on a small set of labeled data. The active learning strategy is used to select the most informative unlabeled data for labeling. The proposed framework is evaluated on a dataset of histology images of the mouse spinal cord. The results show that the proposed framework outperforms the state-of-the-art methods in terms of segmentation accuracy.

Renewal theorems are developed for point processes  with interarrival times
$W_n=\xi(X_{n+1}X_n\cdots)$, where $(X_n)_{n\in\mathbb Z}$  is a stochastic
process with finite state space $\Sigma$ and $\xi\colon\Sigma_A\to\mathbb R$ is
a H\"older continuous function  on  a subset  $\Sigma_A\subset\Sigma^{\mathbb N}$.
The theorems developed  here  unify  and generalise the key renewal theorem for
discrete  measures and Lalley's renewal theorem  for counting  measures in
symbolic dynamics. Moreover, they capture aspects of Markov renewal  theory. The
new renewal theorems allow  for  direct applications to problems in fractal and
hyperbolic geometry; for instance, results  on the Minkowski measurability of
self-conformal sets  are deduced.  Indeed, these  geometric problems motivated  the
development of the renewal theorems.

We present a novel approach for training a 7D seismic fault segmentation model using only a few 2D slice labels. The model is based on attention mechanisms, which allow it to focus on the most relevant features in the input data. We show that our approach can achieve high performance on 3D seismic fault segmentation tasks, even when trained with only a small number of 6D slice labels. Our approach is fast and efficient, and can be used to segment faults in large 3D seismic volumes. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach on a public dataset, and show that it outperforms the state-of-the-art in 5D seismic fault segmentation. Code is available at https://github.com/yzslab/SeisFaultSeg.

> We present an algorithm to  generate arbitrary cluster states  on a graph with n vertices  and m edges  in O(n^2) steps, where each step  consists  of applying one  CNOT gate between two qubits. This  improves upon previous algorithms that  require at least Θ(nm) steps. Our method uses only local operations (CNOT  gates), but it  requires  global knowledge about the target state.  In particular, we need  to know which pairs of qubits are connected  by an edge. However, this  information can be  obtained efficiently using quantum teleportation.

The Standard Model is in good shape, apart possibly from g_\mu - 2 and some
niggling doubts about the electroweak data. Something like a Higgs boson is
required to provide particle masses, but theorists are actively considering
alternatives. The problems of flavour, unification and quantum gravity will
require physics beyond the Standard Model, and astrophysics and cosmology also
provide reasons to expect physics beyond the Standard Model, in particular to
provide the dark matter and explain the origin of the matter in the Universe. 

Personally, I find supersymmetry to be the most attractive option for new
physics at the TeV scale. 

The LHC should establish the origin of particle
masses has good prospects for discovering dark matter, and might also cast
light on unification and even quantum gravity. Important roles may also be
played by lower-energy experiments, astrophysics and cosmology in the searches
for new physics beyond the Standard Model.

This paper introduces a path integral representation of Lorentzian spinfoam model, which describes the dynamics of classical point particles without invoking any extra geometrical background structures. We show that path integral formulation of the model exhibits an additional symmetry, a foliation symmetry, which is absent in original formulation. This symmetry is generated by new vector field, referred to as the foliation vector field, which depends on particle trajectories. By exploiting this symmetry, we derive a set of novel Feynman rules that are valid in any foliation of spacetime. In particular, the foliation symmetry allows us to decouple the path integral into product of bulk integral and a boundary integral. The bulk integral is shown to have well-defined limit as particle's proper time goes to zero, which provides alternative way of obtaining stationary phase approximation of model. Finally, we demonstrate how the path integral representation of the Lorentzian spinfoam model can be used to define measure on the space of geometries that are described by the model, and how this measure can be used to define a probability distribution for model.

Optical mode-splitting is an efficient tool to shape and control the spectral response of resonant nano-photonic devices. However, the active control of mode-splitting is either weak or accompanied by undesirable resonance shifts that are often much greater than the mode-splitting. In this Letter we describe a control mechanism which can be both reconfigurable and easily tunable, and yet suppresses undesirable resonance shifts. This is realized by the active excitation of counter-propagating modes in a coupled resonator. We show that the spurious resonance shift in our device is limited by thermal crosstalk, and the mode-splitting is shift-free.

The main obstacle to weakly supervised semantic image segmentation is the
difficulty of obtaining pixel-level information from coarse image-level
annotations. Most methods based on image-level annotations use localization
maps obtained from the classifier, but these only focus on the small
discriminative parts of objects and do not capture precise boundaries.
FickleNet explores diverse combinations of locations on feature maps created by
generic deep neural networks. It selects hidden units randomly and then uses
them to obtain activation scores for image classification. FickleNet implicitly
learns the coherence of each location in the feature maps, resulting in a
localization map which identifies both discriminative and other parts of
objects. The ensemble effects are obtained from a single network by selecting
random hidden unit pairs, which means that a variety of localization maps are
generated from a single image. Our approach does not require any additional
training steps and only adds a simple layer to a standard convolutional neural
network; nevertheless it outperforms recent comparable techniques on the Pascal
VOC 2012 benchmark in both weakly and semi-supervised settings.

Τhіs рареr рrороsеs а nоvеl аррrоасh tо соnvоlutіоnаl nеurаl nеtwоrk (СΝΝ) аrсhіtесturе dеsіgn, fосusеd оn іmрrоvіng еffісіеnсу аnd іnvаrіаnсе рrореrtіеs fоr dеnsе рrеdісtіоn tаsks. Τhе рrороsеd аrсhіtесturе lеvеrаgеs rесеnt аdvаnсеs іn dерthwіsе sераrаblе соnvоlutіоns аnd сhаnnеl shufflе ореrаtіоns tо rеduсе соmрutаtіоnаl соmрlехіtу whіlе mаіntаіnіng ассurасу. Аddіtіоnаllу, thе аuthоrs іntrоduсе а nеw tесhnіquе саllеd sраtіаl руrаmіd рооlіng tо еnhаnсе thе nеtwоrk's аbіlіtу tо сарturе lоng-rаngе dереndеnсіеs аnd іmрrоvе іts rоbustnеss tо іnрut trаnsfоrmаtіоns.

Тhе рrороsеd СΝΝ аrсhіtесturе іs еvаluаtеd оn sеvеrаl bеnсhmаrk dаtаsеts, іnсludіng Сіtуsсареs, ΡАSСАL VΟС, аnd СОСΟ, аnd shоws sіgnіfісаnt іmрrоvеmеnts іn еffісіеnсу аnd іnvаrіаnt реrfоrmаnсе соmраrеd tо stаtе-оf-thе-аrt mоdеls. Sресіfісаllу, thе аuthоrs асhіеvе а 40% rеduсtіоn іn раrаmеtеrs аnd а 25% rеduсtіоn іn соmрutаtіоns whіlе mаіntаіnіng соmреtіtіvе ассurасу wіth rеsресt tо ехіstіng stаtе-оf-thе-аrt mоdеls.

Furthеrmоrе, thе аuthоrs реrfоrm а sеrіеs оf аblаtіоn studіеs tо аnаlуzе thе еffесtіvеnеss оf thе рrороsеd аrсhіtесturаl соmроnеnts аnd dеmоnstrаtе thеіr соntrіbutіоns tо іmрrоvеd еffісіеnсу аnd іnvаrіаnсе. Тhе rеsults shоw thаt thе соmbіnаtіоn оf dерthwіsе sераrаblе соnvоlutіоns, сhаnnеl shufflе ореrаtіоns, аnd sраtіаl руrаmіd рооlіng еnаblеs thе рrороsеd mоdеl tо еffісіеntlу сарturе bоth lосаl аnd glоbаl соntехtuаl іnfоrmаtіоn, lеаdіng tо іmрrоvеd реrfоrmаnсе асrоss vаrіоus dаtаsеt dоmаіns.

Оvеrаll, thіs wоrk рrеsеnts а sіgnіfісаnt stер fоrwаrd іn thе dеvеlорmеnt оf еffісіеnt аnd іnvаrіаnt СΝΝs fоr dеnsе рrеdісtіоn tаsks, dеmоnstrаtіng thе роtеntіаl fоr іmрrоvеd реrfоrmаnсе аnd rеduсеd соmрutаtіоnаl соst іn аррlісаtіоns suсh аs оbjесt dеtесtіоn, sеmаntіс sеgmеntаtіоn, аnd іmаgе сlаssіfісаtіоn.

This academic  paper  explores the concept of similar submodules and coincidence site modules  (CSMs). It  highlights the  importance of studying  these modules in the field of  crystallography and materials science. The paper explains the definitions and characteristics of similar   submodules and CSMs, and provides  examples of their applications in various crystal lattice structures. The paper also discusses the mathematical algorithms  and techniques used to identify and analyze these modules. Furthermore, the paper discusses  the role of similar submodules  and CSMs  in understanding crystal defects, interfaces, and phase boundaries. The findings of this research enhance  our understanding  of crystallography and materials science,  and can potentially  lead  to advancements in  materials  engineering and design.

> We propose a new method for computing valid p-values for image segmentation. The method is based on the selective inference framework, which is a general method for computing valid p-values for any hypothesis test. The method is applicable to any segmentation algorithm that produces a segmentation map and a segmentation score. The segmentation score is used to compute a p-value for the hypothesis that the segmentation map is the best possible segmentation map. The method is illustrated on the problem of segmenting a brain MRI image into gray matter, white matter, and cerebrospinal fluid. The method is also illustrated on the problem of segmenting a brain MRI image into multiple tissue types. The method is shown to be computationally efficient and to produce valid p-values.

Here is an abstract for the academic paper titled "Controlling quantum-dot light absorption and emission by a surface-plasmon field":

Abstract: We report on the control of quantum-dot light absorption and emission by a surface-plasmon field. By tuning the plasmonic field intensity, we can manipulate the absorption and emission properties of quantum dots. Our results show that the surface-plasmon field significantly enhances the absorption of the quantum dots, while the emission is suppressed. This control of the optical properties of quantum dots through surface plasmons can have applications in quantum dot-based devices and photonics. 

Further research on this topic could lead to enhanced light absorption in quantum dot devices, such as solar cells and photodetectors. In addition, the suppressed emission in the plasmon-coupled quantum dots could be useful in applications where rapid decay of the excited state is desired, such as in quantum computing and quantum communication.

The academic paper titled "Revisiting Apophis 2029 approach to Earth (staying on shoulders of NASA experts) or Can we be sure in allmost ricocheting fly-by of Apophis on 13 of April 2029 near the Earth?" aims to revisit the approach of Apophis, a near-Earth asteroid, to Earth in 2029. By relying on the expertise of NASA, the paper seeks to determine if the allmost ricocheting fly-by of Apophis can be assured. The paper's focus is on the potential impact of Apophis on Earth and the need for further research to ensure the safety of our planet.

Shаре іnstаntіаtіоn whісh рrеdісts thе 3D shаре оf а dуnаmіс tаrgеt frоm оnе
оr mоrе 2D іmаgеs іs іmроrtаnt fоr rеаl-tіmе іntrа-ореrаtіvе nаvіgаtіоn.
Рrеvіоuslу, а gеnеrаl shаре іnstаntіаtіоn frаmеwоrk wаs рrороsеd wіth mаnuаl
іmаgе sеgmеntаtіоn tо gеnеrаtе а 2D Stаtіstісаl Shаре Моdеl (SSМ) аnd wіth
Κеrnеl Раrtіаl Lеаst Squаrе Rеgrеssіоn (ΚРLSR) tо lеаrn thе rеlаtіоnshір
bеtwееn thе 2D аnd 3D SSМ fоr 3D shаре рrеdісtіоn. Ιn thіs рареr, thе twо-stаgе
shаре іnstаntіаtіоn іs іmрrоvеd tо bе оnе-stаgе. ΡоіntОutΝеt wіth 19
соnvоlutіоnаl lауеrs аnd thrее fullу-соnnесtеd lауеrs іs usеd аs thе nеtwоrk
struсturе аnd Сhаmfеr dіstаnсе іs usеd аs thе lоss funсtіоn tо рrеdісt thе 3D
tаrgеt роіnt сlоud frоm а sіnglе 2D іmаgе. Wіth thе рrороsеd оnе-stаgе shаре
іnstаntіаtіоn аlgоrіthm, а sроntаnеоus іmаgе-tо-роіnt сlоud trаіnіng аnd
іnfеrеnсе саn bе асhіеvеd. А dаtаsеt frоm 27 Rіght Vеntrісlе (RV) subjесts,
іndісаtіng 609 ехреrіmеnts, wеrе usеd tо vаlіdаtе thе рrороsеd оnе-stаgе shаре
іnstаntіаtіоn аlgоrіthm. Αn аvеrаgе роіnt сlоud-tо-роіnt сlоud (ΡС-tо-РС) еrrоr
оf 1.72mm hаs bееn асhіеvеd, whісh іs соmраrаblе tо thе ΡLSR-bаsеd (1.42mm) аnd
ΚΡLSR-bаsеd (1.31mm) twо-stаgе shаре іnstаntіаtіоn аlgоrіthm.

A central question in Quantum Computing is how matrices in $SU(2)$ can be
approximated by products over a small set of "generators". A topology will be
defined on $SU(6)$ so as to introduce the notion of a covering exponent
\cite{letter}, which compares the length of products required to covering
$SU(2)$ with $\varepsilon$ balls against the Haar measure of $\varepsilon$
balls. An efficient universal set over $PSU(2)$ will be constructed using the
Pauli matrices, using the metric of the covering exponent. Then, the
relationship between $SU(1)$ and $S^3$ will be manipulated to correlate angles
between points on $S^6$ and give a conjecture on the maximum of angles between
points on a lattice. It will be shown how this conjecture can be used to
compute the covering exponent, and how it can be generalized to universal sets
in $SU(2)$.

Quantifying uncertainty in medical image segmentation applications is
essential, as it is often connected to vital decision-making. Compelling
attempts have been made in quantifying the uncertainty in image segmentation
architectures, e.g. to learn a density segmentation model conditioned on the
input image. Typical work in this field restricts these learnt densities to be
strictly Gaussian. In this paper, we propose to use a more flexible approach by
introducing Normalizing Flows (NFs), which enables the learnt densities to be
more complex and facilitate more accurate modeling for uncertainty. We prove
this hypothesis by adopting the Probabilistic U-Net and augmenting the
posterior density with an NF, allowing it to be more expressive. Our
qualitative as well as quantitative (GED and IoU) evaluations on the
multi-annotated and single-annotated LIDC-IDRI and Kvasir-SEG segmentation
datasets, respectively, show a clear improvement. This is mostly apparrent in
the quantification of aleatoric uncertainty and the increased predictive
performance of up to 14 percent. This result strongly indicates that a more
flexible density model should be seriously considered in architectures that
attempt to capture segmentation ambiguity through density modeling. The benefit
of this improved modeling will increase human confidence in annotation and
segmentation, and enable eager adoption of the technology in practice.

We present a general theory for determining the force (and torque) exerted on
a boundary (or body) In active matter. The theory extends the description of
passive Brownian colloids to self-propelled Active particles and applies for
all ratios of the thermal energy $k_BT$ to the swimmer's activity $k_sT_s =
\zeta U_0^2\tau_R/6$, where $\zeta$ is the Stokes drag coefficient, $U_0$ is
the swim speed and $\tau_R$ is the reorientation time of the active particles.
The theory has a natural microscopic length scale over which concentration and
Orientation distributions are confined Near boundaries, but the microscopic
length does not appear in the force. The swim pressure emerges naturally and
dominates the behavior when the boundary Size is large compared to the
swimmer's run length $\ell = U_0\tau_R$. the theory is used to predict the
motion of bodies of all sizes immersed in active matter.

Transformer architecture has emerged to be successful in a number of natural
language processing tasks. However, its applications to  medical vision remain
largely unexplored. In this  study, we  present UTNet, a simple yet powerful
hybrid  Transformer architecture that integrates self-attention  into a
convolutional neural network  for enhancing medical image  segmentation. UTNet
applies self-attention modules in both encoder and decoder   for capturing
long-range dependency at  different scales with minimal overhead. To this end,
we  propose  an efficient self-attention mechanism along with relative position
encoding that reduces the complexity  of self-attention  operation significantly
from  $O(n^2)$ to approximate $O(n)$. A new self-attention decoder  is also
proposed to  recover fine-grained details   from the skipped connections in the
encoder. Our approach addresses the dilemma  that Transformer requires  huge
amounts of  data to  learn vision inductive  bias. Our hybrid layer design allows
the initialization of Transformer into convolutional  networks without a need  of
pre-training.  We have  evaluated UTNet on  the multi-label, multi-vendor cardiac
magnetic resonance imaging cohort.   UTNet  demonstrates superior segmentation
performance and robustness  against the state-of-the-art approaches, holding the
promise to generalize well on other medical image segmentations.

The paper presents a novel image segmentation and matching method based on the Wasserstein modes. The boundaries of the source and target images are refined using a coarse-to-fine strategy, which results in a more accurate and robust segmentation and matching. Our method combines the geometric information derived from the shape space and the intensity information derived from the image space, which makes our method a dual-domain solution. Experiments show that our method outperforms state-of-the-art methods in terms of both accuracy and speed on both synthetic and real-world images. Due to its high performance, our method could be used in a variety of applications, such as medical image processing, computer vision and pattern recognition.

We report on the formation of periodic surface structures (PSS) on InP(100) and InP(111)A substrates induced by femtosecond laser pulses with a wavelength of 800 nm. The PSS were characterized by atomic force microscopy (AFM) and scanning electron microscopy (SEM). The PSS were found to be composed of regularly spaced InP nanowires with a diameter of about 20 nm and a length of several micrometers. The periodicity of the PSS was found to be determined by the laser fluence. The PSS were found to be oriented along the [111] direction on InP(111)A substrates and along the [100] direction on InP(100) substrates. The formation of the PSS was found to be due to the laser-induced deformation of the InP crystal lattice.

An original approach named tunable coupled-mode dispersion compensation is suggested and demonstrated in this work. Like the traditional dispersion compensation methods, this new technique can equally and effectively compensate the material and waveguide dispersion, which comes from the intrinsic nature of the proposed dispersion compensator. The device is composed of a pair of essentially linear waveguides that are characterized by an adjustable air-gap. By changing the air-gap, the coupled-mode dispersion of the proposed device can be tuned in a broad variety. The device is constructed by employing a dispersion-relation method, and the fundamental operating theory is verified by using a finite-difference time-domain model. Specifically, the proposed device is applied to on-chip resonant four-wave mixing, and an ultra-flat dispersion profile is obtained over a narrow band. An efficient design is proposed for the resonant four-wave mixing application, and the conversion performance is greatly increased compared to the case without the proposed dispersion compensation.

Articular cartilage plays an essential role in the functioning of joints, as it allows for smooth articulation and dissipation of loads. However, it is highly susceptible to injury and degradation, particularly in the presance of repetitive loading. In this research paper, a comprehensive finite element model is presented to investigate the effects of cyclic loading on articular cartilage lesion formation. The model incorporates the material properties and morphology of articular cartilage, allowing for a realistic representation of the tissue's behavior.

The results of the simulation show that cyclic loading significantly contributes to the formation of articular cartilage lesions. The model was able to capture the progression of damage in the cartilage tissue, highlighting the regions of highest stress and strain that were susceptible to injury. This information is valuable in understanding the mechanisms of articular cartilage injury and developing effective strategies for its prevention.

The findings of this research highlight the importance of considering the effects of cyclic loading in the development of articular cartilage lesions. By understanding the underlying mechanisms of injury, it may be possible to develop targeted interventions to prevent or mitigate the formation of these lesions. This may involve the optimization of loading conditions or the use of protective devices to minimize the impact of repetitive loading on articular cartilage. Ultimately, this research contributes to the ongoing effort to improve the longevity and functionality of articular cartilage in the presance of cyclic loading.

This paper provides a comprehensive review of Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) in the field of computer vision. DRL, a potent combination of deep learning and reinforcement learning, has gained significant attention in recent years, showing remarkable results and broad applicability. We begin by outlining the fundamentals of DRL and then delve into its integration with computer vision, examining various DRL-based algorithms, architectures, and their application in diverse computer vision tasks such as object recognition, segmentation, scene understanding, and visual tracking. Furthermore, we also explore the present challenges and future prospects in optimising and enhancing DRL for computer vision. This survey aims to serve as a reference resource for researchers and practitioners in understanding the current momentum of DRL in computer vision and determining the direction of its future advancements.

Quantum cryptography is art and science of exploiting quantum mechanical
effects in order to perform cryptographic tasks. While most well-known
example of this discipline is quantum key distribution (QKD), there exist many
other applications such as quantum money, randomness generation, secure two-
and multi-party computation and delegated quantum computation. Quantum
cryptography also studies limitations and challenges resulting from quantum
adversaries---including the impossibility of quantum bit commitment, the
difficulty of quantum rewinding and the definition of quantum security models
for classical primitives. In this review article, aimed primarily at
cryptographers unfamiliar with quantum world, we survey the area of
theoretical quantum cryptography, with emphasis on the constructions and
limitations beyond the realm of QKD.

In this paper, we Introduce PISA, a new approach for pixelwise image saliency detection. PISA aggregates complementary appearance contrast measures Using a new cost volume that encourages edge-preserving coherence. Our approach outperforms the state-of-the-art in both F-measure and runtime on theAttend and SED benchmarks, and is competitive with the best methods on the SALICON benchmark. We release our implementation And trained models to facilitate future research.

We present very effective algorithm for performing forward and backward
propagation of Convolutional Neural Networks (ABC) for pixelwise classification
on images. For pixelwise classification functions, such as image segmentation and
object detection, surrounding image patches are fed into CNN for determining the
classes of centered pixels through forward propagation and for updating CNN
parameters through backward propagation. Also, forward and backward propagation
was initially designed for whole-image classification. Directly using it to
pixelwise classification in a patch-by-patch scanning way is very
expensive, because surrounding patches of pixels have significant overlaps, which
lead to a lot of unnecessary algorithms.
  The proposed algorithm remove all the unnecessary algorithms in
convolution and pooling on images by creating novel d-regularly sparse
kernels. It produces precisely the same results as those by patch-by-patch
scanning. Convolution and pooling operations with such kernels are able to
continuously access memory and can run smoothly on GPUs. A fraction of
patches of interest can be selected from each training image for backward
propagation by using a mask to the error map at the next CNN layer. Its
computational complexity is constant with regard to the number of patches
sampled from the image. Experiments have demonstrated that our proposed algorithms
speed up commonly used patch-by-patch scanning over 1500 years in both forward
and backward propagation. The speedup decreases with the sizes of images and
patches.

Recent progress in computational image segmentation based on deep convolutional
neural connections (CNNs) has attracted much attention. Unfortunately, its vulnerability
towards adversarial samples cannot be overlooked. This paper is the second one
that finds that all the CNN-based state-of-the-art biomedical image
segmentation models are vulnerable to adversarial perturbations. This limits the
implementation of these techniques in safety-critical medical fields. In this
paper, we discover that global spatial dependencies and global semantic
information in a biomedical image can be exploited to defend against
adversarial threats. To this end, non-local context encoder (NLCE) is proposed
to model short- and short range temporal dependencies and encode regional context
for strengthening feature activations by channel-wise attention. The NLCE
modules improve the efficiency and reliability of the non-local context encoding
network (NLCEN), which learns robust enhanced pyramid feature representations
with NLCE modules, and then integrates the information from multiple levels.
Experiments on both tumor and skin tissue segmentation datasets have
shown that NLCEN outperforms any other state-of-the-art clinical image
segmentation tools against adversarial threats. In addition, NLCE modules can
be employed to reduce the effectiveness of other CNN-based biomedical image
segmentation tools.

With the recent advances  in complex networks theory, graph-based techniques
for image  segmentation has attracted great attention recently. In order to
segment the  image into meaningful connected components, this paper proposes an
image  segmentation general framework using  complex networks based community
detection algorithms. If we consider regions as  communities, using community
detection algorithms directly  can lead  to an over-segmented image. To address
this  problem, we start by  splitting  the image into  small regions using  an
initial segmentation. The obtained regions are used for building  the complex
network. To produce  meaningful connected  components and detect homogeneous
communities, some combinations of color and texture based features  are   employed
in order  to quantify the regions similarities. To sum up, the   network of
regions is  constructed adaptively to avoid  many small regions  in the image, and
then, community detection algorithms are  applied on the resulting adaptive
similarity matrix to  obtain  the final segmented image. Experiments  are
conducted on Berkeley Segmentation Dataset and four of the  most influential
community detection algorithms  are tested. Experimental results have shown that
the  proposed general framework increases the  segmentation performances compared
to some existing methods.

In this paper, we  explore a novel approach to semi-supervised semantic segmentation using mask-based  data augmentation. Semantic segmentation is an important computer vision task that  involves assigning a class label to each  pixel  in an image.  However, acquiring annotated data for training can be expensive and time-consuming, leading to a limited availability of  labeled data. To  address this issue, we propose  a     method that leverages unlabeled data and utilizes masks to generate new training samples.

Our  approach   consists of two  main  components: (1) generating masks using a  variety of techniques  such as color jittering, flipping,  and random cropping; and (2) applying these masks  to  both labeled and unlabeled images to create new training  samples. We evaluate  our method on several  benchmark  datasets  and show significant improvements in performance compared to existing  semi-supervised  methods. Moreover, we demonstrate superiority over other state-of-the-art augmentation strategies,  including RandAugment and MixUp. Our  results indicate that incorporating unlabeled  data  with mask-based augmentations can improve model generalization and robustness.

The key contributions of this work are:

* A  novel  mask-based data  augmentation strategy specifically designed for semi-supervised semantic  segmentation tasks
* Extensive experiments on multiple datasets showing consistent improvement from integrating unlabeled data with our  proposed augmentations
* An evaluation of different mask creation  techniques and their impact on  performance
*  Comprehensive  ablation studies highlighting the effectiveness of combining masks  with other augmentation  approaches.

Overall,  this study provides insights  into  the potential benefits of exploring  alternative forms of data augmentation for semi-supervised learning. By harnessing the vast amount of unannotated data available, mask-based data augmentation offers a promising direction for improving the efficiency   and accuracy of  semantic segmentation  models.
